Lu was startled and could not comprehend what was happening, and he suddenly woke up in shock. His soul trembled, and he broke out in a cold sweat, taking a long time to regain his composure. He never dared to reveal this dream to anyone. Not long after, Emperor Xuanzong ascended the throne from the Guang Wang's residence, and Lu was appointed to a higher position according to the usual protocol. From then on, Buddhism gradually revived, with the restoration of temples, monasteries, and the old system for monks and nuns, all in accordance with what he had dreamed. Lu compared the teacher in his dream to the master of the military office, who was a close disciple of his former teacher. He believed this was a divine sign. Could it be that the gods' will was manifested through subtle signs becoming clear?

    ○郑光
    　　During the spring of the sixth year of the Kai Cheng period, Zheng Shangshu Guang of Ziqing dreamed that he was driving a cow cart, with a radiant sun in the cart, illuminating the earth. He held the reins and walked down a wide street. Suddenly, he woke up in surprise and was amazed. A month later, Emperor Xuanzong ascended the throne, and due to his status as the emperor's maternal uncle, he was repeatedly appointed as the Shangshu and Governor of Ziqing, fulfilling the prophecy of his dream.

    83

    张诜，以贞元中，以前王屋令调于有司。忽梦一中使来，诜即具簪笏迎之。谓诜曰：“有诏召君，可偕去。”诜惊且喜，以为上将用我。即命驾，与中使俱出。见门外有吏十余为驱殿者，诜益喜。遂出开远门，西望而去。其道左有吏甚多，再拜于前
    　　近二百里，至一城。舆马人物喧哗，阗咽于路。槐影四矗，烟幕逦迤。城之西北数里，又一城。外有被甲者数百，罗立门之左右，执戈戟，列幡帜，环卫甚严，若王者居。既至门。中使命诜下马。诜整巾笏，中使引入门。兵士甚多。见宫阙台阁，既峻且丽。又至一门，中使引入百余人，具笏组，列于庭，仪甚谨肃。又有一殿峙然，琼玉华耀，真天子殿。殿左右有士数十，具甲倚剑。殿上有朱紫中使甚多。一人峨冠，被衮龙衣，凭玉几而坐殿之东宇。又有一冠衣者，貌若妇人，亦据玉几殿之西宇。有宫嫔数十，列于前。中使谓诜曰：“上在东宇，可前谒。”即趋至东宇前，再拜。有朱衣中使，立于殿之前轩。

    Explanation

    83

    Zhang Shēn, during the reign of Emperor Dezong of Tang, was appointed by the imperial officials after serving as the magistrate of Wangwu County. Suddenly, he dreamed of an imperial messenger arriving. Zhang Shēn immediately prepared his official attire to welcome him. The messenger said to Zhang Shēn, "There is an imperial decree summoning you; please come with me." Zhang Shēn was both surprised and delighted, thinking that the emperor would soon employ him. He then ordered his carriage and went out with the messenger. Outside the door, there were more than ten officials waiting to escort him, which made Zhang Shēn even happier. They then left through the Kaiyuan Gate and headed west. On the left side of the road, there were many officials bowing twice in front of Zhang Shēn.

    After traveling nearly two hundred miles, they arrived at a city. The streets were bustling with people and carriages, and the roads were crowded. The willow trees cast shadows on both sides, and the smoke rose in a continuous line. About several miles northwest of the city, there was another city. Outside the city gates, there were hundreds of armored soldiers standing in rows on either side, holding spears and flags, forming a strict and orderly formation, as if it were the residence of an emperor. Upon reaching the gate, the messenger ordered Zhang Shēn to dismount. Zhang Shēn adjusted his cap and attire, and the messenger led him into the gate. There were many soldiers inside. They saw the majestic palaces and halls, which were both grand and beautiful. They then arrived at another gate, where the messenger led Zhang Shēn and more than a hundred others, all dressed in official attire, into the courtyard, where they stood in a formal and respectful manner. Another hall stood imposingly before them, adorned with jewels and precious stones, truly the imperial hall. On both sides of the hall, there were dozens of armed soldiers standing. Inside the hall, there were many imperial messengers dressed in red and purple. One person with a tall hat and a ceremonial robe adorned with dragon patterns was seated on a jade table in the eastern part of the palace. Another person, dressed in a manner resembling a woman, was also seated on a jade table in the western part of the palace. There were dozens of palace attendants standing in front. The messenger told Zhang Shēn, "The emperor is in the eastern part of the palace; you may go forward to pay respects." Zhang Shēn hurried to the front of the eastern part of the palace and bowed twice. A red-robed imperial messenger stood in front of the palace's front hall.

    84

    宣曰：“卿今宜促治吾宫庭事，无使有不如法者。”诜又再拜舞蹈。既而中使又引至西宇下，其仪度如东宇。既拜，中使遂引出门。诜悸且甚，因谓之曰：“某久处外藩，未得见天子。向者朝对，无乃不可于礼乎？”中使笑曰：“吾君宽，无惧耳。”言毕东望，有兵士数百驰来。中使谓诜曰：“此警夜之兵也。子疾去，无犯严禁。”即呼吏命驾。惶惑之际而寤，窃异其梦，不敢语于人。后数日，诜拜■陵令。及凡所经历，皆符所梦。又天后祔葬，诜所梦殿东宇下，峨冠被衮龙衣者，乃高宗也。殿西宇下，冠衣貌如妇人者，乃天后也。后数月，因至长安，与其友数辈会宿，具话其事。有以列圣真图示诜者，高宗及天后，果梦中所见也

    Explanation

    84

    宣 said, “You should now hasten to manage my palace affairs, and see that nothing is done improperly.” Shen bowed twice and performed a ritual dance. Soon after, the imperial messenger led him to beneath the western chamber, the ceremony being the same as in the eastern chamber. After bowing, the messenger then led him out through the gate. Shen was filled with fear and anxiety, and thus said to him, “I have long served in the provinces and have never had the opportunity to see the Son of Heaven. Was my earlier audience with the emperor perhaps not in accordance with propriety?” The messenger smiled and said, “Our sovereign is lenient; there is no need to fear.” After speaking these words, he looked eastward, and hundreds of soldiers galloped toward them. The messenger said to Shen, “These are the night watch soldiers. Go quickly, and do not violate the strict prohibition.” He immediately called a clerk to prepare a carriage. In a state of confusion, Shen awoke, secretly finding the dream strange, and dared not speak of it to anyone. A few days later, Shen was appointed as the magistrate of ■陵. All the experiences he encountered thereafter corresponded exactly to what he had dreamed. Moreover, when the Empress Dowager was posthumously buried, the figure Shen had seen beneath the eastern chamber of the hall, wearing a tall cap and a dragon-embroidered robe, was Emperor Gaozong. The figure beneath the western chamber, whose attire and appearance resembled a woman, was the Empress Dowager. A few months later, on his way to Chang’an, Shen met with several friends and stayed the night with them, telling them the whole story. One of them showed Shen the authentic portraits of the imperial ancestors, and indeed, Emperor Gaozong and the Empress Dowager were the very figures he had seen in his dream.

    85

    ○侯生
    　　上谷侯生者，家于荆门，以明经入仕，调补宋州虞城县。初娶南阳韩氏女，五年矣。韩氏尝夕梦黄衣者数辈，召出其门，偕东行十余里，至一官署。其宇下列吏卒数十辈，轩宇华壮，人物极众。又引至一院，有一青衣，危冠方履，状甚峻峙，左右者数百，几案茵席，罗列前后。韩氏再拜。俄有一妇人，年二十许，身长丰丽，衣碧襦绛袖，以金玉钗为首饰，自门而来，称卢氏。

    85

    Hou Sheng
    Hou Sheng, a man from Shanggu, resided in Jingmen. He entered officialdom through his knowledge of Confucian classics and was appointed as the magistrate of Yucheng County in Song Prefecture. He had recently married a woman from the Han family of Nanyang, and it had been five years since their marriage. One night, Han woman dreamed of several people dressed in yellow robes who summoned her out of her door and led her east for more than ten li to an official residence. Under the eaves, there were dozens of officials and soldiers. The buildings were grand and magnificent, and there were many people. They then led her to a courtyard where there was a person dressed in green, wearing a tall hat and square shoes, who appeared very majestic and imposing. Hundreds of attendants stood on both sides, with tables, mats, and seats arranged in front and behind. Han woman bowed twice. Soon, a woman about twenty years old, tall and beautiful, wearing a green robe with red sleeves adorned with gold and jade hairpins, came from the door and introduced herself as Lu.

    86

    谓韩氏日：“妾与子仇敌且久，子知之乎？”韩氏曰：“妾一女子，未尝出深闺，安得有仇敌耶！”卢氏色甚怒，曰：“我前身尝为职官，子诬告我罪而代之，使吾摈斥草野而死，岂非仇敌乎今我诉于上帝，且欲雪前身冤。帝従吾请，汝之死不朝夕矣。”韩氏益惧，欲以词拒，而卢氏喋喋不已。青衣者谓卢氏曰：“汝之冤，诚如是矣。然韩氏固未当死，不可为也。”遂令吏出案牍。吏曰：“韩氏余寿一年。”青衣曰：“可疾遣归，无久留也。”命送至门。行未数里，忽悸而悟。恶之不敢言。自是神色摧沮，若有疾者。侯生讯之，具以梦告。后数月，韩氏又梦卢氏者至其家，谓韩氏曰：“子将死矣。”韩氏惊寤。由是疾益加，岁余遂卒

    Explanation

    86

    Han said to her, "I have been your enemy for a long time, do you know about it?" Han replied, "I am just a woman, never having left my inner chamber, how could there be an enemy?" Lu's face turned very angry, and she said, "In my previous life, I was an official. You falsely accused me of a crime and took my place, causing me to be exiled and die in the wilderness. Isn't that an enemy? Now I have appealed to the Supreme Being, and want to clear the injustice of my previous life. The Supreme Being has agreed to my request, and your death is imminent." Han became increasingly afraid, wanting to refuse with words, but Lu kept talking incessantly. The person in plain clothes said to Lu, "Your injustice is indeed as you say. But Han is not necessarily destined to die. It is not right to do so." Then he ordered the official to bring out the records. The official said, "Han has one year left." The person in plain clothes said, "You can quickly send him back, do not keep him here for long." He ordered to send him to the door. After walking a few miles, Han suddenly felt afraid and woke up. He was afraid and dared not speak of it. From then on, his demeanor became dejected, as if he were ill. Hou asked him about it, and he told him about the dream in detail. Several months later, Han dreamed again that Lu came to his house and said, "You are going to die." Han woke up in shock. From then on, his illness worsened, and he died a year later.

    87

    侯生窃叹异，未尝告于人。后数年，旅游襄汉，途次富水，郡僚兰陵萧某，慕生之善，以女妻之。及萧氏归，常衣绛袖碧襦，以金玉钗为首饰，而又身长丰丽，与韩氏先梦同。生因以韩氏之梦告焉，萧氏闻之，甚不乐，曰：“妾外族卢氏，妾自孩提时，为伯舅见念，命为己女，故以卢为小字，则君亡室之梦信矣。

    Explanation

    87

    Mr. Hou secretly sighed in amazement, never telling anyone. Several years later, while traveling through the regions of Xiang and Han, he encountered a county official from Lanling, Mr. Xiao, at Fushui. Mr. Xiao admired Mr. Hou's virtue and arranged for his daughter to marry him. When Xiao's daughter returned home, she always wore red sleeves and green shirts, adorned with gold and jade hairpins, and was tall and beautiful, matching the description in Han's previous dream. Mr. Hou then told her about Han's dream. Upon hearing this, Xiao's daughter was very unhappy and said, "My family is from the Lu clan. Since childhood, my uncle remembered me and designated me as his own daughter, so I was given the name Lu. Therefore, your deceased wife's dream is true."

    88

    ○太白老僧
    　　大唐中，有平阳路氏子，性好奇，少従道士游，后庐于太白山。尝一日，有老僧叩门，路君延坐，与语久之。僧曰：“檀越好奇者，然未能臻玄奥之枢，徒为居深山中。莫若袭轻裘，驰骏马，游朝市，可不快平生志，宁能与麋鹿为伍乎？”路君谢曰：“吾师之言，若真有道者。然而不能示我玄妙之迹，何为张虚词以自炫耶！”僧曰：“请弟子观我玄妙之踪。”言讫，即于衣中出一合子，径寸余，其色黑而光。既启之，即以身入。俄而化为一鸟，飞冲天。知
    ○开业寺
    　　至德二年十月二十三日，丰乐里开业寺有神人足迹甚长，自寺外门至佛殿。先是，阍人宿门下，梦一人，长二丈余，被金甲，执槊，立于寺门外。俄而，以手曳其门，扃钥尽解，神人即挽而入寺，行至拂殿，顾望久而没。阍人惊寤。及晓，视其门，已开矣。即具以梦白于寺僧。寺僧共视，见神人之迹。遂告于京兆。京兆以闻，肃宗命中使验之，如其言

    Explanation

    88

    太白老僧

    In the Tang Dynasty, there was a young man from the Lu family in Pingyang, who was curious and had traveled with Taoist practitioners since his youth. Later, he settled in Mount Tai. One day, an old monk knocked on his door. The young man invited him to sit and they talked for a long time. The monk said, "You who are curious, but you have not yet reached the profound and mysterious essence. It is futile to live in the mountains alone. Would it not be better to wear fine furs, ride swift horses, and wander through the capital city? Could this not fulfill your lifelong aspirations, rather than associating with deer?" The young man thanked him, saying, "Your words, sir, seem to be those of a truly wise person. However, you have not shown me any signs of your profound wisdom. Why then do you speak of empty words to boast?"

    The monk replied, "Let me show you the signs of my profound wisdom." Saying this, he took out a small box from his clothes, about one inch in size, black and shiny. After opening it, he entered it. Soon after, he transformed into a bird and flew into the sky.

    开业寺

    On October 23, 757 CE, during the reign of Emperor Suzong of Tang, at the Opening Temple in Fengle District, there were footprints of a divine being, very long, from the outer gate to the Buddha hall. Before this, the gatekeeper, who was on duty, dreamed of a person, over six feet tall, wearing golden armor and holding a spear, standing outside the temple gate. Soon after, the person used his hand to pull the door, and all the locks were undone. The divine being then entered the temple, walking to the Buddha hall, and looked around for a long time before disappearing. The gatekeeper woke up in shock. In the morning, he saw that the door was already open. He reported his dream to the monks at the temple. The monks saw the footprints of the divine being. They reported this to the Governor of Jingzhao. The governor sent an imperial envoy to verify the event, and it was as they所述的那样。

    89

    ○淮南军卒
    　　陈少游镇淮南时，尝遣军卒赵某使京师遗公卿书。将行，诫之曰：“吾有急事，候汝还报。以汝骁健，故使西去，不可少留。计日不至，当死。”赵日驰数百里，不敢怠。斋
    　　至华阴县，舍逆旅中。寝末熟，忽见一人绿衣。谓赵曰：“我吏于金天王。王命召君，宜疾去。”赵不测，即与使者偕行。至狱庙前，使者入白赵某至。既而呼赵。趋拜阶下。其堂上列烛，见一人据案而坐，侍卫甚严。徐谓赵曰：“吾有子婿，在蜀数年，欲驰使省视，无可为使者。闻汝善行，日数百里，将命汝使蜀，可乎？”赵辞以：“相国命西使长安，且有日期，不然当死。今为大王往蜀，是弃相国命也，实不敢还广陵。且某父母妻子俱在，忍生不归乡里。非敢以他辞不奉教，唯大王察之。”王曰：“径为我去，当不至是。自蜀还由长安，未晚也。”即留赵宿庙后空舍中，具食饮。忧惑不敢寐。遂往蜀，且惧得罪；固辞不往，又虑祸及。

    Explanation

    89

    ○ A Soldier from Huainan
    When Chen Shaoyou was governing Huainan, he once sent a soldier named Zhao on a mission to the capital to deliver a letter to the high officials. Before departing, he warned him, saying, "I have urgent matters and await your return with a report. Because of your strength and swiftness, I have sent you westward, and you must not linger. If you do not arrive within the expected time, you will be executed." Zhao traveled hundreds of miles each day without showing any sign of delay.

    Upon arriving at Huayin County, he stayed at an inn. As he had not yet fallen asleep, he suddenly saw a man dressed in green clothing. The man said to Zhao, "I serve the King of the Golden Sky. The king has summoned you; you should depart quickly." Puzzled, Zhao immediately went with the messenger. When they arrived at the temple of judgment, the messenger entered to announce Zhao’s arrival. Soon after, Zhao was called. He hurried down the steps and bowed in respect. In the hall, candles were lit, and he saw a man seated behind a table, surrounded by a strict retinue of attendants. The man spoke slowly to Zhao, saying, "I have a son-in-law in Shu who has been there for several years. I wish to send someone quickly to visit him, but have no one suitable to serve as an envoy. I have heard that you are skilled in travel, covering hundreds of miles a day. Shall I send you to Shu?" Zhao replied, "The prime minister has ordered me to go west to Chang’an, and there is a set deadline. If I do not go, I will be executed. If I now go to Shu for you, it would be abandoning the prime minister’s command, and I dare not return to Guangling. Moreover, my parents, wife, and children are all waiting for me; how could I live with the guilt of not returning home? I dare not refuse your instruction for other reasons. I only beg the great king to understand." The king said, "Go directly for me, and it will not come to that. Returning from Shu via Chang’an is not too late." He then had Zhao stay in an empty room behind the temple, providing him with food and drink. Troubled and uncertain, Zhao could not sleep. He then went on to Shu, though fearing he would be punished for disobedience; he firmly refused to go, yet also feared that disaster would reach him.

    90

    计未决，俄而渐晓。闻庙中喧阗有声，因出视，见庭中虎豹麋鹿狐兔禽鸟近数万，又有奇状鬼神千数，罗列曲躬如朝谒礼。顷有诉讼者数人偕入，金天断理甚明，良久退去。既而谓左右呼赵。应声而去，王命上阶，于袖中出书一通付赵曰：“持此，为我至蜀郡，访成都萧敬之者与之。吾此吏辈甚多，但以事机密，虑有所泄，非生人传之不可。汝一二日当疾还，无久留。”因以钱一万遣之。赵拜谢而行。至门，告吏曰：“王赐以万钱，我徒行者，安所赍乎？”吏曰：“置怀中耳，”赵即以钱贮怀中，辄无所碍，亦不觉其重也。行未数里，探衣中，皆纸钱耳，即弃道傍。俄有追者至，以数千钱遗之，曰：“向吾误以阴道所用钱赐君，固无所用，今别赐此矣。”赵受之

    Explanation

    90

    The plan had not yet been decided when it gradually grew light. Hearing a noisy sound in the temple, he went out to see and saw nearly ten thousand tigers, leopards, deer, foxes, rabbits, birds, and other animals in the courtyard, as well as thousands of strange-looking spirits, all arrayed in rows and bowing as if in a formal ceremony of court attendance. Soon, several people came in to bring a lawsuit, and the Golden Heaven judged the case very clearly. After a long time, they left. The king then called Zhao, who responded and went away. The king commanded Zhao to ascend the steps and took out a scroll from his sleeve, giving it to Zhao and saying, "Take this and go to Shu Prefecture for me. Visit Xiao Jingzhi of Chengdu and give it to him. I have many officials here, but because the matter is confidential, I fear it might leak out, and it cannot be entrusted to ordinary people. You should return quickly within one or two days, and do not stay long." The king then gave Zhao a thousand coins. Zhao bowed and thanked him before leaving. Upon reaching the gate, Zhao told the official, "The king gave me a thousand coins, but since I am walking, where can I carry them?" The official replied, "Put them in your pocket." Zhao then placed the coins in his pocket, finding it did not hinder him and he did not feel its weight. After walking a few miles, Zhao checked his clothes and found that they were all paper money, which he immediately discarded by the roadside. Soon, someone followed and caught up with Zhao, giving him several thousand coins and saying, "Before, I mistakenly gave you the money used in the secret path, which you would not have needed. Now, I am giving you this instead." Zhao accepted the coins.

    91

    昼夜兼行，逾旬至成都。访萧敬之，以书付之。敬之启视，喜甚，因命席，谓赵曰：“我，人也，家汝郑间。昔岁赴调京师，途至华阴，遂为金天王所迫为亲。今我妻在此，与生人不殊。向者力求一官，今则遂矣，故命君驰报。”即留赵一日，赠缣数段，以还书遣焉。过长安，遂达少游书。得还报，日夜驰行，至华阴。金天见之大喜，且尉劳：“非汝莫可使者。今遣汝还，设相国讯汝，但言为我使，遣汝为裨将，无惧。”即以数十缣与之，曰：“此人间缣帛，可用之。”赵拜谢，而径归淮南。而少游讯其稽留，赵具以事对。少游怒，不信，系狱中。是夕，少游梦一人，介金甲，仗剑，曰：“金天王告相国，向者实遣赵某使蜀，今闻得罪，愿释之。”少游悸寤，奇叹之且久。明日晨起，话于宾僚，即命释赵，署为裨将。元和中犹在

    Explanation

    91

    Traveling day and night, Zhao arrived in Chengdu after more than ten days. He visited Xiao Jingzhi and handed over a letter to him. Xiao opened the letter and was very happy, then ordered a meal and said to Zhao, "I am a human, and my family is in the Zheng area. Last year, I went to the capital for an appointment and was on the way to Huayin when I was forced to marry the King of Gold Heaven. Now my wife is here, and she is no different from ordinary people. Earlier, I strongly sought an official position, and now it has been achieved, so I have sent you to report this." Xiao kept Zhao for a day, gave him several pieces of silk, and sent him back with the letter. Passing through Chang'an, Zhao delivered the letter to Shao You. Upon receiving the reply, Zhao traveled day and night back to Huayin. The King of Gold Heaven was very happy to see Zhao and comforted him, saying, "No one else can be sent. Now I am sending you back. If the Chancellor interrogates you, just say you were sent by me, and I will send you as a deputy general; do not be afraid." He then gave Zhao several pieces of silk and said, "This is silk from the human world, which you can use." Zhao bowed and thanked him, then directly returned to Huainan. Shao You interrogated Zhao about his delay, and Zhao fully answered with the facts. Shao You was angry, did not believe him, and imprisoned him. That night, Shao You dreamed of a man wearing golden armor and holding a sword, who said, "The King of Gold Heaven informs the Chancellor that earlier he indeed sent Zhao to Shu, and now he has heard of his offense and wishes to release him." Shao You woke up in fear and marveled at the dream for a long time. The next morning, he told his attendants about the dream and immediately ordered Zhao to be released and appointed as a deputy general. Zhao was still alive during the Yuanhe period.

    92

    ○元载张谓
    　　元载布衣时，常与故礼部侍郎张谓友善。贫无仆马，弊衣徒行于陈蔡。一日天暮，忽大风雷，原野曛黑，二人相与诣道左神庙中以避焉。时有盗数辈，皆仗剑佩弧矢匿于庙宇下。元张二人忽见之，惶惧益甚，且虑为其所害。二人即负壁而立，不敢动。俄闻庙中有呼者曰：“元相国、张侍郎且至，群盗当疾去，无有害于贵人。”群盗相目而惊，遂驰去。二人因偕贺：“吾向者以殍死为忧，今日真神人之语也。”且喜且叹。其后，载果相代宗，谓终礼部侍郎。知
    ○陈袁生
    　　贞元初，陈郡袁生者，尝任参军于唐安。罢秩游巴川，舍于逆旅氏。忽有一夫，白衣来谒。既坐，谓生曰：“某，高氏子也，家于此郡新明县。往者常职军伍间，今则免矣。故旅游至此。”生与语，其聪辩敏博，迥出于人，袁生奇之。又曰：“某，善算者，能析君平生事。”生即讯之，遂述既往事，一一如笔写。生大惊，是夕，夜既深，密谓袁生曰“我非人也。

    Explanation

    92

    Yuan Zai and Zhang Wei

    Yuan Zai, when he was a commoner, was close friends with Zhang Wei, who had previously served as Vice Minister of Rites. They were poor, lacking servants or horses, and traveled through the regions of Chen and Cai in tattered clothes. One day, as night fell, a sudden storm with thunder and lightning darkened the countryside. Yuan Zai and Zhang Wei sought shelter in a temple on the side of the road. At that moment, several thieves, armed with swords and bows, were hiding under the temple.

    Upon seeing the thieves, Yuan Zai and Zhang Wei became even more fearful and stood against the wall, afraid to move. Suddenly, a voice from within the temple called out: "The Chancellor Yuan and the Vice Minister Zhang are coming. The thieves should leave quickly, as they will not harm the noble men." The thieves looked at each other in shock and hurriedly fled.

    Yuan Zai and Zhang Wei then congratulated each other, saying, "We were once worried about starving to death, but today we truly believe the words came from a divine being." They were both relieved and grateful. Later, Yuan Zai indeed became a chancellor under Emperor Dezong, and Zhang Wei remained a Vice Minister of Rites.

    Chen and Yuan Sheng

    At the beginning of the reign of Emperor Dezong, Chen Yuan Sheng, who had once served as a military officer in Tang'an, resigned his post and traveled to Bashu. He stayed at an inn. Suddenly, a man dressed in white came to pay his respects. After sitting down, the man said to Yuan Sheng, "I am a son of the High Clan, and my family lives in Xinming County of this prefecture. In the past, I served in the military, but I have since retired and am traveling here."

    Yuan Sheng engaged in conversation with him, and the man's intelligence, eloquence, and breadth of knowledge were remarkable, far surpassing those of ordinary people. Yuan Sheng was impressed. The man then said, "I am skilled in calculations and can analyze your life events." Yuan Sheng asked him to do so, and the man recounted Yuan Sheng's past events, describing them as if they were written down. Yuan Sheng was greatly surprised.

    That night, when it was deep into the night, the man quietly told Yuan Sheng, "I am not a human being."

    93

    幸一陈于君子，可乎？”袁生闻之惧，即起曰：“君非人，果鬼乎是将祸我耶！”高生曰：“吾非鬼，亦非祸君，所以来者，将有托于君耳。我赤水神，有祠在新明之南，去岁淫雨数月，居舍尽圯，郡人无有治者，使我为风日所侵铄，且日为樵牧者欺侮，里中人视我如一抔土耳。今我诉于子，子以为可，则言；不，则去。无恨乎？”袁生曰：“神既有愿，又何不可哉！”神曰：“子来岁当调补新明令，傥为我重建词宇，以时奠祀，则真幸之甚者。愿无忘。”袁生诺之。既而又曰：“君初至邑时，当一见诣。然而人神理隔，虑君仆吏有黩于我，君当屏去其吏，独入庙中，冀尽一言耳。”袁生曰：“谨奉教。

    Explanation

    93

    I hope to present this to a gentleman, may it be acceptable?" Upon hearing this, Yuan Sheng was filled with fear and immediately stood up, saying, "Are you not human? Are you a ghost, and will you bring misfortune to me?" Gao Sheng replied, "I am not a ghost, nor will I bring misfortune to you. The reason I am here is to entrust something to you. I am the deity of the Red River, and there is a shrine in the south of Xinming. Last year, there were months of heavy rain, and all the houses are now ruined. The people of the prefecture have no one to repair it, and I am being eroded by wind and sun, and daily insulted by woodcutters and shepherds. The people of the village treat me like a handful of earth. Now I am appealing to you. If you think it is right, then speak; if not, then leave. I hope you will not be angry." Yuan Sheng said, "If the god has a wish, how can it not be possible?" The deity said, "Next year, you will be appointed as the magistrate of Xinming. If you rebuild the shrine for me and offer timely sacrifices, then it will truly be a great favor. I hope you will not forget." Yuan Sheng agreed. The deity then added, "When you first arrive in the county, you should come to see me once. However, the relationship between humans and gods is distant, and I am concerned that your servants might desecrate me. You should dismiss your servants and enter the temple alone. I hope to speak with you fully." Yuan Sheng said, "I will follow your instructions."

    94

    是岁冬，袁生果补新明令。及至任讯之，果有赤水神庙，在县南数里。旬余，遂诣之。未至百余步，下马屏车吏，独入庙中。见其檐宇摧毁，蓬荒如积。伫望久之，有一白衣丈夫自庙后来，高生也，色甚喜。既拜，谓袁生曰：“君不忘前约，今日乃诣我，幸何甚哉！”于是偕行庙中。见阶垣下有一老僧，具桎梏，数人立其旁。袁生问曰：“此何为者？”神曰：“此僧乃县东兰若道成师也。有殃，故吾系。一岁矣。每旦夕，则鞭捶之。従此旬余，当解之。”袁生又曰：“此僧既存，安得系于此乎？”神曰：“以生魄系之。则其人自沈疾，亦安能知吾之为哉！”神告袁生曰：“君幸诺我建庙，可疾图之。”袁生曰：“不敢忘。”既归，将计其工，然贫甚，无以为资，因自念曰：“神人所言系道成师之魄，当沈疾。又云，従此去旬余，当解之。吾今假发他语，俾建其庙宇，又安有疑乎？”

    Explanation

    94

    During that winter, Yuan was indeed appointed to a new order. When he arrived at his post and investigated the matter, he found that there was indeed a temple dedicated to the Red Water god, located several miles south of the county. After a few days, he went to the temple. Before reaching it by a hundred steps, he dismounted and had the carriage and officials clear the way, then entered the temple alone. He saw that the eaves and roof were in ruins, overgrown with weeds and wild grass. He stood and looked for a long time, and then a man in white clothes came out from behind the temple—it was Gao, who looked very happy. After bowing, Gao said to Yuan, "You did not forget our previous agreement, and today you have come to me. How fortunate!" They then walked together in the temple. At the steps and walls, Yuan saw an old monk wearing shackles, with several people standing beside him. Yuan asked, "Who is this person?" The god replied, "This monk is Daocheng, who lived in a temple in the eastern part of the county. He has suffered misfortune, so I have bound him for a year now. Every morning and evening, I beat and whip him. From now on, in a few days, I will release him." Yuan asked again, "Since this monk still exists, how can he be bound here?" The god said, "I bound his soul. So the person naturally fell ill, and how could he know what I am doing?" The god then told Yuan, "You have kindly promised to build a temple for me. Please hurry and plan it." Yuan replied, "I will not forget." After returning, Yuan planned the work, but he was very poor and had no means to fund it. He then thought to himself, "The words of the god about binding Daocheng's soul, he naturally fell ill. And he said that in a few days, he will be released. I will now fabricate another story to build the temple. How could there be any doubt?"

    95

    于是径往县东兰若问之，果有成师者，卧疾一岁矣。道成曰：“某病且死，旦夕则一身尽痛。”袁生曰：“师疾如是，且近于死矣。然我能愈之。师能以缗货建赤水神庙乎？”道成曰：“疾果愈，又安能以缗货为事哉！”袁生即绐曰：“吾善视鬼，近谒赤水神庙，见师魂具桎梏系于垣下，因召赤水神问其事，曰：‘此僧有宿殃，故系于此。’吾怜师之苦，因告其神：‘何为系生人可疾解之。吾当命此僧以修建庙宇，慎无违也。’神喜而诺我，曰：‘従此去旬余，当舍其罪。’吾故告师，疾将愈，宜修赤水神庙也。无以疾愈，遂怠其心。如此，则祸且及矣。”道成伪语曰：“敬受教。”后旬余，果愈。因召门弟子告曰：“吾少年弃家学浮屠氏法，迨今年五十，不幸沈疾。向者袁君谓我曰：‘师之病，赤水神所为也。疾愈，可修补其庙。’夫置神庙者，所以佑兆人，祈福应。今既有害于我，安得不除之乎？”即与其徒持锤诣庙，尽去神像及祠宇，无一遣者

    Explanation

    95

    A monk named Daocheng fell gravely ill and was bedridden for a year. He said, "I am dying, and my entire body aches day and night." Yuan, a knowledgeable man, replied, "Your illness is severe and you are close to death. However, I can cure you. Can you build a temple to the Red Water God with money?" Daocheng said, "If I am indeed cured, how could I use money for such a purpose!" Yuan then lied and said, "I am skilled in seeing ghosts. Recently, I visited the Red Water God Temple and saw your soul bound with shackles under a wall. I summoned the Red Water God to ask about the matter, and he said, 'This monk has committed past misdeeds, so he is bound here.' I pitied your suffering and told the god, 'Why bind a living person? Release him quickly. I will command this monk to build a temple, so be careful not to disobey.' The god was happy and promised me, saying, 'From now on, after more than ten days, his sin will be forgiven.' Therefore, I told you that your illness will heal, and you should build the Red Water God Temple. If you do not build the temple after your illness heals, you will become complacent. If so, disaster will come upon you." Daocheng feigned agreement and said, "I respectfully accept your instruction." After more than ten days, he indeed recovered. He then called his disciples and said, "When I was young, I left home to study Buddhist teachings. Now at fifty, I am unfortunately suffering from a serious illness. Earlier, Mr. Yuan told me, 'Your illness is caused by the Red Water God. If you are cured, you can repair the temple.' Setting up a temple is to protect the people and respond to prayers for blessings. Now that it has harmed me, how can it not be removed?" Immediately, he took his disciples and carried hammers to the temple, completely removing the statues and the temple, leaving nothing behind.

    96

    又明日，道成谒袁生。袁生喜曰：“师病果愈乎吾之语岂妄耶！”道成曰：“然。幸君救我，何敢忘君之恩乎？”袁生曰：“可疾计修赤水神庙也，不然，且惧为祸。”道成曰：“夫神所以赖于人者，以其福可延，戾可弭，旱亢则雩之以泽，潦淫则萗之以霁。故天子诏天下郡国，虽一邑一里必建其祠，盖用为民之福也。若赤水神者，无以福人而为害于人，焉可不去之！已尽毁其庙矣。”袁生且惊且惧，遂谢之。道成气益丰，而袁生惧甚

    Explanation

    96

    The day after tomorrow, Daocheng visited Yuan Sheng. Yuan Sheng was delighted and said, "Has the teacher's illness indeed recovered? Was my statement false?" Daocheng replied, "Yes. Thank you for saving me, how dare I forget your kindness?" Yuan Sheng said, "You should quickly plan to repair the Red River God Temple, otherwise, you will be afraid of disaster." Daocheng said, "The reason why gods rely on people is because their blessings can be extended, and their misfortunes can be averted. In case of drought, they pray for rain; in case of flooding, they pray for clear skies. Therefore, the emperor decrees to all the prefectures and kingdoms, even in the smallest town or village, they must build a shrine, in order to benefit the people. If the Red River God does not benefit people but harms them, how can it not be removed? I have already completely destroyed its temple." Yuan Sheng was both surprised and fearful, and thanked him. Daocheng's spirit became more abundant, while Yuan Sheng was very afraid.

    97

    后月余，吏有罪，袁生朴之，无何，吏死，其家诉于郡，坐徙端溪。行至三峡，忽遇一白衣立于路左，视之，乃赤水神也。曰：“向托君修我祠宇，奈何致道成毁我之舍，弃我之像，使一旦无所归，君之罪也。今君弃逐穷荒，亦我报仇耳。”袁生即谢曰：“毁君者，道成也。何为罪我？”神曰：“道成师福盛甚，吾不能动。今君禄与命衰，故我得以报。”言已不见。生恶之，后数日，竟以疾卒
    ○太原小吏
    　　王锷镇太原，尝一日亭午之际，有小吏见一神人，长丈余，介金仗剑，自衙门缓步而来，既而伫立久之，若有所伺。小吏见之惧甚，白于衙将靳垣、张和。偕视之，如小吏言。俄有暴风起，因忽不见。后月余而锷薨，时元和中也

    Explanation

    97

    A Month Later

    A month later, the official committed a crime, and Yuan Sheng defended him. Soon after, the official died, and his family petitioned the county administration. Yuan Sheng was sentenced to exile to Duanxi. As he traveled to the Three Gorges, he suddenly encountered a white-robed figure standing on the left side of the road. He looked at him and saw that it was the Red Water God. The deity said, "Previously, I entrusted you to repair my shrine. How could you allow Daocheng to destroy my shrine and discard my image, leaving me with nowhere to return? This is your fault. Now that you are being exiled to the farthest reaches, it is merely my revenge." Yuan Sheng immediately apologized, saying, "It was Daocheng who destroyed you. Why should I be blamed?" The deity replied, "Daocheng’s blessings are great; I could not act against him. Now that your fortune and life force have waned, I am able to take my revenge." Having spoken, the deity vanished. Yuan Sheng disliked this encounter, and after several days, he finally died of illness.

    ○ A Minor Official in Taiyuan

    Wang E governed Taiyuan. One day at midday, a minor official saw a divine figure, over ten chi tall, wearing armor and wielding a sword, slowly walking from the government office. He then stood there for a long time, as if waiting for something. The minor official was very frightened and reported the sight to the officials Jin Yuan and Zhang He. They went together to see it, and it was as the minor official had said. Soon, a violent wind arose, and then the figure suddenly disappeared. A month later, Wang E died. At that time, it was during the Yuanhe era.

    98

    ○村人陈翁
    　　云朔之间尝大旱。时暑亦甚，里人病热者以千数。有氓陈翁者，因独行田间，忽逢一人，仪状甚异，擐金甲，左右佩弧矢，执长剑，御良马，朱缨金佩，光采华焕，鞭马疾驰。适遇陈翁，因驻马而语曰：“汝非里中人乎？”翁曰：“某农人，家于此已有年矣。”神人曰：“我，天使。上帝以汝里中人俱病热，岂独骄阳之所为乎且有厉鬼在君邑中，故邑人多病。上命我逐之。”已而不见。陈翁即以其事白于里人。自是云朔之间病热皆愈
    ○崔泽
    　　王锷镇太原，有清河崔泽者，长庆中刺坊州。常避暑于庭，时风月清朗。忽见一丈夫，身甚长，峨冠广袖，自堂之前轩而降，立于阶所，厉声而呼。凡三呼而止。崔氏一家皆见。泽惧而恶，命家僮逼之，已亡见矣。是夕，泽被疾。至明日，发使献书，愿解官归老。相府不许。后月余，卒于郡

    Explanation

    98

    ○ Village Man Chen翁
    In the Yun-Shuo region, there was once a severe drought. The heat was also intense, and among the villagers, thousands suffered from heat illness. There was a villager named Chen翁, who was walking alone in the fields when he suddenly encountered a man whose appearance was very strange. The man was wearing golden armor, had a bow and arrows on both sides, was holding a long sword, and was riding a fine horse. He had red tassels and golden ornaments, radiating a splendid and brilliant aura. He whipped the horse to gallop. Just as he met Chen翁, he stopped the horse and spoke: "Are you not one of the villagers?" Chen翁 replied, "I am a farmer, and I have lived here for many years." The divine being said, "I am an envoy of heaven. The Heavenly Deity has caused the people of your village to suffer from heat illness. Is it not just due to the scorching sun? Moreover, there is a malevolent ghost in your town, which is why the people are ill. The Heavenly Deity has commanded me to chase it away." And then he disappeared. Chen翁 immediately reported the matter to the villagers. From then on, the heat illness in the Yun-Shuo region was cured.

    ○ Cui Ze
    Wang E governed Taiyuan, and there was a man from Qinghe named Cui Ze, who served as the prefect of Fangzhou during the Longqing period. He often sought refuge from the heat in his courtyard, and at that time, the wind and moon were clear and bright. Suddenly, he saw a tall man, wearing a high hat and wide sleeves, descending from the front of the hall, standing on the steps, and shouting loudly. He shouted three times and then stopped. The entire Cui family saw him. Cui Ze was frightened and repulsed, and he ordered his servant to drive him away. But the man had already disappeared. That night, Cui Ze fell ill. The next day, he sent a messenger to present a letter, expressing his wish to resign from his official position and retire. The chancellor's office did not permit it. After a month or so, Cui Ze died in the prefecture.

    99

    ○韩愈（一）
    　　吏部侍郎韩愈，长庆四年夏，以疾不治务，至秋九月免，疾益甚。冬十一月，于靖安里昼卧，见一神人，长丈余，被甲仗剑，佩弧矢，仪状甚峻，至寝室，立于榻前。久而谓愈曰：“帝命与卿计事。”愈遽起整冠而坐，曰；“臣不幸有疾，敢以踞见王。”神人曰：“威粹骨蕝国，世与韩氏为仇，今欲讨之而力不足，卿以为何如？”对曰：“臣愿従大王讨之。”神人颔去。于是书其词置于座侧，数日不能解。至十二月而卒。斋
    ○李逢吉
    　　故相李逢吉，尝为司空范希朝従事于单于府。时金城寺有老僧无为者，年七十余。尝一日独处禅斋，负壁而坐，瞬目数息。忽有一介甲持殳者由寺而至。食顷，闻报李従事来。自是逢吉将游金城寺，无为辄见向者神人先至，率以为常。衙将简郢与无为弟子法真善，常为郢语之

    Explanation

    99

    Han Yu (I)

    The Department of Personnel Deputy Minister Han Yu, during the fourth year of the Changqing era in the summer, neglected his official duties due to illness. By the ninth month of autumn, he was dismissed from office, and his illness worsened. In the eleventh month of winter, while lying down during the day in Jing'an Li, he saw a divine figure, over ten feet tall, clad in armor, wielding a sword, and carrying a bow and arrow. The figure had a stern demeanor and entered his bedroom, standing at the foot of his bed. After a while, the figure told Han Yu, "The emperor has commanded me to discuss matters with you." Han Yu quickly rose, adjusted his cap, and sat up, saying, "I am unfortunate to have an illness, but how dare I sit here in such a casual manner before Your Majesty?" The divine figure replied, "The powerful and malevolent nation of Weicui Guo has been an enemy of the Han family for generations. Now we wish to attack them, but our strength is insufficient. What do you think?" Han Yu responded, "I am willing to follow Your Majesty in this campaign." The divine figure nodded and departed. Han Yu then wrote down the words and placed them beside his seat, but he could not understand them for several days. He passed away in December.

    Li Fengji

    The former prime minister Li Fengji once served as an official under Fan Xichao at the Xiongnu chieftain's office. At the time, there was an old monk named Wuwei at the Golden City Temple, who was over seventy years old. One day, he was alone in his meditation hall, sitting against the wall, and blinking his eyes while counting his breaths. Suddenly, a figure clad in armor and holding a long staff appeared at the temple. Soon after, he heard that Li Fengji was coming. From then on, whenever Li Fengji visited the Golden City Temple, the old monk Wuwei would see the same divine figure arrive first, and this became a regular occurrence. The military officer Jian Ying, who was friendly with Wuwei's disciple Fazhen, often spoke to him about these events.

    100

    ○李回
    　　唐故相李回，少时常久疾，兄軿召巫觋，于庭中设酒食以乐神。方面壁而卧，忽闻庭中喧然，回视，见堂下有数十人，或衣黄衣绿，竞接酒食而啖之。良久将散，巫欲彻其席，忽有一人自空而下，左右两翅。诸鬼皆辟易而退，且曰：“陆大夫神至矣。”巫者亦惊曰：“陆大夫神来。”即命致酒食于庭。其首俯于筵上，食之且尽，乃就饮其酒，俄顷，其貌赪然，若有醉色，遂飞去。群鬼亦随而失。后数日，回疾愈
    ○郄元位
    　　河东衙将郄元位者，太和初常奉使京辇。行至沙苑，会日暮。见一人，长丈余，衣紫佩金，容状丰伟，御白马，其马亦高丈余，导従近十辈，形状非常，执弧矢，自南来。元位甚惊异，立马避之。宰人忽举鞭西指，若有所见，其导従辈俱随指而望。元位亦西望，寂然无睹。及回视之，皆不见矣。元位瘁然汗发，髀战心怵，不觉堕马。因病热，肩舆以归。旬余方愈。时河东连帅司空李愿卒。知

    Explanation

    100

    ○李回
    The former Chancellor Li Hui of the Tang Dynasty, when he was young, suffered from a long-term illness. His elder brother, Qian, summoned shamans to set up wine and food in the courtyard to please the gods. Li Hui lay facing the wall, when suddenly he heard noise in the courtyard. He turned around and saw dozens of people in the hall, some wearing yellow and green clothes, competing to take and eat the wine and food. After a long time, they were about to disperse, and the shaman wanted to clear the table. Suddenly, a person descended from the sky, with wings on both sides. All the ghosts retreated in fear, and said, "The spirit of Master Lu has arrived." The shaman also exclaimed, "The spirit of Master Lu has come." He immediately ordered to bring wine and food to the courtyard. The figure's head bowed over the table, almost finished eating, then drank the wine. In a moment, his face turned red, as if drunk, and then flew away. The ghosts also disappeared. After several days, Li Hui's illness was cured.

    ○郄元位
    Xie Yuanwei, a military officer under the Hedong governorship, was frequently sent on missions to the capital during the Tahe era. He traveled to Shayan as evening approached. He saw a person over ten feet tall, wearing purple and carrying gold, with a majestic appearance, riding a white horse, which was also over ten feet tall. Accompanied by nearly ten attendants, all of whom had unusual appearances and carried bows and arrows, the figure came from the south. Xie Yuanwei was greatly surprised and dismounted to avoid the person. The attendant suddenly raised his whip to point west, as if seeing something, and the attendants followed the direction and looked. Xie Yuanwei also looked west, but saw nothing. When he turned back, he saw nothing. Xie Yuanwei suddenly broke out in a cold sweat, his thighs trembled, and his heart was terrified, causing him to fall off his horse. He fell ill with a fever and was carried back home. He recovered after more than ten days. At that time, Li Yuan, the Governor of Hedong and Grand Master of Works, died.

    101

    ○夏阳赵尉
    　　冯翊之属县夏阳，据大河。县东有池馆，当太华、中条，烟霭岚霏，昏旦在望。又有瀵泉穴其南，泉水清澈，毫缕无隐。太和中，有赵生者，尉于夏阳。尝一夕雨霁，赵生与友数辈，联步望月于瀵泉之上。忽见一人，貌甚黑，被绿袍，自水中流沿泳久之，吟曰：“夜月明皎皎，绿波空悠悠。”赵生方惊，其人忽回望水滨，若有所惧，遂入水，惟露其首。有顷亦没。赵生明日又至泉所，是岸傍数十步，有神祠，表其门曰：“瀵水神。”赵生因入庙，见神坐之左右，抟埴为偶人。被禄袍者，视其貌，若前时所见水中人也。赵生曰：“此瀵壤也，尚能惑众，非怪而何？”将用刬其庙。有县吏曰：“此神庙，且能以风雨助生植。苟若毁其屋，适足为邑人之患。”于是不果隳

    Explanation

    101

    In the county of Xiayang, which belongs to Fengyi Prefecture and is situated along the Yellow River, there is a pavilion to the east of the county. Facing Mount Hua and the Zhongtiao Mountains, the pavilion is surrounded by mist and mountain haze, visible from dawn to dusk. To the south of the pavilion lies the Feno Spring, whose water is crystal clear, revealing even the smallest details. During the middle period of the Northern Wei dynasty, there was a man named Zhao who served as an official in Xiayang. One evening after the rain had stopped, Zhao and his friends walked together and gazed at the moon by the Feno Spring. Suddenly, they saw a person with a dark complexion and wearing a green robe, who had been swimming in the water for a long time and then recited, "The night moon shines brightly, the green waves flow endlessly." Zhao was startled, and the person suddenly turned back to look at the shore, as if frightened, and then dived back into the water, leaving only his head visible. After a short while, he disappeared as well. The next day, Zhao returned to the spring and found a temple about dozens of steps from the shore. The entrance of the temple was marked with the words "God of the Feno Spring." Zhao entered the temple and saw that the god was seated on either side, with clay figurines molded beside him. The figure wearing a green robe resembled the person he had seen in the water the previous night. Zhao said, "This is the god of the Feno Spring, who can still deceive people. What else could it be but a supernatural phenomenon?" He intended to destroy the temple. A county official said, "This is a temple of the god, which can help with the growth of crops through rain and wind. If you destroy the temple, it would only cause trouble for the people of the county." Thus, Zhao did not ultimately destroy the temple.

    102

    ○卢嗣宗
    　　蒲津有舜祠，又有娥皇女英祠，在舜祠之侧。土偶之容，颇尽巧丽。开成中，范阳卢嗣宗假职于蒲津。一日，与其友数辈同游舜庙，至娥皇女英祠，嗣宗戏曰：“吾原为帝子之隶，可乎？”再拜而祝者久之。众皆谓曰：“何侮易之言黩于神乎！”嗣宗笑益酣。自是往往独游娥皇祠，酒酣，多为亵黩语。俄被疾，肩舁以归，色悸而战，身汗如沥。其夕遂卒。家僮辈见十余人，捽拽嗣宗出门，望舜祠而去。及视嗣宗尸，其背有赤文甚多，若为所扑。蒲之人咸异其事
    ○高生
    　　天宝中，有渤海高生者，亡其名。病热而瘠，其臆痛不可忍。召医视之，医曰：“有鬼在臆中，药可以及。”于是煮药而饮之，忽觉暗中动摇。有顷，吐涎斗余，其中凝固不可解。以刀剖之，有一人涎中起。初甚么麽，俄长数尺。高生欲苦之，其人趋出，降阶遽不见。自是疾间

    Explanation

    102

    ○卢嗣宗
    　　In Puzhen, there is a shrine dedicated to Shun, and nearby, there are shrines dedicated to Ehuang and Nüying. The clay figures in these shrines are quite exquisite and beautiful. During the Kai Cheng period, Lu Sizong of Fanyang held a temporary post in Puzhen. One day, he visited the Shun shrine with several friends, and they went to the Ehuang and Nüying shrine. Lu Sizong joked, "I wish to be a servant of the imperial sons, may I?" He bowed twice and prayed for a long time. His friends all said, "How disrespectful and impure are your words to the gods!" Lu Sizong laughed even more heartily. From then on, he often visited the Ehuang shrine alone, drinking heavily and making many impure remarks. Suddenly, he fell ill and was carried home on a shoulder yoke, with a fearful and trembling face and profuse sweating. That night, he died. The household servants saw more than ten people drag Lu Sizong out of the door and head towards the Shun shrine. When they examined Lu Sizong's body, they found many red marks on his back, as if he had been struck. The people of Puzhen were all amazed by the event.

    ○高生
    　　During the Tian Bao period, there was a man from Bohai whose name is unknown. He suffered from fever and emaciation, with unbearable chest pain. He called a doctor to examine him, and the doctor said, "There is a ghost in your chest, and medicine can reach it." So he boiled the medicine and drank it. Suddenly, he felt something moving in the darkness. After a while, he vomited several liters of saliva, which was solid and could not be dissolved. He cut it open with a knife, and a person rose from the saliva. Initially small, the person grew to several feet tall. Gao wanted to harm him, but the person rushed out and disappeared immediately. From then on, his disease subsided.

    103

    ○郑德懋
    　　荥阳郑德懋，常独乘马，逢一婢，姿色甚美，马前拜云：“崔夫人奉迎郑郎。”鄂然曰：“素不识崔夫人，我又未婚，何故相迎？”婢曰：“夫人小女颇有容质，且以清门令族，宜相匹敌。”郑知非人，欲拒之。即有黄衣苍头十余人至，曰：“夫人趣郎”进辄控马，其行甚疾，耳中但闻风鸣，奄至一处，崇垣高门，外皆列植揪桐。郑立于门外，婢先白，须臾，命引郑郎入。进历数门，馆宇甚盛。夫人著梅绿罗裙，可年四十许，姿容可爱，立于东阶下。侍婢八九，皆鲜整。郑趋谒再拜。夫人曰：“无怪相屈耶以郑郎清族美才，愿托姻好。小女无堪，幸能垂意。”郑见逼，不知所对，但唯而已。

    Explanation

    103

    A Man from Xingyang

    V, a man from Xingyang, often rode alone on horseback. One day, he encountered a maid who was exceedingly beautiful in appearance. She bowed before him on the road and said, "Madam Cui invites you, V." Startled, V replied, "I have never met Madam Cui, and I am still unmarried. Why do you invite me?" The maid answered, "Madam Cui's daughter is quite beautiful and virtuous, and given her noble family and fine lineage, she is a suitable match for you." V realized that this was not an ordinary situation and wanted to refuse. Immediately, more than ten elderly men in yellow robes arrived and said, "Madam Cui urges you to come." They immediately controlled the horse, and the journey was very fast. V only heard the wind whistling in his ears as they suddenly arrived at a place with tall walls and high gates, surrounded by rows of willows and poplars. V stood at the door, and the maid announced him first. Soon, he was commanded to enter. He passed through several gates, and the buildings were grand and magnificent. The lady, dressed in a green silk skirt, was about forty years old and had an adorable appearance. She stood at the eastern steps. There were eight or nine maidservants, all fresh and tidy. V hurriedly paid his respects and bowed twice. The lady said, "It is not strange to invite you, as V comes from a noble family with fine talent, and we wish to form a marital alliance. Our daughter is not perfect, but if you could take notice, it would be greatly appreciated." Feeling pressured, V did not know how to respond and only nodded in agreement.

    104

    夫人乃上堂，命引郑郎自西阶升。堂上悉以花罽荐地，左右施局脚床，七宝屏风，黄金屈膝，门垂碧箔，银钩珠络。长筵列馔，皆极丰洁。乃命坐。夫人善清谈，叙置轻重，世难以比。食毕命酒，以银贮之，可三斗余，琥珀色，酌以镂杯，侍婢行酒，味极甘香。向暮，一婢前白，女郎已严妆讫。乃命。引郑郎出就外间，浴以百味香汤，左右进衣冠履佩。美婢十人扶入，恣为调谑。自堂及门，步致花烛，乃延就帐。女年十四五，姿色甚艳，目所未见。被服粲丽，冠绝当时。郑遂欣然。其后遂成礼。明日，夫人命女与就东堂，堂中置红罗绣帐，衾褥茵席，皆悉精绝。女善弹箜篌，曲词新异。郑问所迎婚前乘来马，今在何许，曰：“今已反矣。”主

    Explanation

    104

    The lady then ascended the hall and ordered someone to lead Zheng Lang up the western steps. The entire hall was covered with flowered carpets, and on both sides were placed low tables, seven-treasure screens, golden knee rests, green curtains hanging at the doors, and silver hooks with pearl nets. A long banquet table was set with abundant and clean food. She then ordered him to sit. The lady was skilled in clear discourse, arranging priorities, and her abilities were hard to match in the world. After the meal, wine was ordered, stored in silver, amounting to about three dou, with an amber color. It was served in engraved cups by attendants, and the wine was extremely sweet and fragrant. As evening approached, a maid reported that the young lady had finished dressing. She then ordered. Zheng Lang was led out to the outer room, where he was bathed in a hundred-flavored fragrant bath, and his clothes, hat, shoes, and ornaments were presented. Ten beautiful maids helped him in, and they freely teased him. From the hall to the door, they led him with flowered candles, and then invited him into the tent. The girl was around fourteen or fifteen years old, her beauty was very striking, and her appearance had never been seen before. Her attire was splendid, and her beauty surpassed all of her time. Zheng then happily. Afterward, the ceremony was completed. The next day, the lady ordered the girl to go to the eastern hall. In the hall, there were red embroidered curtains, bedding, and mats, all of which were extremely fine. The girl was skilled at playing the konghou, and her songs and lyrics were new and unique. Zheng asked about the horse he rode when he came to marry, and where it was now, and she replied, "It has already returned."

    105

    如此百余日。郑虽情爱颇重，而心稍嫌忌，因谓女曰：“可得同归乎？”女惨然曰：“幸托契会，得侍巾栉，然幽冥理隔，不遂如何？”因涕泣交下。郑审其怪异，乃白夫人曰：“家中相失，颇有疑怪。乞赐还也。”夫人曰：“适蒙见顾，良深感慕。然幽冥殊涂，理当暂隔。分离之际，能不泫然。”郑亦泣下。乃大醼会，与别曰：“后三年，当相迎也。”郑因拜辞。妇出门，挥泪握手曰：“虽有后期，尚延年岁。欢会尚浅，乖离苦长。努力自爱！”郑亦悲惋。妇以衬体红衫及金钗一双赠别，曰：“君未相忘，以此为念。”乃分袂而去。夫人敕送郑郎，乃前青骢，被带甚精

    Explanation

    105

    For over a hundred days, V was deeply affectionate towards the woman, but his heart grew slightly jealous and suspicious. He then asked her, "Can we go home together?" The woman sadly replied, "I am fortunate to have met you by chance and to be able to serve you, but there is a separation between the living and the dead. How can we be together?" She began to cry and sob. V realized the strange nature of the woman and reported to her superior, saying, "I have been lost in the family, and they are quite suspicious. I request to be returned." The superior said, "Just now, I received your visit, and I am deeply grateful and admiring. However, the paths between the living and the dead are different, and it is reasonable to be temporarily separated. At the moment of separation, how can one not shed tears?" V also wept. They then held a grand banquet and said their farewells, with the superior promising, "In three years, I will come to fetch you." V bowed and took his leave. The woman went out the door, waving tears and shaking hands, saying, "Even though there is a future meeting, the years still extend. Our joyous meetings are still short, and the separation is painful and long. Take care of yourself!" V also felt sad. The woman gave him a red robe and a pair of gold hairpins as gifts, saying, "If you do not forget me, keep this in mind." They parted ways, and the superior ordered the escort of V, giving him a green horse with a very fine saddle and bridle.

    106

    郑乘马出门，倏忽复至其家。奴遂云：“家中失已一年矣。”视其所赠，皆真物也。其家语云：“郎君出行后，其马自归，不见有人送来。”郑始寻其故处，唯见大坟，旁有小冢。茔前列树皆已枯矣，而前所见，悉华茂成阴。其左右人传：“崔夫人及小郎墓也。”郑尤异之。自度三年之期，必当死矣。后至期，果见前所使婢乘车来迎，郑曰：“生死固有定命。苟得乐处，吾复何忧。”乃悉分判家事，预为终期。明日乃卒

    Explanation

    106

    V rode out on his horse, and in the blink of an eye, he returned to his home. His servant then said, "The young master has been missing for a year." Upon examining the gifts he had left, they were all real and genuine. The family said, "After the young master left, his horse returned by itself, but no one was seen bringing it back." V then searched for the place where he had been, and he found only a large tomb, with a small grave beside it. The trees in front of the tomb were all withered, but the ones he had seen before were lush and provided shade. Those around him said, "These are the graves of Cui's wife and her son." V was particularly struck by this. He calculated that his three-year mourning period was coming to an end, and he must die. When the time came, he indeed saw the servant he had sent before, riding in a carriage to greet him. V said, "Life and death are determined by fate. If I can find happiness, what else do I have to worry about?" He then divided and settled all family affairs, preparing for the end. The next day, he died.

    107

    ○李林甫（一）
    　　唐李林甫为相既久，自以阴祸且多，天下颇怨望，有鬼灾，乃致方术士以禳去之。后得一术士，曰：“相国豪贵久矣，积怨者亦多矣，为祸之基，非一朝一夕之故。虽然，庶可免者，朝夕之祸也。”林甫曰：“若之何？”术士曰：“可于长安市求一善射者以备之。”林甫乃于西市召募之，得焉，自云：“尝厕军伍间，以善射称，近为病，他无所知。”林甫即资其衣食，月计以给。后一夕，林甫会宴于庭。燕赵翼侍，度曲未终，忽然中绝。善射者异而听之，无闻矣。

    Explanation

    107

    During the Tang dynasty, Li Linfu had served as chancellor for a long time. Believing that his hidden misdeeds were numerous, he found the world to be quite resentful, leading to ghostly calamities. He then summoned practitioners of esoteric arts to dispel them. Later, he found a sorcerer who said, "The chancellor has been wealthy and powerful for a long time, and there are many who bear resentment against him. The foundation of his calamities is not the result of a single day or night. Although, perhaps what can be avoided is the daily calamity." Linfu asked, "What should be done?" The sorcerer replied, "One could seek a skilled archer in the capital to guard against it." Linfu then recruited one in the western market. The man said, "I used to serve in the military, and was known for my archery. Recently, I have been ill, and know nothing else." Linfu then provided him with clothing and food, and supplied him monthly. One evening, Linfu held a banquet in the courtyard. The musicians and dancers were performing, and the music had not yet finished when it suddenly stopped. The skilled archer listened in surprise, but heard nothing.

    108

    乃默筹曰：“夜未阑，忽如是，非有他耶抑术士之言耶！”乃执弓矢逾垣以入，伺之，忽见垣之南有一物堕而下，又一人逾来，善射者一发中之，乃惊去。因至林甫长乐之地，见歌者舞者，噤而不能啭其喉，屹而不得翻其袖，寂寂然若木偶状者。因视垣南堕下之物，即一囊而结者。解，其中有数百签，皆林甫及家僮名氏也。于是以名呼，一一而应，遂宴饮如初。其明日，术士来，且贺曰：“赖此人，不然，几为所祸，乃负冤而死者也。明公久专机要，积戾万状。自兹十稔，乃非吾之所知。”其后林甫籍没，果期十年也

    Explanation

    108

    He silently pondered and said, “The night is not yet over, yet something has happened so suddenly—could it be something else, or is it the words of the fortune-teller?” He then took up his bow and arrows, climbed over the wall, and entered to watch. Suddenly, he saw something fall from the south side of the wall, and another person climbed over. The skilled archer shot it with one arrow and hit it, and the figure fled in alarm. He then went to the Changle estate of Lin Fu, where he saw singers and dancers, but they were silent and unable to utter a sound, stiff and unable to raise their sleeves, motionless as wooden puppets. He then looked at the object that had fallen from the south side of the wall and saw that it was a bag tied shut. He unfastened it and found inside hundreds of slips, all bearing the names of Lin Fu and his attendants. He then called out each name in turn, and each one responded. Thus, the feast resumed as before. The next day, the fortune-teller arrived and said with celebration, “Thanks to this person, otherwise I would have been ruined, and I would have died unjustly. You have long held the reins of power, accumulating countless grievances. From this point, ten years—this is beyond my knowledge.” Later, Lin Fu had his property confiscated, and indeed, ten years passed.

    109

    ○窦裕
    　　大历中，有进士窦裕者，家寄淮海，下第将之成都。至洋州，无疾卒。常与淮阴令吴兴沈生善，别有年矣，声尘两绝，莫知其适。沈生自淮海调补金堂令，至洋州，舍于馆亭中。是夕，风月晴朗，夜将半，生独若有所亡，而不得其寝。俄见一白衣丈夫，自门步来，且吟且嗟，似有恨而不舒者。久之，吟曰：知
    　　“家依楚水岸，身寄洋州馆
    　　望月独相思，诗襟泪痕满。”生见之，甚觉类窦裕，特起，与语未及，遂无见矣。乃叹曰：“吾与窦君别久矣，岂为鬼耶！”明日，驾而去。行未数里，有殡其路前，有识者曰：“进士窦裕殡宫。”生惊，即弛至馆，问馆吏。曰：“有进士窦裕，自京游蜀，至此暴亡。太守命殡于馆南二里外，道左殡宫是也。”即致奠拜泣而去

    Explanation

    109

    The Ghost of Sin Yu

    During the reign of Emperor Daizong in the Tang Dynasty, there was a scholar named Sin Yu, who came from the Huaihai region. After failing the imperial examinations, he intended to travel to Chengdu. Upon reaching Yangzhou, he died suddenly without any illness. Sin Yu had been friends with Shen Sheng, the magistrate of Huaiyin from Wu Prefecture, but they had lost contact for many years, and Shen Sheng did not know where Sin Yu had gone. Shen Sheng was transferred from Huaihai to serve as the magistrate of Jintang and arrived in Yangzhou, where he stayed at an inn. That night, the moon was bright and the wind was clear. Just as midnight approached, Shen Sheng felt as if he had lost something and could not sleep. Soon, he saw a man in white robes walking towards the door, reciting poetry and sighing, as if filled with sorrow and unable to express it. After a while, the man recited:

    "Home rests by the shores of the Chu River,
    Body lodged in a pavilion of Yangzhou.
    Gazing at the moon, I think of you alone,
    My sleeves wet with tears."

    Shen Sheng recognized the man as resembling Sin Yu. He stood up and tried to speak, but the man disappeared. Shen Sheng sighed, "I have been separated from my friend Sin Yu for so long, could he have become a ghost?" The next day, Shen Sheng left the inn and traveled a few miles when he saw a funeral pavilion on the side of the road. A passerby informed him, "This is the funeral pavilion for the scholar Sin Yu." Shen Sheng was shocked and hurried back to the inn to ask the innkeeper. The innkeeper replied, "There was a scholar named Sin Yu from the capital who traveled to Sichuan and died suddenly here. The prefect ordered his burial two miles south of the inn, and this is the funeral pavilion on the left side of the road." Shen Sheng paid his respects, bowed, wept, and then left.

    110

    ○浔阳李生
    　　李生者，贞元中举进士下第，归浔阳，途次商洛。会汉南节使入觐，为道骑所迫，四顾唯苍山万重，不知所适。时日暮，马劣，无仆徒，见荆棘之深，有殡宫在焉，生遂投匿其中。使既过，方将前去，又不知道途之几何，乃叹曰：“吾之寄是，岂非命哉！”于是止于殡宫中。先拜而祝曰：“某家庐山，下第南归，至此为府公前驱所迫，既不得进，又不得退，是以来。魂如有知，愿容一夕之安。”既而闲望，时风月澄霁，虽郊原数里，皆可洞见。又有殡宫在百步外。仿佛见一人，渐近，乃一女子，缕饰严丽，短不尽尺，至殡宫南，入穴中。生且听之，闻其言曰：“金华夫人奉白崔女郎：今夕风月好，可以肆目，时欢再得，原稍留念。”穴中应曰：“属有贵客寄吾之舍，吾不忍去。乖一夕之欢，不足甚矣。”其人乃去，归殡宫下。生明日至逆旅问之，有知者，是博陵崔氏女也，随父为尉江南，至此而殁，遂藁葬焉。生感之，乃以酒膳致奠而去

    Explanation

    110

    ○Xunyang Li Sheng

    Li Sheng, during the Zhenyuan era, failed the imperial examination for the jinshi degree and returned to Xunyang, passing through Shangluo along the way. Coinciding with the Southern Han envoy's audience with the emperor, he was pursued by the official's horsemen. Looking around, he saw only countless green mountains and did not know where to go. It was evening, and his horse was poor, and he had no servants or attendants. Seeing the deep thorns, he noticed a burial place nearby and hid there. After the envoy had passed, he was about to continue his journey, but he did not know the distance or direction of the road. He sighed and said, "That I should find refuge here, is it not fate?" He then stopped at the burial place. First, he bowed and prayed, "I am from Mount Lu, returning south after failing the examination. I have been forced to stop here by the advance guard of the governor. I cannot proceed nor retreat, so I have come here. If your spirit is aware, I wish for a night's rest." Afterward, he leisurely gazed around, and at that time, the wind and moon were clear and bright. The surrounding countryside for several miles could be clearly seen. There was another burial place a hundred steps away. He saw a person approaching, who turned out to be a woman adorned with fine and beautiful decorations, short, not reaching a full foot in length. She went to the south of the burial place and entered a cave. Li Sheng listened, and he heard her say, "Lady Jinhua conveys to Lady Cui: The wind and moon are beautiful tonight, and we can enjoy the view. If we can meet again, I hope you will remember me." The woman in the cave responded, "There is a distinguished guest staying in my house, and I cannot leave. Missing a night of joy is not very significant." She then left and returned to the burial place. The next day, Li Sheng went to an inn and asked about the woman. Someone told him that she was a daughter of the Boqing Cui family, who followed her father to serve as a military officer in Jiangnan and had died there, and was hastily buried. Li Sheng was moved and offered a sacrifice with wine and food before leaving.

    111

    ○陆乔
    　　元和初，有进士陆乔者，好为歌诗，人颇称之。家于丹阳，所居有台沼，号为胜境。乔家富而好客。一夕，风月晴莹，有扣门者，出视之，见一丈夫，衣冠甚伟，仪状秀逸。乔延入，与生谈议，朗畅出于意表。乔重之，以为人无及者。因请其名氏，曰：“我，沈约也。闻君善诗，故来候耳。”乔惊起曰：“某一贱士，不意君之见临也。愿得少留，以侍谈笑。”既而命酒，约曰：“吾平生不饮酒，非阻君也。”又谓乔曰：“吾友人范仆射云，子知之乎？”乔对曰：“某常读《梁史》，熟范公之名久矣。

    Explanation

    111

    Lu Qiao
    In the early years of the Yuanhe reign, there was a scholar named Lu Qiao, who was fond of composing poetry and was widely praised for it. He lived in Danyang, where his residence featured a terrace and pond, known as a scenic place. Lu Qiao was wealthy and enjoyed entertaining guests. One evening, on a clear and moonlit night, someone knocked on his door. Upon opening it, he saw a man with impressive attire and a graceful, refined appearance. Lu Qiao invited him in and engaged in conversation, which was clear and smooth, surpassing his expectations. He held the man in high regard, thinking no one could match him. He then asked for his name and surname, to which the man replied, "I am Shen Yue. I heard you are good at poetry, so I came to visit." Lu Qiao was surprised and said, "I am just a lowly person, never expecting to be visited by you. I wish to keep you for a short while to enjoy your company." Afterward, he offered wine, but Shen Yue politely declined, saying, "I have never drunk alcohol in my life, but it is not because I am refusing you." He then mentioned his friend Fan Puye, a high-ranking official during the Liang Dynasty, asking if Lu Qiao knew him. Lu Qiao responded that he had read the History of the Liang Dynasty and was familiar with Fan Puye's name.

    112

    ”约曰：“吾将邀之。”乔曰：“幸甚。”约乃命侍者邀范仆射。顷之，云至，乔即拜，延坐，云谓约曰：“休文安得而至是耶！”约曰：“吾慕主人能诗，且好宾客，步月至此，遂相谈谑久之。”约呼左右曰：“往召青箱来。”俄有一儿至，年可十岁余，风貌明秀。约指谓乔曰：“此吾爱子也。少聪敏，好读书。吾甚怜之，因以青箱名焉，欲使传吾学也。不幸先吾逝，今令谒君。”即命其子拜乔。又曰：“此子亦好为诗，近従吾与仆射同过台城，因命为感旧，援笔立成，甚有可观。”即讽之曰：知

    Explanation

    112

    约曰：“吾将邀之。”乔曰：“幸甚。”约乃命侍者邀范仆射。顷之，云至，乔即拜，延坐，云谓约曰：“休文安得而至是耶！”约曰：“吾慕主人能诗，且好宾客，步月至此，遂相谈谑久之。”约呼左右曰：“往召青箱来。”俄有一儿至，年可十岁余，风貌明秀。约指谓乔曰：“此吾爱子也。少聪敏，好读书。吾甚怜之，因以青箱名焉，欲使传吾学也。不幸先吾逝，今令谒君。”即命其子拜乔。又曰：“此子亦好为诗，近従吾与仆射同过台城，因命为感旧，援笔立成，甚有可观。”即讽之曰：

    约说：“我要邀请他。”乔说：“那太好了。”约于是命令侍者去邀请范仆射。不久，范仆射到了，乔立刻行礼，邀请他坐下。范仆射对约说：“休文怎么会到这里来呢！”约说：“我仰慕主人能写诗，而且喜欢宾客，所以步行月下到这里来，于是我们聊了很久，谈笑风生。”约叫来左右的人说：“去把青箱叫来。”一会儿，有一个孩子来了，大约十岁左右，相貌俊秀。约指着孩子对乔说：“这是我的爱子。他小时候聪明，喜欢读书。我非常疼爱他，因此给他取名青箱，希望他能传承我的学问。不幸的是，他在我之前去世了，现在让他来拜见您。”随即命令他的儿子向乔行礼。又说：“这孩子也喜欢写诗，最近我陪着他和仆射一起经过台城，于是让他写了一首《感旧》，他提笔立成，非常出色。”随即吟诵道：

    113

    “六代旧江川，兴亡几百年
    　　繁华今寂寞，朝市昔喧阗
    　　夜月琉璃水，春风卵色天
    　　伤时与怀古，垂泪国门前。”乔叹赏久之。因问约曰：“某常览昭明所集之《选》，见其编录诗句，皆不拘音律，谓之‘齐梁体。’自唐朝沈佺期、宋之问方好为律诗。青箱之诗，乃效今体，何哉？”约曰：“今日为之，而为今体。亦何讶乎？”云又谓约曰：“昔我与君及玄晖、彦升俱游于竟陵之门，日夕笑语卢博，此时之欢，不可追矣。及萧公禅代，吾与君俱为佐命之臣，虽位甚崇，恩愈厚，而心常忧惕，无曩日之欢矣。

    Explanation

    113

    The ancient rivers and landscapes of the Six Dynasties, through which dynasties rose and fell over several hundred years,
    Now lie in desolation, where once bustling markets and streets echoed with life.
    Under the moonlight, the water shimmers like glass, and the sky in spring is a pale, hazy hue.
    Lamenting the present and yearning for the past, I shed tears at the gate of the nation.
    Qiao admired these lines for a long time. He then asked Yao, saying: "I often read the Wenxuan compiled by Zhaoming, and saw that the poems it collected were not bound by rhyme or meter, and were called the 'Qi-Liang style.' It was not until the Tang dynasty, with Shen Quanqi and Song Zhiwen, that regulated verse became popular. Why do the poems of the modern era follow this new style?" Yao replied: "If we write today, we naturally follow the modern style. What is there to be surprised about?" Yun then said to Yao: "In the past, I and you, along with Xuanhui and Yan Sheng, all frequented the literary circle of Jingling. We spent our days and nights laughing and playing games, and those times of joy cannot be recaptured. When Emperor Xiao of Sui peacefully took over the throne, you and I both became loyal ministers of the new dynasty. Though our positions were high and the emperor's favor was even greater, our hearts were often filled with anxiety, and we no longer had the joy of those past days."

    114

    诸葛长民有言：‘贫贱常思富贵，富贵又践危机。’此言不虚哉？”约亦吁嗟久之。又叹曰：“自梁及今，四百年矣。江山风月，不异当时，但人物潜换耳。能不悲乎！”既而谓云曰：“吾为蔡公郢州记室，常梦一人告我曰：‘吾君后当至端揆，然终不及台司’。及吾为仆射尚书令，论者颇以此见许，而终不得。乃知人事无非命也。”时夜已分，云谓约曰：“可归矣。”因相与去，谓乔曰：“此地当有兵起，不过二岁。”乔送至门，行未数步，俱亡所见。乔话于亲友。后岁余，李锜叛。又一年而乔卒

    Explanation

    114

    Zhuge Changmin once said: "Those in poverty often aspire to wealth, but those in wealth often face crises." Is this saying not true?" Yue also sighed deeply for a long time. He then said, "From the Liang Dynasty to the present, it has been four hundred years. The landscape and moonlight are no different from those of the past, but the people have changed silently. How could one not feel sad?" He then said to Yun, "I served as the literary secretary of Cai Gong in Jingzhou. I often dreamed of a person who told me, 'My master will eventually reach the highest position, but will not surpass the Three Excellencies.' When I became the Grand Secretary and Minister of the Central Secretariat, those who discuss it mostly agree with this, but I ultimately did not achieve it. Thus, I understand that human affairs are not without destiny." It was already late at night, and Yun said to Yue, "It is time to go home." They then parted ways and told Qiao, "There will be a military uprising here, but it will not last more than two years." Qiao saw them off to the door, and before they had taken a few steps, they both disappeared. Qiao told his friends and relatives about this. A year or so later, Li Qi rebelled. Another year passed, and Qiao died.

    115

    ○郭翥
    　　元和间，有郭翥者，当为鄂州武昌尉。与沛国刘执谦友善，二人每相语，常恨幽显不得通，约先没者当来告。后，执谦卒数月，翥居华阴，一夕独处，户外嗟吁，久而言曰：“闻郭君无恙。”翥聆其音，知执谦也。曰：“可一面也。”曰：“请去烛，当与子谈耳。”翥即彻烛，引其袂而入。与同榻，话旧历历然。又言冥途罪福甚明，不可欺。夜既分，翥忽觉有秽气发于左右，须臾不可受。即以手而扪之，其躯甚大，不类执谦。翥有膂力，知为他怪，因揽其袂，以身加之，牢不可动，掩鼻而卧。既而告去，翥佯与语留之。将晓，求去愈急，曰：“将曙矣，不遣我，祸且及子。”翥不答。顷之，遂不闻语。俄天晓，一胡人，长七尺余，如卒数日者。时当暑，秽不可近，即命弃去郊外。忽有里人数辈望见，疾来视之，惊曰：“果吾兄也。亡数日矣，昨夜忽失所在。”乃取尸而去

    Explanation

    115

    During the Yuanhe period, there was a man named Guo Zhu who was appointed as the magistrate of Wuchang, Ezhou. He was close friends with Liu Zhiqian from Pei State, and they often lamented that the living and the dead could not communicate. They made a pact that whoever died first would come to inform the other. After Liu Zhiqian passed away several months later, Guo Zhu was living in Huayin. One night, while he was alone, he heard someone outside sighing and weeping. After a while, the voice said, “Have you heard that Guo Jun is still well?” Guo Zhu recognized the voice as Liu Zhiqian’s. He said, “Can we meet?” The voice replied, “Please extinguish the candle, and I will talk with you.” Guo Zhu immediately blew out the candle and pulled the sleeve of the figure, who entered with him. They lay down together on the same bed and recounted their past in great detail. The figure also spoke about the afterlife, where good and bad deeds are clearly judged and cannot be deceived. As the night progressed, Guo Zhu suddenly noticed a foul odor emanating from the figure. The stench became unbearable, and he reached out to touch the figure, only to find that it was much larger than Liu Zhiqian. Realizing that this was a different spirit, Guo Zhu, who was strong, grabbed the figure’s sleeve and pressed his body against it, holding it firmly in place. He covered his nose and lay down. The figure then said it was time to leave, and Guo Zhu pretended to talk to it, trying to keep it there. As dawn approached, the figure became increasingly insistent on leaving, saying, “Dawn is approaching. If you do not let me go, disaster will befall you.” Guo Zhu did not respond. Soon, the figure stopped speaking altogether. Just as the sun rose, a foreigner appeared, over seven feet tall, looking as if he had been dead for several days. It was summer, and the foul smell was unbearable. Guo Zhu ordered the body to be taken outside the city. Suddenly, several villagers saw the figure and rushed over. They were shocked to see that it was their brother, who had been missing for several days. They took the body away.

    116

    ○利俗坊民
    　　长庆初，洛阳利俗坊有民行车数辆，将出长夏门。有一人负布囊，求寄囊于车中，且戒勿妄开。因返入利俗坊，才入，有哭声。受寄者因发囊视之。其口结以生绠，内有一物，其状如牛胞，及黑绳长数尺。民惊，遽敛结之。有顷，其人亦复，曰：“我足痛，欲憩君车中行数里，可乎？”民知其异，乃许之。其人登车，览囊不悦，顾谓民曰：“君何无信？”民谢之。又曰：“我非人，冥司俾予录五百人，明历陕、虢、晋、绛，及至此，人多虫，唯得二十五人耳。今须往徐泗。”又曰：“君晓予言‘虫’乎患赤疮即虫耳。”车行二里，遂辞：“有程，不可久留。君有寿，不复忧矣。”忽负囊下车，失所在。某年夏，诸州人多患赤疮，亦有死者。知

    Explanation

    116

    Original Text:
    ○利俗坊民
    　　长庆初，洛阳利俗坊有民行车数辆，将出长夏门。有一人负布囊，求寄囊于车中，且戒勿妄开。因返入利俗坊，才入，有哭声。受寄者因发囊视之。其口结以生绠，内有一物，其状如牛胞，及黑绳长数尺。民惊，遽敛结之。有顷，其人亦复，曰：“我足痛，欲憩君车中行数里，可乎？”民知其异，乃许之。其人登车，览囊不悦，顾谓民曰：“君何无信？”民谢之。又曰：“我非人，冥司俾予录五百人，明历陕、虢、晋、绛，及至此，人多虫，唯得二十五人耳。今须往徐泗。”又曰：“君晓予言‘虫’乎患赤疮即虫耳。”车行二里，遂辞：“有程，不可久留。君有寿，不复忧矣。”忽负囊下车，失所在。某年夏，诸州人多患赤疮，亦有死者。

    The People of Lisu Fang

    In the early years of the Changqing era, there were several vehicles traveling through the Lisu Fang district of Luoyang, preparing to leave through the Changxia Gate. A man carrying a cloth bag asked to store it in one of the vehicles, warning the others not to open it. After returning to the Lisu Fang district, he heard crying upon entering. The person who had stored the bag then opened it to look inside. The mouth of the bag was tied with a rope, and inside was an object that resembled a cow's bladder and a black rope several feet long. The people were startled and quickly tied the bag shut again. Shortly after, the man returned and said, "My feet hurt; may I rest in your vehicle for a few miles, if that is acceptable?" The people, recognizing something unusual, agreed. The man climbed into the vehicle, looked at the bag unhappily, and turned to the people, saying, "Why are you not trustworthy?" The people apologized. He then said, "I am not a human being; the underworld has ordered me to record the names of five hundred people, passing through Shan, Guo, Jin, and Jiang, and up to this point, many people have been afflicted with insects, but I have only managed to record twenty-five. Now I must go to Xu and Si." He added, "Do you understand what I mean by 'insects'? It refers to the red sores." After the vehicle had traveled two miles, he bid farewell, saying, "I have a schedule to keep and cannot stay long. You will live a long life and no longer need to worry." Suddenly, he carried the bag off the vehicle and disappeared from sight. In a certain summer, many people in various states suffered from red sores, and some even died.

    117

    ○太原部将
    　　长庆中，裴度为北部留守，有部将赵姓者，病热且甚。其子煮药于室，既置药于鼎中，构火，赵见一黄衣人自门来，止于药鼎傍，挈一囊，中有药屑，其色洁曰，如麦粉状，已而致屑于鼎中而去。赵告其子，子曰：“岂非鬼乎是欲重吾父之疾也。”遂去药。赵见向者黄衣人再至，又致药屑于鼎中。赵恶之，亦命弃去。复一日昼寝，其子又煮药，药熟而赵寤，遂进以饮之。后数日果卒
    ○成公逵
    　　李公颜居守北都时，有部将成少仪者，其子曰公逵，常梦一白衣人曰：“地府命我召汝。”逵拒之。使者曰：“冥官遣召一属龙人，汝既属龙，何以逃之？”公逵绐曰：“某非属龙者，君何为见诬？”使者稍解，顾曰：“今舍汝归，当更召属龙者。”公逵惊寤。且以其梦白于少仪。少仪有卒十余人，常在其门下。至明日，一卒无疾而卒。少仪因讯其年，其父曰：“属龙。”果公逵之所梦也。知

    Explanation

    117

    ○太原部将
    　　During the Longqing era, Pei Du served as the governor of the northern region. There was a Surname Zhao, a military officer, who was suffering from a severe fever. His son was boiling medicine in the room. After placing the medicine in the pot and lighting the fire, Zhao saw a man in yellow robes enter through the door and stop beside the medicine pot. He carried a bag containing medicinal powder, which was as clean as wheat flour. The man then added the powder to the pot and left. Zhao informed his son, who said, "Isn't this a ghost trying to worsen my father's illness?" They discarded the medicine. Zhao then saw the same yellow-robed man return and add more medicinal powder to the pot. Zhao was displeased and ordered the medicine to be thrown away. On another day, Zhao was napping during the day, and his son again boiled the medicine. When the medicine was ready, Zhao woke up and drank it. A few days later, Zhao indeed passed away.

    ○成公逵
    　　When Li Gong Yan was stationed in the northern capital, there was a military officer named Cheng Shao Yi. His son, Gong Kui, often dreamed of a man in white robes who said, "The underworld has ordered me to summon you." Gong Kui refused. The messenger said, "The officials of the underworld have been sent to summon a person born in the year of the dragon. Since you are a dragon, how can you escape?" Gong Kui replied deceitfully, "I am not a dragon, why would you slander me?" The messenger seemed to understand and said, "Now we will send you back. We will summon the person born in the year of the dragon." Gong Kui woke up in shock. He then told his father, Cheng Shao Yi, about his dream. Cheng Shao Yi had over ten soldiers under his command. The next day, one of his soldiers died suddenly without any apparent illness. Cheng Shao Yi asked about the soldier's birth year, and the soldier's father said, "He was born in the year of the dragon." This confirmed Gong Kui's dream.

    118

    ○董观（一）
    　　董观，太原人，善阴阳占候之术。唐元和中，与僧灵习善，偕适吴楚间。习道卒，观亦归并州。宝历中，观游汾泾，至泥阳郡，会于龙兴寺。堂宇宏丽，有经数千百编。观遂留止，期尽阅乃还。知
    　　先是，院之东庑北室，空而扃钥。观因请居，寺僧不可，曰：“居是室者，多病或死。且多妖异。”观少年恃气力，曰：“某愿得之。”遂居焉。旬余，夜寐，辄有胡人十数，挈乐持酒来，歌笑其中，若无人。如是数夕，观虽惧，尚不言于寺僧。一日经罢，时已曛黑，观怠甚，闭室而寝。未熟。忽见灵习在榻前，谓观曰：“师行矣。”观惊且恚曰：“师，鬼也。何为而至？”习笑曰：“子运穷数尽，故我得以候子。”即牵观袂去榻。观回视，见其身尚偃，如寝熟。

    Explanation

    118

    Dong Guan (Part One)

    Dong Guan was a man from Taiyuan, skilled in the art of divination and observing omens. During the Yuanhe period of the Tang Dynasty, he traveled with a monk named Ling Xi to the Wu and Chu regions. Ling Xi died on the journey, and Dong Guan returned to Bingzhou. During the Baoli period, Dong Guan traveled to the areas around the Fen and Jing rivers and arrived at Niyang County, where he met at Longxing Temple. The temple's architecture was grand and beautiful, and it housed thousands of scriptures. Dong Guan decided to stay and read them all before returning.

    Previously, a room in the east wing of the temple, located in the north, was empty and locked. Dong Guan requested to live there, but the monks refused, saying, "Those who live in this room often fall ill or die. Moreover, there are many strange and supernatural occurrences." Despite this, Dong Guan, a young man who relied on his strength, said, "I wish to have it." He then occupied the room. After more than ten days, at night when he slept, he would often see ten or more foreigners bringing music and wine, singing and laughing within the room as if it were empty. This continued for several nights. Although Dong Guan was afraid, he still did not tell the monks about his experiences. One day, after finishing his studies, it was already dusk, and Dong Guan was very tired, so he closed the door and went to sleep. He had not yet fallen asleep when he suddenly saw Ling Xi standing by the bed, saying to him, "The master has left." Dong Guan was both surprised and angry, saying, "The master is a ghost. Why have you come?" Ling Xi smiled and said, "Your destiny is exhausted and your fate is over, so I am able to meet you." He then led Dong Guan away from the bed. Dong Guan looked back and saw that his body was still lying down, as if he were sleeping soundly.

    119

    乃叹曰：“嗟乎！我家远，父母尚在。今死此，谁蔽吾尸耶！”习曰：“何子之言失而忧之深乎夫所以为人者，以其能运手足善视听而已。此精魂扶之使然，非自然也。精魂离身，故曰死。是以手足不能为，视听不能施，虽六尺之躯，尚安用乎子宁足念！”观谢之，因问习：“常闻我教有中阴去身者，谁为耶！”习曰：“吾与子谓死而未更生也。”遂相与行。其所向，虽关键甚严，辄不碍。于是出泥阳城西去。其地多草，茸密红碧，如毳毯状。行十余里，一水广不数尺，流而西南。

    Explanation

    119

    The speaker sighed and said, "Alas! My family is far away, and my parents are still alive. Now, if I were to die here, who would protect my body?" Xue replied, "Why are your words so mistaken and why are you so deeply worried? What makes us human is our ability to move our limbs and perceive the world through our senses. It is the spirit that sustains us, not a natural state. When the spirit departs from the body, that is called death. Thus, the limbs cannot act, the senses cannot function, and even a body six feet tall is of no use. Should you be concerned about such a thing?" Guan Xie listened to this and asked Xue, "I have often heard that according to our teachings, there is an intermediate state between life and death. Who experiences this state?" Xue said, "I and you consider death as a state without rebirth." They then set off together. Their path was unobstructed even by the most severe barriers. They emerged from the western part of Niyang City. The land was covered with lush, dense grass that was a mixture of red and green, resembling a soft, colorful carpet. After traveling more than ten miles, they came across a river that was not very wide and flowed southwest.

    120

    观问习，习曰：“此俗所谓奈河。其源出于地府耶。”观即视其水，皆血，而腥秽不可近。又见岸上有冠带袴襦凡数百，习曰：“此逝者之衣。由此趋冥道耳。”又望水西有二城，南北可一里余，草树蒙蔽，庐舍骈接。习与观曰：“与子俱往彼，君生南城徐氏，为次子；我生北城侯氏，为长子。生十年，当重与君舍家归佛氏。”观曰：“吾闻人死当为冥官追捕，案籍罪福，苟平生事行无大过，然后更生人间。今我死未尽夕，遂能如是耶！”曰：“不然。

    Explanation

    120

    Guan asked Xi, who replied, "This is what people commonly call the River of Oblivion. Its source comes from the Underworld, does it not?" Guan looked at the water, which was all blood, and its foul smell made it unbearable to approach. He also saw hundreds of people on the shore, dressed in various garments, and Xi said, "These are the clothes of the deceased. They are following the path to the Underworld." Looking west of the river, Guan saw two cities, stretching for more than one li north to south, overgrown with grass and trees, and houses closely connected. Xi said to Guan, "Let us go there together. You will be born into the Xu family in the southern city as their second son; I will be born into the Hou family in the northern city as their eldest son. After ten years, we will again leave our homes and return to the Buddhist path." Guan said, "I have heard that when a person dies, they should be pursued by officials of the Underworld, who register their sins and merits. As long as one's life has not been marred by major faults, they can be reborn into the human world. But now that I have not even finished dying, how could I be like this?" Xi replied, "Not so."

    121

    冥途与世人无异，脱不为不道，宁桎梏可及身哉？”言已，习即牵衣跃而过。观方攀岸将下，水豁然而开，广丈余，观惊眙惶惑。忽有牵观者，观回视，一人尽体皆毛，状若狮子，其貌即人也。良久，谓观曰：“师何往？”曰：“往此南城耳。”其人曰：“吾命汝阅《大藏经》，宜疾还，不可久留。”遂持观臂，急东西指郡城而归。未至数里，又见一人，状如前召观者，大呼曰：“可持去。将无籍。”顷之，遂至寺。时天以曙，见所居室，有僧数十拥其门，视己身在榻，二人排观入门，忽有水自上沃其体，遂寤
    　　寺僧曰观卒一夕矣。于是具以事语僧。后数日，于佛宇中见二土偶人像为左右侍，乃观前所见者。观因誓心精思，留阅藏经，虽寒暑无少堕。凡数年而归。时宝历二年五月十五也。知
    　　会昌中，诏除天下佛寺，观亦斥去。后至长安，以占候游公卿门，言事往往而中。常为沂州临沂县尉。余在京师，闻其事于观也

    Explanation

    121

    The Underworld is no different from the world of the living. If one does not act unrighteously, why should shackles ever reach one's body?" Having spoken, Xixi immediately led the observer by the hand and leaped across. As the observer was about to descend from the bank, the water suddenly opened up, revealing a space over ten feet wide. The observer was startled and confused. Suddenly, someone pulled at the observer, who looked back and saw a person covered in fur, resembling a lion in appearance but with a human face. After a long while, the person said to the observer, "Master, where are you going?" The observer replied, "To the southern city here." The person said, "I command you to read the Dazangjing. You should return quickly, and you should not stay long." Then he held the observer's arm and hurriedly pointed east and west towards the city, guiding him back. Not having reached a few miles, the observer saw another person, resembling the one who had called him before. The person shouted loudly, "You can take him away. Will there be no record?" Soon after, they arrived at the temple. By then, the sky had already dawned. The observer saw his living quarters, with dozens of monks surrounding the door. He looked down and saw himself on the bed. Two people pushed the observer inside, and suddenly water poured over his body, and he woke up.

    The temple monk said that the observer had died that night. Then the observer recounted everything to the monk. After several days, in the temple, he saw two clay figures serving as attendants, which were the ones he had seen before. The observer then vowed to think deeply and stayed to read the Dazangjing, persevering even through cold and heat. After several years, he returned. It was the 15th day of the fifth month in the second year of Baoli.

    During the Huichang era, an imperial edict abolished all Buddhist temples, and the observer was also dismissed. Later, he went to Chang'an, traveling among the officials to predict auspicious signs, and his predictions were often accurate. He often served as the magistrate of Linyi County in Yizhou. I was in the capital, and I heard this story from the observer.

    122

    ○吴任生
    　　吴郡任生者，善视鬼，庐于洞庭山。貌常若童儿，吴楚之俗，莫能究其甲子。宝历中，有前昆山尉杨氏子侨居吴郡。常一日，里中三数辈相与泛舟，俱游虎丘寺。时任生在舟中，且语及鬼神事。杨生曰：“人鬼殊迹，故鬼卒不可见矣。”任生笑曰：“鬼甚多，人不能识耳，我独识之。”然顾一妇人，衣青衣，拥竖儿，步于岸。生指语曰：“此鬼也。其拥者，乃婴儿之生魂耳。”杨曰：“然则何以辨其鬼耶！”生曰：“君第观我与语。”即厉声呼曰：“尔，鬼也，窃生人之子乎？”其妇人闻而惊慑，遂疾回去，步未十数，遽亡见矣。杨生且叹且异。及晚还，去郭数里，岸傍一家陈筵席，有女巫鼓舞于其左，乃醮神也。杨生与任生俱问之。巫曰：“今日里中人有婴儿暴卒，今则寤矣，故设筵以谢。”遂命出婴儿以视，则真妇人所拥者。诸客惊叹之，谢任生曰：“先生真道术者，吾不得而知也。
