    One year later, one night he was alone in his hall when he suddenly saw a person dressed in purple and carrying a sword, rushing forward and saying to Huilian, "The Heavenly Emperor has a command, appointing you as the chief of the Life and Death Office to establish Yanbola King with a document." The person then presented a brocade box containing documents to Huilian, saying, "This is the Heavenly Emperor's command." The scroll was adorned with jade and gold, marked with patterned silk. Additionally, he was given ceremonial regalia including an ivory tablet, a purple sash, a golden turtle, and a jade belt. Huilian was both joyful and fearful, very confused and anxious, with no time to ask questions, and thus accepted it. He stood in the front hall when a servant rushed in and said, "The palace officials and guards are about to arrive." Soon after, hundreds of people dressed in embroidered clothes with red foreheads, wearing weapons on both sides, rushed in and arranged themselves in several rows, bowing twice. One person stepped forward and said, "I am fortunate to be your servant, dare I express my gratitude." After finishing his words, he bowed again. After the bow, they stood in front in separate rows. The servant then said, "The commanders of the Five Peaks' guards are here."

    167

    ”复有百余人趋入，罗为五行，衣如五方色，皆再拜。相者又曰：“礼器乐悬吏、鼓吹吏、车舆乘马吏、符印簿书吏、帑藏厨膳吏。”近数百人，皆趋而至。有顷，相者曰：“诸岳卫兵及礼器乐悬车与乘马等，请使躬自阅之。”惠连曰：“诸岳卫兵安在？”对曰：“自有所，自有所耳。”惠连即命驾，于是控一白马至，具以金玉，其导引控御従辈，皆向者绣衣也。数骑夹道前驱，引惠连东北而去。传呼甚严。可行数里，兵士万余，或骑或步，尽介金执戈，列于路。枪槊旗旆，文绣交焕。

    Explanation

    167

    Again, there were over a hundred people hurrying in, arranged in five rows, dressed in the colors of the five cardinal directions, all bowing twice. The official said, "Officials responsible for ritual vessels and musical instruments, officials in charge of musical ensembles, officials managing vehicles, carriages, and horses, officials handling seals, documents, and records, officials managing treasury, storage, and food services." Another group of several hundred people hurried in. After a while, the official said, "The guards of the Five Sacred Mountains and the ritual vessels, musical instruments, chariots, and horses, please allow me to personally inspect them." Huilian asked, "Where are the guards of the Five Sacred Mountains?" The official replied, "They have their own place, their own place." Huilian immediately ordered the carriage, and thus a white horse was controlled and arrived, adorned with gold and jade, and the attendants who guided, controlled, and managed the horses were all the same as before, wearing embroidered robes. Several riders preceded along the path on both sides, leading Huilian northeastward. The shouts and calls were very strict. After traveling several miles, there were over ten thousand soldiers, some riding and some walking, all armored in metal and holding spears, lined up on the road. Spears, lances, banners, and flags, all adorned with intricate embroidery and shining brightly.

    168

    俄见朱门外，有数十人，皆衣绿执笏，曲躬而拜者，曰：“此属吏也。”其门内，悉张帷帘几榻，若王者居。惠连既升阶，据几而坐。俄绿衣者十辈，各斋簿书，请惠连判署。已而相者引惠连于东庑下一院。其前庭有车舆乘马甚多。又有乐鼓箫及符印管钥，尽致于榻上，以黄纹帕蔽之。其榻绕四墉。又有玉册，用紫金填字，似篆籀书，盘屈若龙凤之势吏白曰：“此阎波罗王之册也。”有一人具簪冕来谒，惠连与抗礼，既坐，谓惠连曰：“上帝以邺郡内黄县南兰若海悟禅师有德，立心画一册，有阎波罗王礼甚。

    Explanation

    168

    Soon after, outside the red gate, there were dozens of people, all dressed in green robes holding ceremonial tablets, bowing deeply in respect. They said, "These are the officials." Inside the gate, all the curtains, screens, and furniture were arranged, as if it were the residence of a king. Huilian ascended the steps and sat on the table. Soon, ten officials in green robes arrived, each carrying official documents, requesting Huilian to approve and sign them. The guide then led Huilian to a courtyard on the eastern side of the building. In front of the courtyard, there were many carriages, carts, and horses. There were also musical instruments, seals, and keys, all placed on the table and covered with yellow-patterned cloth. The table was surrounded by the four walls. There was also a jade tablet, inscribed with purple gold in ancient script, intricately shaped like dragons and phoenixes. An official said, "This is the document of Yama, the god of death." A man dressed in ceremonial headgear came to pay respects, and Huilian treated him as an equal. After sitting down, the man said to Huilian, "The supreme deity has recognized the virtue of the Buddhist monk Haiwu from the monastery in Nanyang, Anyang County, Hebei Province, and has created a record for him, which is highly honored by Yama."

    169

    言以执事有至行，故拜执事为司命主者，统册立使。某幸列宾掾，故得侍左右。”惠连问曰：“阎波罗王居何？”府掾曰：“地府之尊者也，标冠岳渎，总幽冥之务，非有奇特之行者，不在是选。”惠连思曰：“吾行册礼于幽冥，岂非身已死乎？”又念及妻子，怏怏有不平之色。府掾已察其旨，谓惠连曰：“执事有忧色，得非以妻子为念乎？”惠连曰：“然。”府掾曰：“册命之礼用明日，执事可暂归治其家。然执事官至崇，幸不以幽显为恨。”言讫遂起。惠连即命驾出行，而昏然若醉者，即据案假寐。及寤，已在县，时天才晓

    Explanation

    169

    Because the official has high moral conduct, I was appointed as the chief of the God of Fate, overseeing the registration and appointment of officials. I am fortunate to be among the attendants, thus I can serve at your side. Huilian asked, "Where does King Yanbola reside?" The official replied, "He is a highly esteemed figure in the underworld, distinguished among mountains and rivers, and oversees the affairs of the underworld. Those without extraordinary conduct are not chosen." Huilian thought, "I am conducting the registration ceremony in the underworld. Isn't this because I have already died?" He also thought of his wife and children, looking unhappy and discontented. The official noticed his intention and said to Huilian, "The official looks worried. Isn't it because you are concerned about your wife and children?" Huilian replied, "Yes." The official said, "The ceremony will take place tomorrow. You can temporarily return to take care of your family. But your position is very high. I hope you do not regret the difference between the living and the dead." After speaking, he stood up. Huilian immediately ordered a carriage to leave, and he felt drowsy, as if drunk. He leaned on the desk and took a nap. When he woke up, he was already in the county, and it was just dawn.

    170

    惊叹且久，自度上帝命，固不可免，即具白妻子，为理命。又白于县令，令曹某不信。惠连遂汤沐，具绅冕，卧于榻。是夕，县吏数辈，皆闻空中有声若风雨，自北来，直入惠连之室。食顷，惠连卒。又闻其声北向而去。叹骇，因遣使往邺郡内黄县南问，果是兰若院禅师海悟者，近卒矣
    ○刘宪
    　　尚书李寰镇平阳时，有衙将刘宪者，河朔人，性刚直，有胆勇。一夕，见一白衣来至其家，谓宪曰：“府僚命汝甚急，可疾赴召也。”宪怒曰：“吾军中裨将，未尝有过，府僚安得见命乎？”白衣曰：“君第去，勿辞。不然祸及。”宪震声叱之，白衣驰去，行未数步，已亡所在。宪方悟鬼也。夜深又至，呼宪。宪私自计曰：“吾闻死生有命，焉可以逃之。”即与偕往。出城数里，至一公署，见冥官在厅，有吏数十辈，列其左右。

    Explanation

    170

    Astonished for a long time, Huilian considered the will of the Heavenly Emperor and concluded that it was inevitable. He immediately informed his wife and children and prepared for his fate. He also informed the county magistrate, but Magistrate Mr. Cao did not believe him. Huilian then bathed, dressed in official robes and hat, and lay on the bed. That night, several county officials all heard a sound like wind and rain in the air, coming from the north and directly entering Huilian's room. After a meal, Huilian died. They heard the sound go away to the north. They were astonished and sent messengers to the south of Neihuang County in Yeh Prefecture to inquire. It turned out that it was Chan Master Haiwu of Lanruo Temple, who had recently passed away.

    ○ Liu Xian

    When Shangshu Li Huan was stationed in Pingyang, there was a military officer named Liu Xian, from the He River region. He was of a straightforward and brave nature. One night, he saw a white-clad figure come to his house. The figure said to Xian, "The officials of the governor's office have urgently summoned you; you must go quickly." Xian angrily said, "I am a deputy general in the army, never having committed any fault; how could the officials of the governor's office summon me?" The white-clad figure said, "Just go, do not refuse. Otherwise, disaster will befall you." Xian shouted at him with a loud voice, and the white-clad figure galloped away, disappearing after taking only a few steps. Xian then realized it was a ghost. Late at night, it came again, calling Xian. Xian privately thought, "I have heard that life and death are predestined; how can one escape it?" He immediately went with it. They went out of the city for several miles, arriving at a public office. They saw a judge of the underworld in the hall, with dozens of officials standing on either side.

    171

    冥官闻宪至，整巾帻，降阶尽礼。已而延坐，谓宪曰：“吾以子勇烈闻，故遣奉命。”宪曰：“未委明公见召之旨。”冥官曰：“地府有巡察使，以巡省岳渎道路，有不如法者，得以察之，亦重事，非刚烈者不可以委焉。愿足下俯而任之。”宪谢曰：“某无他才，愿更择刚勇者委之。”冥官又曰：“子何拒之深耶！”于是命案掾立召洪洞县吏王信讫，即遣一吏送宪归。宪惊寤。后数日，寰命宪使北都，行次洪洞县，因以事话于县寮，县寮曰：“县有吏王信者，卒数日矣。”主

    Explanation

    171

    The underworld official heard that Xian had arrived and tidied his cap, descending the steps to greet him with full courtesy. He then invited Xian to sit and said, "I have heard of your bravery and valor, so I have sent you this mission." Xian replied, "I do not understand the reason for your summons, sir." The underworld official said, "In the underworld, there are patrol officers who inspect the roads of the mountains and rivers. If anyone does not follow the rules, they can be investigated. This is a significant task, one that can only be entrusted to someone who is brave and resolute. I hope you will accept this responsibility." Xian declined, saying, "I have no other talents; I would rather you choose someone else who is more suitable." The underworld official said, "Why are you so reluctant to accept this task?" He then ordered the case official to immediately summon the county clerk Wang Xin from Hongdong County and sent a clerk to escort Xian back home. Xian woke up in a state of shock. A few days later, the governor ordered Xian to go to the northern capital. On the way, he passed through Hongdong County and discussed matters with the county officials. The officials said, "There was a clerk named Wang Xin in the county who died several days ago."

    172

    ○张汶
    　　右常侍杨潜，尝自尚书郎出刺西河郡。时属县平遥，有乡吏张汶者，无疾暴卒，数日而寤。初汶见亡兄来诣其门，汶甚惊，因谓曰：“吾兄非鬼耶何为而来？”兄泣曰：“我自去人间，常常属念亲友，若瞽不得忘视也。思平生欢，岂可得乎今冥官使我，得归而省汝。”汉曰：“冥官为谁？”曰：“地府之官，权位甚尊。吾今为其吏，往往奉使至里中，比以幽明异路，不可诣汝之门。今冥官召汝，汝可疾赴。”汶惧，辞之不可。牵汶袂而去。

    Explanation

    172

    Right Attendant Yang Qian, who once served as a Shangshu Lang and was appointed to govern Xihé Prefecture, had a notable encounter with a local official named Zhang Wen in Pingyao County. Zhang Wen, without any apparent illness, suddenly died and awoke several days later. Initially, he saw his deceased brother visit his door. Zhang Wen was greatly startled and asked, "Are you a ghost? Why have you come?" His brother replied, "Since I left the world, I have often thought of my friends and relatives. It is like being blind and unable to forget what I see. Can I relive the joys of our past lives? Now, the underworld official has sent me to visit you." Zhang Wen asked, "Who is this underworld official?" His brother replied, "He is an official in the underworld, holding a very high position. I am now one of his subordinates and often carry out missions in the village. Because the realms of the living and the dead are different, I could not visit you before. Now, the underworld official has summoned you. You should come quickly." Zhang Wen was afraid and tried to decline, but his brother insisted, taking Zhang Wen's sleeve and leading him away.

    173

    行十数里，路曛黑不可辨，但闻车马驰逐，人物喧语，亦闻其妻子兄弟呼者哭者，皆曰：“且议丧具。”汶但与兄俱进，莫知道途之几何。因自念：“我今死矣，然常闻人死，当尽见亲友之殁者，今我即呼之，安知其不可哉？”汶有表弟武季伦者，卒且数年，与汶善，即呼之，果闻季伦应曰：“诺。”既而俱悲泣，汶因谓曰：“今弟之居，为何所也何为曛黑如是？”季伦曰：“冥途幽晦，无日月之光故也。”又曰：“恨不可尽，今将去矣。

    Explanation

    173

    Walking ten or so li, the road had grown so dark that it was indistinguishable. He heard only the sound of carriages and horses galloping, and the voices of people speaking. He also heard his wife, children, and brothers calling and weeping, all saying, “We are now discussing funeral preparations.” Wen proceeded with his brother, knowing not how far the path extended. Then he reflected to himself: “I am now dead. Yet I have often heard that when one dies, one should see all one’s deceased relatives and friends. If I call upon them now, how can I know it is not possible?”

    Wen had a cousin by marriage named Wu Jilun, who had been dead for several years and had been close to Wen. He called out to him, and indeed, he heard Jilun respond, saying, “Yes.” Soon after, they both wept together. Wen then said, “Now, where is your dwelling? Why is it so dark here?” Jilun replied, “This is the path of the dead, dark and obscure, for there is no light of the sun or moon.” He added, “Regrets cannot be fully expressed, but now I must go.”

    174

    ”汶曰：“今何往？”季伦曰：“吾平生时，积罪万状，自委身冥途，日以戮辱。向闻兄之语，故来与兄言，今不可留。”又悲泣久之，遂别。呼亲族中亡殁者数十，咸如季伦应呼而至，多言身被涂炭，词甚凄咽。汶虽前去，亦不知将止何所，但常闻妻子兄弟号哭及语音，历然在左右，因遍呼其名，则如不闻焉。久之，有一人厉呼曰：“平遥县吏张汶。”汶既应曰：“诺。”又有一人责怒汶，问平生之过有几。汶固拒之，于是命案掾出汶之籍。顷闻案掾称曰：“张汶未死，愿遣之。

    Explanation

    174

    "Wen said, 'Where are you going?' Ji Lun replied, 'My life has been filled with countless sins. Since I have been consigned to the underworld, I am daily subjected to humiliation and punishment. I heard your words earlier and came to speak with you, but I cannot stay.' He then wept for a long time and parted ways. He called out the names of dozens of deceased relatives, and they all came as if responding to his call, speaking of their torment and suffering, their words filled with sorrow and lamentation. Though I have gone ahead, I do not know where I will stop, but I often hear the cries of my wife, children, and brothers, as well as their voices, clearly present on all sides. When I called out their names, it was as if they did not hear me. After a while, someone shouted angrily, saying, 'The official of Pingyao County, Zhang Wen.' Zhang Wen replied, 'Yes.' Then another person angrily questioned Zhang Wen, asking how many sins he had committed in his lifetime. Zhang Wen firmly denied it. Then they ordered the clerk to bring out Zhang Wen's record. Soon, the clerk announced, 'Zhang Wen is not dead, and we wish to send him away.'"

    175

    ”冥官怒曰：“汶未当死，何召之？”掾曰：“张汶兄今为此吏，向者许久处冥途，为役且甚，请以弟代，虽未允其请，今自召至此。”冥官怒其兄曰：“何为自召生人，不顾吾法。”即命囚之，而遣汶归。汶谢而出。遂独行，以道路曛晦，惶惑且甚。俄顷，忽见一烛在数十里外，光影极微，汶喜曰：“此烛将非人居乎？”驰走，望影而去。可行百余里，方觉其影稍近，迫而就之，乃见己身偃卧于榻，其室有烛，果汶见者。自是寤。汶即以冥中所闻妻子兄弟号哭及议丧具，讯其家，无一异者。知

    Explanation

    175

    The Underworld Official became angry and said, "Wen did not deserve to die, why did you summon him?" The clerk replied, "My brother Wen is now this official. Previously, he spent a long time in the underworld as a laborer and suffered greatly. I requested to take his place, although my request was not granted. Now, he has summoned me here himself." The Underworld Official rebuked his brother, saying, "Why did you summon a living person without regard for my laws?" He immediately ordered him to be imprisoned and sent Wen back. Wen thanked and left. He then traveled alone, as the road was dim and obscure, and he was extremely anxious and confused. In a short while, he suddenly saw a candle several dozen miles away, with a very faint light. Wen said happily, "Is this candle not from a human dwelling?" He ran quickly, following the shadow. After traveling over a hundred miles, he finally felt that the shadow was getting closer. He approached it and saw himself lying on a bed, with a candle in the room, which was indeed what he had seen. From then on, he woke up. Wen immediately recounted what he had heard in the underworld about his wife, brothers, and sisters crying and discussing funeral arrangements. He inquired at home, and there was not one thing that was different. He knew.

    176

    ○崔君
    　　故崔宁镇易时，犍为守清河崔君，既以启尹真人函，是夕，崔君为冥司所召。其冥官即故相吕諲也，与崔君友善，相见悲泣。已而谓崔曰：“尹真人有石函在贵郡，何为辄开今奉上帝命，召君按验，将如之何？”崔谢曰：“昏俗聋瞽，不识神仙事，故辄开真人之函，罪诚重。然以三宥之典，其不识不知者俱得原赦。傥公宽之，庶获自新耳。”諲曰：“帝命至严，地府卑屑，何敢违乎？”即招按掾出崔君籍。有顷，按掾至，白曰：“崔君余位五任，余寿十五年。今上帝有命，折寿十三年，尽夺其官。”崔又谢曰：“与公平生为友，今日之罪，诚自招，然故人岂不能宥之？”諲曰：“折寿削官，则固不可逃，然可以为足下致二年假职，优其廪禄，用副吾子之托。”崔又载拜谢。

    Explanation

    176

    Cui Jun**

    During the time when Cui Ning was stationed in Yizhou, Cui Jun, the governor of Jianwei, having already opened the stone box of Immortal Yin, that night, Cui Jun was summoned by the officials of the underworld. The underworld official was none other than the former prime minister Lü Yan, who was a close friend of Cui Jun. They met and wept together. Soon after, Lü Yan said to Cui Jun, "Immortal Yin has a stone box in your prefecture. Why did you open it without permission? Now, I am here on the command of the Heavenly Emperor to summon you for verification. What shall we do?" Cui Jun apologized, saying, "The ignorant and blind people of this age do not understand the affairs of immortals, so I rashly opened the box of the Immortal. My sin is indeed grave. However, according to the Threefold Pardon Principle, those who are ignorant or unaware may be pardoned. If you could show mercy, perhaps I might be able to reform myself." Lü Yan replied, "The command of the Heavenly Emperor is extremely strict, and the officials of the underworld are lowly. How dare we disobey?" He then summoned the recorder to bring out Cui Jun's record. Shortly after, the recorder arrived and reported, "Cui Jun has five remaining official positions and fifteen years left of his life. Now, the Heavenly Emperor has decreed that his lifespan will be reduced by thirteen years, and all his official positions will be taken away." Cui Jun apologized again, saying, "I have been your friend all my life. Today's crime is truly self-inflicted, but can an old friend not show leniency?" Lü Yan said, "Reducing your lifespan and stripping you of your official positions is inevitable, but I can grant you a temporary position for two years and provide generous stipends to fulfill my friend's trust." Cui Jun then repeatedly bowed and expressed his gratitude.

    177

    言粗毕，忽有云气蔼然，红光自空而下，諲及庭掾仆吏俱惊惧而起，曰：“天符下！”遂揖崔于一室中。崔即于隙间潜之，见諲具巾笏，率庭掾分立于庭，咸挽而拱，云中有一人，紫衣金鱼，执一幅书，宣道帝命。于是諲及庭掾再拜受书，使驾云而上，顷之遂没。諲命崔君出坐，启天符视之，且叹且泣，谓崔曰：“子识元三乎？”崔曰：“乃布衣之旧耳。”諲曰：“血属无类。吁，可悲夫！某虽与元三为友，至是亦无能拯之，徒积悲叹！”词已又泣。既而命一吏送崔君归。再拜而出，与使者俱行，入郡城廨中，已身卧于榻，妻孥辈哭而环之。使者引崔俯于榻，魂与身翕然而合，遂寤。其家云：“卒三日矣。”本郡以白廉使。崔即治装，尽室往蜀，具告于宁。宁遂署摄副使，月给俸钱二十万。时元载方执国政，宁与载善，书遣甚多，闻崔之言，惧其连坐，因命亲吏赍五百金，赂载左右，尽购得其收百余幅，皆焚之。后月余，元载籍没。又二年，崔亦终矣

    Explanation

    177

    The speech was roughly concluded, when suddenly a cloud-like vapor appeared, with red light descending from the sky. Yin and the court officials and servants all got up in fear, saying, "The heavenly symbol has descended!" Yin then led Cui into a room. Cui immediately peeked through a crack and saw Yin with his headgear and笏 (a tablet used in court), leading the court officials to stand in rows in the courtyard. All of them bowed and clasped their hands. In the cloud, there was a person in a purple robe with golden fish decorations, holding a scroll, and announcing the emperor's command. Yin and the court officials bowed twice and received the scroll, and were lifted up by the cloud and soon disappeared. Yin ordered Cui to come out and sit, and opened the heavenly symbol to look at it, sighing and crying, and said to Cui, "Do you know Yuan San?" Cui replied, "He is just an old acquaintance from my days as a commoner." Yin said, "No blood relations are alike. Alas, how sad! Although I am friends with Yuan San, by now I can no longer save him, only accumulating grief and sighs." After finishing speaking, he wept again. Then he ordered a clerk to send Cui back. Cui bowed twice and left, accompanied by the messenger, entering the government office in the county town. He was already lying on a bed, with his wife and children crying around him. The messenger led Cui to bow before the bed, and his spirit and body merged, and he woke up. His family said, "He has been dead for three days." The county reported this to the censor. Cui immediately packed his belongings, and with his entire family went to Shu, fully informing Ning. Ning then appointed Cui as acting deputy governor, giving him a monthly salary of 200,000 coins. At that time, Yuan Zai was in control of the state affairs, and Ning had a good relationship with him, sending many letters. Upon hearing Cui's words, he feared being implicated and ordered his close officials to carry 500 gold pieces, bribing Yuan Zai's attendants, buying and burning over a hundred scrolls. Two years later, Cui also died.

    178

    ○刘溉
    　　彭城刘溉者，贞元中为韩城令，卒于官。家甚贫，因寄韩城佛寺中。岁未半，其县丞窦亦卒，三日而寤。知
    　　初，窦生昼寐，梦一吏导而西去，经高原大泽，数百里，抵一城。既入门，导吏亡去。生惧甚，即出城门。门有卫卒，举剑而列。窦生讯之，卫卒举剑南指曰：“由此走生道耳。”窦始知身死，背汗而髀怵。即南去，虽殆，不敢息。俄见十余人立道左，有一人呼窦生，挚其手以泣，熟视之，乃刘溉，曰：“吾子何为而来？”窦具以告，曰：“我自与足下别，若委身于陷井中，念平生时安可得？”因涕泣。窦即讯冥途事，溉泣不语。久之，又曰：“我妻子安在得无恙乎？”窦曰：“贤子侨居韩城佛寺中，将半岁矣。”溉曰：“子今去，为我问讯。我以穷泉困辱，邈不可脱，每念妻孥，若肘而不忘步。幽显之恨，何可尽道哉？”别，谓窦曰：“我有诗赠君，曰：

    Explanation

    178

    ○ Liu Gai
    　　Liu Gai, from Pengcheng, served as the magistrate of Hancheng during the reign of Emperor Dezong. He died in office, leaving his family in poverty. They then lived in a temple in Hancheng. Before the end of the year, the county magistrate Sin also died and woke up after three days. At first, Sin slept during the day and dreamed of a clerk leading him westward, passing through highlands and large lakes for hundreds of miles, and reaching a city. After entering the gate, the guiding clerk disappeared. Sin was very afraid and immediately left the city gate. There were guards at the gate, holding swords and standing in formation. Sin asked them, and the guards raised their swords and pointed south, saying, "This is the way to the path of life." Sin then knew he was dead, his back was sweaty, and his thighs trembled. He immediately went south, even though he was exhausted, he dared not rest. Soon, he saw more than ten people standing on the side of the road, one person called out to Sin, took his hand and wept, looked at him carefully, and it was Liu Gai. He said, "What are you doing here?" Sin told him everything, and Liu Gai said, "Since we parted ways, as if I had fallen into a pit, thinking about my life, how could I have it again?" He then wept. Sin then asked about the afterlife, and Liu Gai wept but did not speak. After a long time, he said, "Where are my wife and children, are they safe?" Sin said, "Your son is living in a temple in Hancheng for almost half a year." Liu Gai said, "You will go now, to ask for news. I am in a deep and shameful place, far removed and unable to escape. Every time I think of my wife and children, as if they were close but I never forget to walk. The sorrow of being in the afterlife, how can it be fully expressed?" After parting, Liu Gai said to Sin, "I have a poem to give to you, it says:

    179

    冥路杳杳人不知，不用苦说使人悲
    　　喜得逢君传家信，后会茫茫何处期。已而又泣。窦遂告别。未十余里，闻击钟声极震响，因悸而寤。知
    　　窦即师锡従祖兄，其甥崔氏子常以事语于人
    ○樊钦贲
    　　寇天师谦之，后魏时得道者也。常刻石为记，藏于嵩山。上元初，有洛川郜城县民，因采药于山，得之以献县令樊文。言于州，州以上闻，高宗皇帝诏藏于内府。其铭记文甚多，奥不可解。略曰“木子当天下”；又曰“止戈龙”；又曰“李代代不可移宗”；又曰“中鼎显真容”；又曰“基千万岁”。所谓“木子当天下”者，盖言唐氏受命也。“止戈龙”者，言天后临朝也。止戈为“武”，武，天后氏也。“李代代不移宗”者，谓中宗中兴，再新天地。“中鼎显真容”者，实真宗之庙讳，“真”为睿圣之徽谥，得不信乎？“基千万岁”者，“基”玄宗名也，“千万岁”，盖历数久长也。后中宗御历，樊文男钦贲以石记本上献，上命编于国史

    Explanation

    179

    The Path of the Dead is far and unknown, people do not know; there is no need to speak of it to cause sorrow.
    Gladly met you to deliver family news, the future meeting is uncertain and hard to predict. Afterward, he wept again. Dong bid farewell. Not even ten miles away, he heard the sound of the bell ringing very loudly, awoke due to fear. Knew that Dong was the cousin of Shixi, his nephew, Cui's son, often told others about the matter.

    Master Kou Qianzhi, a person who attained enlightenment during the Northern Wei period, often carved stones as records, stored in Mount Song. At the beginning of the Shangyuan era, a resident of Gaocun County in Luochuan, while gathering herbs in the mountains, found it and presented it to County Magistrate Fan Wen. Reported to the prefecture, the prefecture reported it to the imperial court, Emperor Gaozong ordered it to be stored in the imperial treasury. The inscriptions on the stone are numerous, deep and difficult to understand. Roughly says "wood and son will rule the world"; also says "the one who stops the sword is the dragon"; also says "the Li family will never change its lineage"; also says "in the middle of the tripod, the true form will be revealed"; also says "the foundation will last for a thousand years." What is meant by "wood and son will rule the world," it refers to the Tang dynasty being granted the mandate of heaven. "The one who stops the sword is the dragon," it refers to Empress Wu Zetian. "The Li family will never change its lineage," it means the Tang dynasty's continuity. "In the middle of the tripod, the true form will be revealed," it is the temple name of Emperor Zhenzong, "Zhen" is the posthumous title of Emperor Zhenzong, how can this not be believed? "The foundation will last for a thousand years," "Ji" is the name of Emperor Xuanzong, "a thousand years," indicating a long lineage. After Emperor Zhongzong ascended the throne, Fan Wen's son, Qinben, presented the original stone inscription to the court, the emperor ordered it to be compiled into the national history.

    180

    ○姜师度
    　　卫先生大经，解梁人，以文学闻。不狎俗，常闭门绝人事。生而敏悟。周知天文历象，穷冥索玄，后以寿终，墓于解梁之野。开元中大水，姜师度奉诏凿无碱河以溉盐田，刬室庐、溃丘墓甚多，解梁人皆病之。既至卫先生墓前，发基地，得一石刻字为铭，盖先生之词也。曰：“姜师度更移向南三五步。”工人得之，以状言之于师度。师度异其事，叹咏久之。顾谓僚吏曰：“卫先生真奇士也。”即命工人迁其河，远先生之墓数十步

    Explanation

    180

    Wei Dajing, a man renowned for his literary achievements, was from Jieyang. He avoided the vulgar and often shut himself off from worldly matters. Born with keen insight, he fully understood astronomy and calendar systems, seeking profound truths. Later, he died of old age and was buried in the wilds of Jieyang. During the reign of Emperor Xuanzong (Tang Dynasty), a great flood occurred, and Jiang Shidu, following an imperial decree, dug the Wubian River to irrigate the salt fields. This project involved demolishing many houses and tombs, which the people of Jieyang resented. Upon reaching Wei Dajing's tomb, Jiang Shidu excavated the foundation and found an inscribed stone with an epitaph, which was the words of the master. It read: "Jiang Shidu, move it three or five steps to the south." The workers found this and reported it to Jiang Shidu. Jiang Shidu was amazed by the incident and exclaimed and praised for a long time. Turning to his officials, he said, "Wei Dajing is truly a remarkable man." He immediately ordered the workers to move the river several dozen steps away from the master's tomb.

    181

    ○邬载
    　　开元中，江南大水，溺而死者数千。郡以状闻，玄宗诏侍御史邬君载往巡之。载至江南，忽见道傍有古墓，水溃其穴。公念之，命迁其骸于高原上。既发墓，得一石，凿而成文，盖志其墓也。志后有铭二十言，乃卜地者之词。词曰：斋
    　　“尔后一千岁，此地化为泉。赖逢邬侍御，移我向高原”
    　　载览而异之。因校其年，果千岁矣。知
    ○韩愈（二）
    　　泉州之南有山焉。其山峻起壁立，下有潭水，深不可测，周十余亩。中有蛟螭，尝为人患，人有误近，或马牛就而饮者，辄为吞食，泉人苦之有年矣。由是近山居者，咸挈引妻子徙去他郡，以逃其患。元和五年，一夕闻山南有雷震暴兴，震数百里，若山崩之状，一郡惊惧。里人洎牛马鸡犬俱失声仆地，流汗被体。屋瓦交击，木树颠拔。自戌及子，雷电方息。明旦往视之，其山摧堕，石壁数百仞殆尽，俱填其潭。

    Explanation

    181

    Wu Junzai

    During the Kaiyuan era, a great flood struck the southern regions, drowning thousands of people. The local government reported this disaster to the central government, and Emperor Xuanzong ordered the imperial censor Wu Junzai to inspect the area. Upon arriving, Wu Junzai suddenly saw an ancient tomb by the roadside, whose entrance had been eroded by the water. He ordered the remains to be moved to high ground. After opening the tomb, they found a stone with inscriptions carved on it, which was a record of the tomb. Following the inscriptions, there were twenty words, which were the words of the person who chose the burial site. The words said:

    "Thousand years after, this place will turn into a spring. Thanks to meeting the imperial censor Wu, we were moved to high ground."

    Wu Junzai read this and found it remarkable. He checked the year and confirmed that it was indeed a thousand years later.

    Han Yu (II)

    South of Quanzhou, there is a mountain. The mountain rises abruptly and stands vertically. Below it is a deep pool, whose depth is unknown, covering an area of about ten mu. There are dragons and snakes in the water, which have been a problem for the people. Anyone who accidentally approaches or even animals that come to drink are often swallowed. The people of Quanzhou have suffered from this for many years. As a result, those living near the mountain have all moved their families to other cities to escape the danger.

    In the fifth year of the Yuanhe era, one night, there was a sudden and violent thunderstorm in the south of the mountain, shaking the earth for hundreds of miles, as if the mountain itself was collapsing. The entire city was in fear. The locals, along with their cattle, horses, chickens, and dogs, all fell to the ground, sweating profusely. The tiles on the roofs clashed, and the trees were uprooted. From 7 PM to 1 AM, the thunder and lightning finally ceased. The next morning, they went to see what had happened. The mountain had collapsed, and the stone cliffs, hundreds of ren high, were almost completely destroyed, filling the pond.

    182

    水溢流，注满四野，蛟螭之血，遍若玄黄，而石壁之上，有凿成文字一十九言，字势甚古。郡中士庶，无能知者。自是居人无复患矣。惧者既息，迁者亦归，结屋架庐，接比其地。郡守因之名其地为“石铭里”，盖因字为铭，且识其异也。后有客于泉者，能传其字，持至东洛。时故吏部侍郎韩愈自尚书郎为河南令，见而识之。其文曰：“诏示黑水鲤鱼，天公卑杀牛人，壬癸神书急急。”然则详究其义，似上帝责蛟螭之词令，戮其害也。其字则蝌蚪篆书，故泉人无有识者矣

    Explanation

    182

    A flood overflowed, spreading to fill the four fields, and the blood of the dragon and the serpent spread like black and yellow. On the stone wall, there were carved words, nineteen in number, with an ancient style. The scholars and commoners of the county could not understand them. Since then, the residents no longer suffered. Those who had been afraid ceased to fear, and those who had moved also returned, building houses and huts, connecting their land. The prefecture governor named the place "Shi Ming Li" because of it, as a memorial to the characters and to recognize its uniqueness. Later, a guest from the spring was able to transmit the characters, bringing them to Dong Luo. At that time, the former Ministry of Personnel Deputy Secretary Han Yu, who was a Prefect of Henan from the position of Secretary of the Ministry of Rites, saw and recognized them. The text reads: "Edict to the black water carp, Heaven's deity humbles the ox-slaughtering man, the divine book of Ren and Gui is urgent." Thus, examining its meaning in detail, it seems to be a command from the Heavenly Deity to punish the dragon and serpent for their harm. The characters are in the form of seal script, so the people of the spring did not recognize them.

    183

    ○裴度
    　　元和元中秋九月，淮西师吴少诚死，子元济拒命，诏邻淮西者以兵四攻之，凡数年不克。十三年，召丞相晋国公裴度将而击焉。度既至，因命封人深池濠，且发其地。有得一石者，上有雕出文字为铭，封人持以献度。文曰：“井底一竿竹，竹色深绿绿。鸡未肥，酒未熟，障车儿郎且须缩。”度得之，以示従事，令辩其义焉，咸不能究。度方念之，俄有一卒自行间跃而贺曰：“吴元济逆天子命，纵狂兵为反谋。赖天子威圣与丞相德，合不久逆竖成擒矣。敢贺丞相功。

    Explanation

    183

    In the autumn of the ninth month of the first year of the Yuanhe era, Wu Shaocheng, the military governor of Huai West, died. His son, Wu Yuanji, defied imperial orders and rebelled. The emperor then ordered neighboring regions to attack Huai West from all sides with their armies, but after several years, they were unable to defeat him. In the thirteenth year of the Yuanhe era, the chancellor, Duke of Jin, Pei Du, was summoned to lead the campaign. Upon his arrival, Pei Du ordered the workers to deepen the moat and fortify the area, and he also ordered the digging of the ground. One of the workers found a stone with inscribed text, which he presented to Pei Du. The text read: "A bamboo pole stands at the bottom of a well, its color deep green. The chicken is not yet fat, the wine not yet brewed, the boys blocking the carriage should retreat." Pei Du received the stone and showed it to his subordinates, asking them to interpret its meaning. None of them could fully understand it. As Pei Du was pondering the inscription, a soldier suddenly leaped forward from the ranks and exclaimed: "Wu Yuanji defies the emperor's command, allowing his troops to plot rebellion. Thanks to the emperor's divine authority and the chancellor's virtue, it will not be long before the rebel is captured. I dare to congratulate the chancellor on his victory."

    184

    ”度惊讯之，对曰：“封人得石铭，是其兆也。且‘井底一竿竹，竹色深深绿’者，言吴少诚由行间一卒，遂拥十万兵，为一方师，且喻其荣也。‘鸡未肥’者，言无肉也。夫以‘肥’去‘肉’为‘己’字也。‘酒未熟’者，言无水也。以‘酒’去‘水’，为‘酉’字。‘障车儿郎’谓兵革之士也。‘且须缩’者，谓宜退守其所也。推是言之，则己酉日当克也。苟未及期，则可俟矣。”度喜顾左右曰：“卒，辨者也。”叹而异之。是岁冬十月，相国李朔将兵入淮西，生得元济，尽诸反者。度因校其日，果己酉焉。于是度益奇卒之辨，擢为裨将

    Explanation

    184

    Dù was surprised and asked about it. The response was: "A seal engraver found an inscription on a stone, which is an omen. Moreover, 'A bamboo pole at the bottom of a well, with deep green color' refers to Wu Shaocheng rising from a common soldier to command ten thousand troops, becoming a leader of one region, and symbolizing his glory. 'The chicken is not fat' means there is no meat. By removing 'meat' from 'fat,' we get the character '己.' 'The wine is not ripe' means there is no water. By removing 'water' from 'wine,' we get the character '酉.' 'Obstructing the carriage' refers to soldiers or guards. 'And should retreat' means one should withdraw and defend one's position. From this, it can be deduced that on the day of Jǐ Yǒu, there will be a victory. If it has not occurred by the expected time, it can still happen later." Dù was pleased and turned to those around him, saying, "The soldier is a discerning person." He sighed and found it remarkable. In the tenth month of that year, the Chancellor Li Shuo led his troops into Huai West, capturing Yuan Ji and all the rebels. Dù then checked the date and found it to be indeed Jǐ Yǒu. Thus, Dù further admired the soldier's discernment and promoted him to the rank of deputy general.

    185

    ○张惟清
    　　黑山之阴有李卫公庙。宝历中，张惟清都护单于，其従事卢立尝梦一人，颀长黑衣，告立曰：“吾居于卫公庙且久矣，子幸迁我于军城中。”已而遂去。及晓，立不谕，即入白于惟清曰：“卫公于国有大勋，今庙宇隳残，飘濡且甚，愿新其土木之制。”惟清喜而可其语。先是单于府以惟清有美化，状其政绩，请护军骆忠表闻于上。有诏命中书舍人高公釴文其事，刻于碑。诏既至而未有碑石，惟清方命使采石于云中郡，未还。及修卫公庙，铲其西，得一石，方而长，其下有刻出“张”字，历然可辨。工人持以献惟清。惟清喜曰：“天赐吾之碑石。”即召従事视之。立且惊且异，因起贺而白前梦。于是以石为碑，刻高公之文焉。知

    Explanation

    185

    In the shadow of Heishan Mountain lies the Temple of Li Weigong. During the Bao Li era, Zhang Weiqing, the Protector General of the Xiongnu, had a clerk named Lu Li who once dreamed of a tall man dressed in black. The man told Lu Li, "I have lived in the temple of Wei Gong for a long time. Please move me to the military city." Afterward, he disappeared. Upon waking, Lu Li was confused and immediately reported to Zhang Weiqing, saying, "Wei Gong has great merit for the state. Now the temple is in ruins, severely affected by wind and rain. I wish to renovate its structure." Zhang Weiqing was pleased and agreed to his request. Previously, the Xiongnu government had praised Zhang Weiqing's good governance, described his achievements, and requested General Luo Zhong to report to the emperor. An edict was issued for the Secretary of the Imperial Secretariat, Gao Gong Tai, to write about the matter and carve it on a stele. The edict had arrived, but there was no stele. Zhang Weiqing then sent someone to collect stone from Yunzhong Prefecture, who had not yet returned. When repairing the temple of Wei Gong, the western part was excavated, and a stone was found, square and long, with a clearly visible "Zhang" character carved on it. The workers presented it to Zhang Weiqing. Zhang Weiqing was delighted and said, "Heaven has given me the stele stone." He then summoned the clerk to see it. Lu Li was both surprised and amazed and stood up to congratulate, reporting the previous dream. Thus, the stone was used as a stele, and Gao Gong Tai's writing was carved on it.

    186

    ○王璠
    　　大和中，王璠廉问丹阳，因沟其城，既凿深数尺，得一石，铭文曰：“山有石，石有玉，玉有瑕，即休也。”工人得之，具以事告白献于璠。详其义，久而不能解。即命僚佐辨之，皆无能析其理者。数日，有一叟请谒璠之吏，且密谓曰：“吾闻王公得石铭，今有辨者乎？”吏曰：“公方念之。其义为何如君岂即能究耶！”叟曰：“是不祥也。夫‘山有石，石有玉，玉有瑕，即休也’，皆叙王公之世也。且公之先曰崟，崟生础，以文而观，是‘山有石’也。础生璠，是‘石有玉’也。崟之子曰瑕休，是‘玉有瑕即休’。休者，绝之兆。推是而辨，其绝绪乎！”吏谢之。叟言竟而去。至大和九年冬，璠卒，夷其宗，果符叟之解也

    Explanation

    186

    During the Dàhé period, Wang Fán, while investigating Dan-yáng, had a moat dug around the city. When they dug several feet deep, they discovered a stone with inscriptions that read: "There is a mountain with a stone, the stone contains jade, the jade has a flaw, and thus it ceases to be." The workers found the stone and reported the matter to Wang Fán in detail. After studying the meaning, they could not understand it. He then ordered his officials to interpret it, but none could explain the reasoning. Several days later, an old man asked to see Wang Fán’s secretary and privately said, "I heard that Master Wang found an inscribed stone. Is there anyone who can interpret it now?" The secretary replied, "Master Wang is still thinking about it. How could you possibly understand it right away?" The old man said, "This is an ominous sign. The phrase 'There is a mountain with a stone, the stone contains jade, the jade has a flaw, and thus it ceases to be' describes Master Wang’s lineage. Your ancestor was named Jīn, who gave birth to Chu. From the text, 'There is a mountain with a stone' refers to this. Chu gave birth to Fán, which means 'the stone contains jade.' Jīn’s son was named Xiá Xiū, which means 'the jade has a flaw and thus it ceases to be.' 'Xiū' is a sign of the end of the lineage. Based on this interpretation, it seems the lineage will come to an end!" The secretary thanked him. The old man finished speaking and left. In the ninth year of the Dàhé period in winter, Wang Fán died, and his clan was destroyed, just as the old man had predicted.

    187

    ○柳光
    　　太和中，有柳光者，尝南游，因行山道，会日晚，误入山崦中。松引盘曲，行数里，至一石室。云水环拥，清泉交贯，室有茵榻，若人居者。前对霞翠，固非人境。光因临流凝伫，忽见一缶合于地，光即趋之，其缶下有泉，周不尽尺，其水清激，举卮以饮，若甘醴。尽十余卮而已醉甚，遂偃于榻。及晓方寤，因视石壁有雕刻文字极多，遂写其字置于袖，词曰：主

    Explanation

    187

    During the Tahe era, there was a man named Liu Guang who traveled south. As he walked along a mountain path, the evening approached, and he mistakenly entered a deep mountain ravine. The pines wound and twisted, and he walked several miles before reaching a stone chamber. Clouds and water surrounded the chamber, and clear springs intersected within it. The chamber contained a couch, as if it were inhabited by a person. In front of him was a colorful sunset, indicating that it was certainly not a place for human habitation. Liu Guang stood by the stream, gazing fixedly, when he suddenly saw a jar lying on the ground. He immediately approached it, and beneath the jar was a spring, no more than a foot wide. The water was clear and refreshing. He lifted a cup to drink, and it tasted like sweet liquor. After drinking more than ten cups, he became extremely drunk and lay down on the couch. By morning, he awoke and saw that the stone wall was covered with numerous inscriptions. He wrote down the characters and placed them in his sleeve. The inscriptions read:

    188

    “武之在卯，尧王八季。我弃其寝，我去其扆。深深然，高高然，人不吾知，人不吾谓。由今之后，二百余祀，焰焰其光，和和其始。东方有兔，小首元尾，经过吾道，来至吾里。饮吾泉以醉，登吾榻而寐。刻乎其壁，奥乎其义，人谁以辨其东平子。”光先阅，阅而异之。遂行，出径数十步，回望其室，尽亡见矣
    　　光究之不得，有吕生者，视而解之，未几告曰：“吾尽详矣。此乃得道者语也。夫唐氏之初，建号武德，武之二年，其岁己卯，则‘武之在卯’，其义见矣，盖武德二年也。‘尧王’者，谓高祖之号神尧。曰‘八季’者，亦二年也。‘我弃其寝，我去其扆’者，言其去，盖绝去之时，乃武德二年也。‘深深然，高高然，人不吾知，人不吾谓’者，言其隐而人不知也。‘由今之后，二百余祀’者，言君者来也。且唐氏之初，今果二百余矣。

    Explanation

    188

    During the year of the rabbit, in the reign of Emperor Gaozu of Tang, who took the title "Emperor of Tang" (唐王) for eight years, I abandoned my palace and left my throne. Deep and high, I was not known nor recognized by others. From this point onward, more than two hundred years later, the light shone brightly and harmoniously at its beginning. In the east, there was a rabbit with a small head and a long tail, passing through my path and arriving at my village. It drank from my spring and became intoxicated, climbing onto my bed to sleep. Carved into the wall, the profound meaning was difficult to discern, and who could understand the message left by Dongpingzi? Guang read it and found it unusual. He then walked out a few dozen steps and looked back at the room, but it was nowhere to be seen.

    Guang could not understand it. A man named Lü came and examined it, explaining shortly afterward: "I have understood everything. This is the speech of one who has attained the Dao. At the beginning of the Tang dynasty, the title 'Wude' was established, and in the second year of Wude, which was the year of the rabbit (己卯), '武之在卯' refers to the second year of Wude. '尧王' refers to Emperor Gaozu, who took the title 'Emperor of Tang' (唐王). '八季' also refers to eight years. '我弃其寝，我去其扆' means that he left, and indeed, he left in the second year of Wude. '深深然，高高然，人不吾知，人不吾谓' means that he was hidden and unknown by others. '由今之后，二百余祀' means that the ruler will come. And indeed, the Tang dynasty has now lasted for more than two hundred years."

    189

    ‘焰焰其光，和和其始’者，‘焰焰其光’谓岁在丁未也。焰者火，岂非南方之丙丁之谓乎未亦火之位也。’和和其始’，谓今天子建号曰‘太和’，‘其始’盖元年也。‘东方有兔，小首元尾’者，叙君之名氏。‘东方’，甲乙木也。‘兔’者，卯也。‘卯’以附‘木’，是‘柳’字也。‘小首元尾’，是光也。‘经吾道’、‘来吾里’，言君之来也。‘饮吾泉以醉，登吾榻而寐’，言君之止也。‘刻乎其壁，奥乎其义。谁人以辨，其东平子’，谓其义奥而隐，独吾能辨之。‘东平’，吾之邑也，即又信矣。”如是而辨，果得道者之遣记也。知

    Explanation

    189

    The bright and intense light, and the gentle and harmonious beginning—this refers to the year of the Goat, "丁未." The flame is fire, is it not the Southern fire of the Heavenly Stems, and "未" is also the position of fire. The "gentle and harmonious beginning" refers to the current emperor who has established the era name "Taihe," and "its beginning" is the first year. The phrase "in the east there is a rabbit, with a small head and a long tail" describes the emperor's name and surname. "The east" is the position of wood in the Heavenly Stems, "rabbit" is the sign of the rabbit, and "卯" is attached to "wood," which forms the character "柳." "Small head and long tail" refers to the light. "Traveling along my path" and "coming to my village" describe the emperor's arrival. "Drinking from my spring to get drunk, and sleeping on my bed" describe the emperor's stay. "Inscribed on the wall, profound and hidden in meaning. Who can interpret it? It is the East Pingzi," meaning that the meaning is profound and hidden, and only I can interpret it. "East Pingzi" is my hometown, and thus it is more credible. Thus, it is determined that this is indeed a record left by one who has attained the Dao.

    190

    ○李师道（二）
    　　唐元和中，李师道据青齐，蓄兵勇锐，地广千里，储积数百万，不贡不觐。宪宗命将讨之，王师不利，而师道益骄。乃建新宫，拟天子正殿，卜日而居。是夕云物遽晦，风雷如撼，遂为震击倾圯，俄复继以天火，了无遗者。青齐人相顾语曰：“为人臣而逆其君者，祸固宜矣。今谪见于天，安可逃其戾乎？”旬余，师道果诛死

    Explanation

    190

    During the Yuanhe period of the Tang Dynasty, Li Shidao occupied the regions of Qing and Qi, maintaining a large and brave army, controlling a territory of a thousand miles, and accumulating millions in wealth. He refused to pay tribute or visit the emperor. Emperor Xianzong ordered generals to attack him, but the imperial army was unsuccessful, and Li Shidao became even more arrogant. He then built a new palace modeled after the emperor's main hall and chose a day to move in. That night, the sky suddenly turned dark, the wind and thunder shook violently, and it was struck by lightning and collapsed. Soon after, it was destroyed by a fire from heaven, leaving nothing standing. The people of Qing and Qi looked at each other and said, "Those who rebel against their sovereign as subjects certainly deserve punishment. Now the divine retribution is evident; how can they escape their punishment?" A few days later, Li Shidao was indeed executed.

    191

    ○萧氏子
    　　唐长庆中，兰陵萧氏子，以胆勇称。客游湘楚，至长沙郡，舍于仰山寺。是夕，独处撤烛，忽暴雷震荡檐宇，久而不止。俄闻西垣下窣窣有声，萧恃膂力，曾不之畏，榻前有巨捶，持至垣下，俯而扑焉。一举而中，有声甚厉，若呼吟者。因连扑数十，声遂绝，风雨亦霁。萧喜曰：“怪且死矣。”迨晓，西垣下睹一鬼极异：身尽青，伛而庳，有金斧木楔，以麻缕结其体焉，瞬而喘，若甚困状。于是具告寺僧观之。或曰：“此雷鬼也，盖上帝之使耳。子何为侮于上帝，祸且及矣。”里中人具牲酒祀之。俄而云气曛晦，自室中发，出户升天，鬼亦従去，既而雷声又兴，仅数食顷方息。萧气益锐，里中人皆以壮士名焉

    Explanation

    191

    A Son of the Xiao Family

    During the Longqing era of the Tang Dynasty, a son of the Xiao family from Lanling was renowned for his bravery. Traveling as a guest in the regions of Xiang and Chu, he arrived at Changsha Prefecture and stayed at Yanshan Temple. One evening, as he was alone and had lit a candle, a sudden thunderstorm shook the eaves, lasting for a long time. Soon, he heard a rustling sound under the western wall. Xiao, relying on his physical strength, was not afraid. By the bed, there was a large mallet. He carried it to the wall and bent down to strike the source of the sound. With one blow, he struck it, and a loud cry was heard, as if it was moaning. He then struck it dozens of times in succession, and the sound finally ceased. The rain and wind also stopped. Xiao was pleased and said, "The monster must be dead." By dawn, under the western wall, he saw a very strange ghost: its body was entirely green, it was hunched and short, and it had a golden axe and wooden wedge, tied with hemp threads around its body. It blinked and panted, as if very exhausted. He then reported it to the temple monks for them to see. One of them said, "This is a thunder ghost, it is the messenger of God. Why do you insult God? Disaster will soon befall you." The villagers prepared sacrificial animals and wine to worship it. Soon, clouds and mist became dark and dim, emerging from the room, it went out the door and ascended to heaven, and the ghost also followed. Afterward, thunder sounded again, lasting for a few meals before stopping. Xiao's courage grew even stronger, and the villagers all called him a brave warrior.

    192

    ○东阳郡山
    　　唐东阳郡滨于浙江，有山周数百里，江水曲而环焉。迁滞舟楫，人颇病之。常侍敬昕，大和中出守，其山一夕云物曛晦，暴风雷电，动荡室庐，江水腾溢，莫不惶惑。迨晓方霁，人往视之，已劈而中分，相远数百步，引江流直而贯焉，其环曲处悉填以石。遂无萦绕之患。知

    192

    The mountains of Dongyang Prefecture during the Tang Dynasty bordered the Zhejiang River, surrounding several hundred li. The river bent and encircled the mountains, obstructing boats and rafts, causing great trouble for the people. During the Dazhong period, the high-ranking official Jing Xin was sent to govern Dongyang. One night, the mountains were shrouded in dim clouds and mist, and a severe storm with strong winds, thunder, and lightning shook houses and buildings. The river water overflowed, and no one was not alarmed and confused. Until dawn, the storm finally cleared up, and people went to see what had happened. The mountains were already split in half, separated by several hundred steps, and the river flow was guided straight through, filling all the curved sections with stones. Thus, the trouble of winding around was no longer a problem.

    193

    ○智空
    　　唐晋陵郡建元寺僧智空，本郡人，道行闻于里中，年七十余。一夕，既阖关，忽大风雷，若起于禅堂，殷然不绝，烛灭而尘坌，晦黑且甚，檐宇摇震。矍然自念曰：“吾弃家为僧，迨兹四纪。暴雷如是，岂神龙有怒我者不然，有罪当雷震死耳。”既而声益甚，复坐而祝曰：“某少学浮屠氏，为沙门迨五十余年，岂所行乖于释氏教耶不然，且有黩神龙耶设如是，安敢逃其死！傥不然，则愿亟使开霁，俾举寺僧得自解也。”言竟，大声一举，若发左右，茵榻倾糜，昏霾颠悖。由是惊慴仆地。仅食顷，声方息，云月晴朗。然觉有腥腐气，如在室内，因烛视之，于垣下得一蛟，皮长数丈，血满于地。乃是禅堂北有槐，高数十寻，为雷震死，循理而裂，中有蛟蟠之迹焉

    Explanation

    193

    The Monk Zhikong

    During the Tang Dynasty, there was a monk named Zhikong at Jianyuan Temple in Jinling Prefecture. He was a native of the prefecture, and his spiritual practice was renowned in the local area. He was over seventy years old. One night, after closing the temple gates, a sudden strong wind and thunder arose as if from the meditation hall, rumbling continuously. The candles went out, and dust filled the air, making it very dark and murky. The eaves and buildings shook. Suddenly, he thought to himself, "I abandoned my family to become a monk, and I have been a monk for over forty years. Such a violent thunderstorm, could it be that the dragon is angry with me? Otherwise, I deserve to be struck dead by lightning." As the sound became even louder, he sat down again and prayed, "When I was young, I studied Buddhism and have been a monk for over fifty years. Could my actions be contrary to Buddhist teachings? Otherwise, could I be offending the dragon? If that is the case, how dare I escape death! If not, then I hope for the storm to clear quickly, so that all the monks in the temple can be freed." After he finished speaking, a loud clap of thunder sounded, as if it came from all sides. The mats and beds were overturned and destroyed, and the darkness and confusion were overwhelming. He was so shocked that he fell to the ground. Only a short time later, the sound finally stopped, and the sky cleared, revealing the moon and stars. But he noticed a foul smell, as if it were inside the room. He lit a candle to see and found a dragon under the wall, with a skin several zhang long, and blood covering the ground. It was a tree in the north of the meditation hall, tens of ren tall, which was struck dead by lightning, splitting along its grain, with traces of a dragon coiled inside.

    194

    ○百丈泓
    　　唐河东群东南百余里有积水，谓之“百丈泓”。清澈，纤毫必鉴。在驿路之左，槐柳环拥，烟影如束，途出于此者，乃为憩驾之所。大和五年夏，有徐生自洛阳抵河东，至此水，困殆既甚，因而暂息，且吟且望。将午，忽闻水中有细声若蝇蚋之噪，俄而纤光发，其音稍响，輷若击毂，其光如索而曳焉。生始异之，声久益繁，遂有雷自波间声，震光为电，接云气。至旅次遽话其事，答曰：“此百丈泓也。岁旱未尝不指期而雨。今旱且甚，吾师命属官祷焉，巫者曰：‘某日当有甚雨。’果是日矣。

    Explanation

    194

    In the eastern part of the Tang territory, about a hundred miles southeast of Hedong, there is a body of water called "Baizhang Hong." It is so clear that even the finest details can be seen. On the left side of the post road, it is surrounded by willows and elms, with smoke and shadows clustering together, making it a place for travelers to rest. In the fifth year of the Dazhong era during the summer, Xu Sheng traveled from Luoyang to Hedong. Upon reaching this water, he was extremely tired and exhausted, so he took a brief rest, reciting poetry and looking around. As noon approached, he suddenly heard a faint sound like the buzzing of flies coming from the water. Soon, a thin light appeared, and the sound grew louder, like the pounding of wheels, with the light resembling a rope being dragged. Xu Sheng was surprised by this, and as the sound grew more frequent, a thunderclap came from the water, with the light flashing like lightning and connecting with the clouds. He quickly told the story to his traveling companions, who replied, "This is Baizhang Hong. It never fails to rain at the predicted time during droughts. Now the drought is severe, and my teacher has ordered the officials to pray at this place. The shaman said, 'There will be heavy rain on a certain day.' And indeed, it happened on that day."

    195

    ○杨询美従子
    　　唐御史杨询美居广陵郡。従子数人皆幼，始従师学。尝一夕，风雨雷电震耀，诸子俱出户望，且笑且詈曰：“我闻雷有鬼，不知鬼安在，愿得而杀之可乎？”既而雷声愈震，林木倾靡。忽一声轰然，若在于庑，诸子惊甚，即驰入户，负壁而立，不敢辄动。复闻雷声若大呵地吼，庐舍摇动，诸子益惧。近食顷，雷电方息，天月清霁，庭有大槐，击拔其根而劈之。诸子觉两髀痛不可忍，具告询美，命家僮执烛视之，诸髀咸有赤文，横布十数，状类杖痕，似鬼雷之所为也

    Explanation

    195

    Imperial Censor Yang Xunmei's Nephews

    During the Tang dynasty, Imperial Censor Yang Xunmei lived in Guangling Prefecture. He had several nephews who were young and had just begun their studies. One night, there was a storm with wind, rain, thunder, and lightning. The nephews all went out to look and said, "We heard that there are ghosts in the thunder, but where are they? Can we catch them and kill them?" Soon, the thunder became even louder, and the trees were knocked down. Suddenly, there was a loud boom, as if it was right in the eaves. The nephews were very frightened, so they ran back into the house, stood against the wall, and dared not move. Then they heard the thunder as if the earth was roaring, and the houses were shaking. The nephews were even more afraid. After a meal's worth of time, the thunder and lightning stopped, and the sky cleared up. There was a large locust tree in the courtyard, and its roots were pulled out and split. The nephews felt pain in their thighs that they could not bear, and they told Yang Xunmei about it. He ordered a servant to bring a candle to look at them, and they all had red marks, spread across ten or so places, looking like marks from a stick, as if they were caused by ghostly thunder.

    196

    ○韦思玄
    　　宝应中，有京兆韦思玄，侨居洛阳。性尚奇，尝慕神仙之术。后游嵩山，有道士教曰：“夫饵金液者，可以延寿。吾子当先学炼金，如是，则可以肩赤松、驾广成矣。”思玄于是求炼金之术。积十年，遇术士数百，终不能得其妙。后一日，有居士辛锐者，貌甚清瘦，愀然有寒色，衣弊裘，叩思玄门，谓思玄曰：“吾病士，穷无所归。闻先生好古尚奇，集天下异人方士。我故来谒耳，愿先生纳之。”思玄即止居士于舍。

    Explanation

    196

    During the reign of Emperor Suzong (Baoying period), there was a man named Wei Si Xuan from Jingzhao who was temporarily residing in Luoyang. He had a nature that preferred the unusual and once admired the techniques of immortals. Later, he traveled to Mount Song and encountered a Taoist who taught him: "Those who consume elixirs of gold can extend their lifespan. You should first learn to refine gold, and if so, then you can shoulder the burden of Cesi and ride upon Guangchengzi." Wei Si Xuan thus sought to learn the art of refining gold. Over ten years, he met hundreds of practitioners but ultimately could not grasp the subtleties. One day, a recluse named Xin Rui appeared, looking very thin and with a sad, pale appearance, wearing tattered fur, and knocked on Wei Si Xuan's door. He said to Wei Si Xuan, "I am a sick man, destitute and without a place to go. I have heard that Master Wei enjoys antiquity and the extraordinary, gathering all kinds of strange people and magicians. I come to pay my respects, and I wish Master Wei would accept me." Wei Si Xuan immediately invited the recluse to stay in his house.

    197

    其后居士身疾，痈尽溃血且甚。韦氏一家尽恶之。思玄尝诏术士数人会食，而居士不得预。既具膳，居士突至客前，溺于筵席上，尽湿，客怒皆起，韦氏家僮亦竞来骂之。居士遂告去，行至庭，忽亡所见。思玄与客甚异之。回视其溺，乃紫金液也，奇光璨然，真旷代之宝。思玄且惊且叹。有解者曰：“居士，紫金精也。徵其名氏，信矣。且‘辛’者，盖‘西方庚辛金’也。而‘锐’字，‘兑’従金，‘兑’亦西方之正位。推其义，则吾之解若合符然。

    Explanation

    197

    Later, the scholar fell ill, and his abscess burst, bleeding profusely. The entire We family despised him. Si Xuan once summoned several sages to a feast, but the scholar was not invited. After the meal was prepared, the scholar suddenly appeared before the guests and urinated on the banquet table, completely wetting it. The guests, angry, all stood up, and even the servants of the We family came to scold him. The scholar then left, walking to the courtyard, where he suddenly disappeared. Si Xuan and the guests were greatly puzzled. When he looked back at what he had urinated, it was purple gold liquid, with a brilliant, dazzling light, truly a treasure of the ages. Si Xuan was both surprised and sighed. Someone explained, saying, "The scholar is the essence of purple gold. Verifying his name and status, it is true. And 'Xin' is the Western 'Geng-Xin-Jin' (metal). The character '锐' contains '兑,' which follows metal, and '兑' is also the proper position of the West. Analyzing the meaning, then my explanation fits perfectly."

    198

    ○李员
    　　进士李员，河东人也，居长安延寿里。元和初夏，一夕，员独处其室，方偃于榻，寐未熟，忽闻室之西隅有微声，纤而远，锵然若韵金石乐，如是久不绝。俄而有歌者，其音极清越，泠泠然，又久不已。员窃志其歌词曰：斋
    　　“色分蓝叶青，声比磬中鸣
    　　七月初七日，吾当示汝形。”
    　　歌竟，其音阕。员且惊且异，朝日命家僮穷其迹，不能得焉。是夕，员方独处，又闻其声，凄越且久，亦歌如前。词竟，员心知为怪也，默然异之。如是凡数夕，亦闻焉。后至秋，始六日，夜有甚雨，隤其堂之北垣。明日，垣北又闻其声，员惊而视之，于北垣下得一缶，仅尺余，制用金成，形状奇古，与金之缶甚异。若翳其光，隐然有文，视不可见，盖千百年之器也。叩之，则其韵极长。即命涤去尘藓，方可读之，字皆小篆书，乃崔子玉座右铭也。员得而异之，然竟不知何代所制也

    Explanation

    198

    The Tale of Li Yuan

    Li Yuan, a scholar who had passed the imperial examinations, was from Hedong and lived in the Yan寿li district of Chang'an. In the early summer of the Yuanhe era, one night, Li Yuan was alone in his room, lying on his bed, not yet fully asleep. Suddenly, he heard a faint sound coming from the western corner of his room, which was both fine and distant, ringing like the music of metal and stone, lasting for a long time. Soon after, there was a singer whose voice was extremely clear and high-pitched, sounding crisp and lasting for a long time. Li Yuan secretly memorized the lyrics:

    "Divided into the blue-green leaves,
    The sound is like the chime of a stone.
    On the seventh day of the seventh month,
    I will show you my form."

    When the song ended, the sound ceased. Li Yuan was both surprised and puzzled. The next morning, he sent his servant to investigate the source of the sound, but they could not find anything. That night, Li Yuan was again alone, and he heard the same sound, which was both sorrowful and high-pitched, lasting for a long time. It was the same song as before. When the song ended, Li Yuan knew it must be a strange occurrence, and he remained silent, feeling very strange about it. This happened for several nights in a row, and he continued to hear the sound. Later, in the autumn, on the sixth day, there was a heavy rain at night, and the northern wall of his hall collapsed. The next day, he heard the sound again from the north side of the wall. Li Yuan was startled and looked, and he found a jar buried under the northern wall, about a foot long, made of gold, with an unusual and ancient shape, different from any golden jar he had seen. When he cleaned off the dirt and moss, he could see faint writing on it, though it was not visible at first glance. It was clearly a thousand-year-old artifact. When he tapped it, the sound was very long. He then cleaned it thoroughly and read the inscriptions, which were all in small seal script. They were the Zuo You Ming (Left and Right Admonitions) by Cui Ziyu. Li Yuan was amazed by this discovery, but he still did not know which dynasty it was from.

    199

    ○虞乡道士
    　　虞乡有山观，甚幽寂，有涤阳道士居焉。大和中，道士尝一夕独登坛望，见庭忽有异光，自井泉中发。俄有一物，状若兔，其色若精金，随光而出，环绕醮坛。久之，复入于井。自是每夕辄见。道士异其事，不敢告于人。后因淘井，得一金兔，甚小，奇光烂然，即置于巾箱中。时御史李戎职于蒲津，与道士友善，道士因以遣之。其后戎自奉先县令为忻州刺史，其金兔忽亡去，后月余而戎卒

    Explanation

    199

    In the area of虞鄉, there was a mountain temple where a Taoist priest named涤阳道士resided. During the period of大和中, the Taoist priest once climbed the altar alone one night to observe. He suddenly saw unusual light in the courtyard, which originated from a well. A creature resembling a rabbit, with a color resembling pure gold, emerged with the light and circled the altar. After some time, the creature returned to the well. From then on, the creature appeared every night. The Taoist priest did not dare to tell anyone about the phenomenon. Later, when the well was cleaned, a small golden rabbit with a brilliant light was discovered. The Taoist priest placed the golden rabbit in a small box. At that time, the official Li Rong held a position in the area of蒲津 and was friendly with the Taoist priest. The Taoist priest sent the golden rabbit to Li Rong. After that, Li Rong moved from the position of county magistrate in奉先 to the position of prefect in忻州. The golden rabbit suddenly disappeared. After a month, Li Rong died.

    200

    ○吕生
    　　大历中，有吕生者，自会稽上虞尉调集于京师，既而侨居永崇里。尝一夕，与其友数辈会食于其室。食毕，将就寝，俄有一妪，容服洁白，长二尺许，出室之北隅，缓步而来，其状极异。众视之，相目以笑。其妪渐迫其榻，且语曰：“君有会，不能不命耶何待吾之薄欤！”吕生叱之，遂退去，至北隅乃亡所见。且惊且异，莫知其来也
    　　明日，生独寤于室，又见其妪在北隅下，将前且退，惶然若有所惧。生又叱之，遂没。明日，生默念曰：“是必怪也。今夕将至，若不除之，必为吾患不朝夕矣。”即命一剑置其榻下。是夕，果自北隅徐步而来，颜色不惧，至榻前。生以剑挥之。其妪忽上榻，以臂揕生胸，余又跃于左右，举袂而舞。久之，又有一妪忽上榻，复以臂揕生。生遽觉一身尽凛然，若霜被于体。生又以剑乱挥，俄有数妪亦随而舞焉。

    Explanation

    200

    During the Tang dynasty, during the Dali period, there was a man named Lü Sheng, who was a magistrate in Shangyu, Zhejiang, and was summoned to serve in the capital. Afterward, he temporarily resided in Yongchong Lane. One night, he gathered with several friends for a meal in his room. After the meal, as they were about to go to bed, suddenly an old woman appeared, her appearance and attire pure and white, about two feet tall, emerging from the northern corner of the room, walking slowly toward them. Her appearance was extremely unusual. The others looked at her and laughed among themselves. The old woman gradually approached his bed and said, "You have a gathering, yet you do not summon me? Why do you treat me so poorly?" Lü Sheng rebuked her, and she retreated, disappearing at the northern corner. He was both surprised and puzzled, unsure of where she had come from.

    The next day, Lü Sheng alone woke up in his room and saw the old woman again at the northern corner, hesitating to come forward, looking anxious as if she was afraid of something. Lü Sheng rebuked her again, and she vanished. The next day, Lü Sheng quietly thought to himself, "This must be a supernatural phenomenon. Tonight, if it comes, if I do not get rid of it, it will surely become a problem for me soon." He then placed a sword under his bed. That night, the old woman indeed emerged slowly from the northern corner, her face without fear, approaching the bed. Lü Sheng swung the sword at her. The old woman suddenly climbed onto the bed, thrusting her arm into his chest, while others leaped around him, lifting their sleeves and dancing. After a while, another old woman suddenly climbed onto the bed, again thrusting her arm into him. Lü Sheng suddenly felt his entire body turn cold, as if covered in frost. He swung the sword wildly, and soon several old women followed, dancing along with him.

    201

    生挥剑不已，又为十余妪，各长寸许，虽愈多而貌如一焉，皆不可辨，环走四垣。生惧甚，计不能出。中者一妪谓书生曰：“吾将合为一矣，君且观之。”言已，遂相望而来，俱至榻前，翕然而合，又为一妪，与始见者不异。生惧益甚，乃谓曰：“尔何怪，而敢如是挠生人耶当疾去。不然，吾求方士，将以神术制汝。汝又安能为耶！”妪笑曰：“君言过矣。若有术士，吾愿见之。吾之来，戏君耳，非敢害也，幸君无惧。吾亦还其所矣。”言毕，遂退于北隅而没。明日，生以事语于人

    Explanation

    201

    The scholar kept swinging the sword, and he also turned into more than ten old women, each about an inch long. Although there were more, they all looked the same, and none could be distinguished, as they circled the four walls. The scholar was very afraid and could not think of a way out. One of the old women in the middle said to the scholar, "I will merge into one. Please watch." After finishing speaking, she retreated to the north corner and disappeared. The next day, the scholar told others about the incident.

    202

    有田氏子者，善以符术除去怪魅，名闻长安中，见说，喜跃曰：“是我事也。去之若爪一蚁耳。今夕愿往君舍且伺焉。”至夜，生与田氏子俱坐于室，未几，而妪果来至榻前。田氏子叱曰：“魅疾去。”妪扬然其色，不顾左右，徐步而来去者久之，谓田生曰：“非君之所知也。”其妪忽挥其手，手堕于地，又为一妪，甚小，跃而升榻，突入田生口中。田生惊曰：“吾死乎？”妪谓生曰：“吾比言不为君害，君不听。今田生之疾果何如哉然亦将成君之富耳。”言毕又去。明日，有谓吕生者：“宜于北隅发之，可见矣。”生喜而归，命家僮于其所没穷焉，果不至丈，得一瓶，可受斛许，贮水银甚多。生方悟其妪乃水银精也。田生竟以寒怵而卒。知

    Explanation

    202

    There was a man named Tiansi who was skilled in using talismans to dispel strange spirits. His fame spread throughout Chang'an. When he was told about the old woman's strange behavior, he joyfully exclaimed, "This is my task. Removing her is as easy as picking up an ant with a claw. Tonight, I wish to go to your house and wait for her." That night, the man and Tiansi sat together in the room. Soon after, the old woman indeed came to the foot of the bed. Tiansi shouted, "Spirit, depart quickly!" The old woman boldly ignored those around her and slowly walked in, staying for a long time. She then said to Tiansi, "This is beyond your understanding." Suddenly, the old woman waved her hand, and it fell to the ground. Then another small old woman appeared, leaping onto the bed, and suddenly entered Tiansi's mouth. Tiansi was shocked and exclaimed, "Am I going to die?" The old woman said, "I have already said I would not harm you, but you did not listen. What is the state of Tiansi's illness now? But it will make you wealthy." After speaking, she left. The next day, someone told the man, "It is appropriate to dig at the northwest corner; you will see it." The man was delighted and returned home, ordering his servant to dig deeply at the spot. It was not even a foot deep when they found a jar that could hold several shi (a unit of volume), containing a large amount of mercury. The man finally realized that the old woman was a spirit of mercury. Tiansi eventually died of cold and fright.

    203

    ○严生
    　　冯翊严生者，家于汉南。尝游岘山，得一物，其状若弹丸，色黑而大，有光，视之洁彻，若轻冰焉。生持以示于人，或曰：“珠也。”生因以“弹珠”名之，常置于箱中。其后生游长安，乃于春明门逢一胡人，叩焉而言：“衣橐中有奇宝，愿有得一见。”生即以“弹珠”示之。胡人捧之而喜曰：“此天下之奇货也，愿以三十万为价。”曰：“此宝安所用而君厚其价如是哉！”胡人曰：“我，西国人。此乃吾国之至宝，国人谓之‘清水珠’，若置于浊水，泠然洞彻矣。自亡此宝且三岁，吾国之井泉尽浊，国人俱病。故此越海逾山，来中夏以求之。今果得于子矣。”胡人即命注浊水于缶，以珠投之，俄而其水澹然清莹，纤毫可辩。生于是以珠与胡，获其价而去

    Explanation

    203

    ○ Yan Sheng
    Yan Sheng of Fengyi resided in Han Nan. Once, while traveling on Xian Mountain, he found an object, its form like a small projectile, black in color, large in size, and gleaming with light. When viewed, it was clean and transparent, as if made of light ice. Yan Sheng showed it to others, and someone said, “It is a pearl.” Yan Sheng then named it “dan zhu” (ball pearl) and often kept it in a box. Later, when he traveled to Chang’an, he met a Hu person at the Spring Ming Gate, who approached and said, “I have a strange treasure in my pouch; I wish to see it.” Yan Sheng immediately showed him the “dan zhu.” The Hu person took it with joy and said, “This is a rare treasure in the world; I am willing to pay thirty thousand for it.” Yan Sheng replied, “What use is this treasure to you that you value it so highly?” The Hu person said, “I am from the Western lands. This is the most precious treasure of my country, which the people call the ‘Clear Water Pearl.’ If placed in murky water, it will become cold and clear. Since I lost this treasure nearly three years ago, all the wells and springs in my country have turned murky, and the people have all fallen ill. Therefore, I have crossed over mountains and seas to come to the Central Kingdom to seek it. Now, I have indeed obtained it from you.” The Hu person then ordered murky water to be poured into a jar and cast the pearl into it. Soon, the water became calm and clear, bright and pure, with even the smallest details visible. Yan Sheng then gave the pearl to the Hu person, received the price, and departed.

    204

    ○玉清三宝
    　　杜陵韦弇，字景昭。开元中，举进士第，寓游于蜀。蜀多胜地，会春末，弇与其友数辈为花酒宴，虽夜不殆。一日，有请者曰：“郡南去十里有郑氏亭，亭起苑中，真尘外境也。愿偕去。”弇闻其说，喜甚，遂与俱

    Explanation

    204

    ○ The Three Treasures of the Jade Purity
    　　Wei Yan of Duling, courtesy name Jingzhao, passed the imperial examination for the jinshi degree during the Kaiyuan period of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang. He lived and traveled in Shu, a region known for its scenic beauty. As spring came to an end, Wei Yan and his friends held feasts and banquets, enjoying themselves well into the night. One day, someone invited them, saying, "Ten miles south of the city, there is a Zheng's Pavilion. It rises in a garden and is truly a place beyond the mundane world. I wish to go with you." Wei Yan, hearing this, was very happy, so he went with them.

    205

    南出十里，得郑氏亭。端空危危，横然四峙，门用花壁，砌用烟矗。弇望之，不暇他视，真所谓尘外境也。使者揖弇入。既入，见亭上有神仙十数，皆极色也，凝立若伫，半掉云袂，飘飘然。其侍列左右者亦十数。纹绣杳渺，殆不可识。有一人望弇而语曰：“韦进士来。”命左右请上亭。斜栏层去，既上且拜。群仙喜曰：“君不闻刘、阮事乎今日亦如是。愿奉一醉，将尽春色。君以为何如？”弇谢曰：“不意今日得为刘、阮，幸何甚哉！然则此为何所女郎又何为者愿一闻知。”群仙曰：“我，玉清之女也，居于此久矣。此乃玉清宫也。向闻君为下第进士，寓游至此，将以一言奉请，又惧君子不顾，且贻其辱，是以假郑氏之亭以命君，果副吾志。

    Explanation

    205

    Traveling south for ten miles, I arrived at Zheng's Pavilion. The pavilion stood tall and isolated, with a grand and imposing presence. Its doors were adorned with floral carvings, and its walls rose like smoke, creating an ethereal and mystical atmosphere. I gazed upon it, unable to look away, for it was truly a scene beyond the mundane world. A messenger bowed and invited me to enter. Once inside, I saw a dozen or so deities on the pavilion, all of them resplendent in their beauty. They stood still, as if in contemplation, their sleeves fluttering like clouds, creating a sense of ethereal movement. There were also a dozen or so attendants standing on either side of them. The intricate patterns and decorations were faint and almost indistinguishable, adding to the mysterious and otherworldly quality of the scene. One of the deities, looking at me, said, "Wei Scholar, you have arrived." They commanded their attendants to invite me to ascend the pavilion. I ascended through a series of steps and bowed upon reaching the top. The deities greeted me with joy, saying, "Have you not heard of the story of Liu and Ruan? Today, you are treated to the same experience. We wish to share a drink with you and enjoy the spring scenery. What do you think?" I responded with a polite thank you, saying, "I did not expect to be treated like Liu and Ruan today. How fortunate I am! But what are these female deities, and what is their purpose? I would like to know." The deities replied, "We are daughters of the Jade Clarity, and we have lived here for a long time. This is the Jade Clarity Palace. We had heard that you were a scholar who had failed the imperial examination and were traveling here. We wished to invite you, but we were afraid you might not come, which would be an embarrassment. Therefore, we used Zheng's Pavilion to invite you, and you have indeed fulfilled our wish."

    206

    虽然，此仙府也，虽云不可滞世间人，君居之，固无损耳。幸不以为疑。”即命酒乐宴亭中。丝竹尽举，飘然泠然，凌玄越冥，不为人间声曲。酒既酣，群仙曰：“吾闻唐天子尚神仙，吾有新乐一曲，曰《紫云》，愿授圣主。君，唐人也，为吾传之一进，可乎？”曰：“弇，一儒也。在长安中，徒为区区于尘土间，望天子门且不可见之，又非知音者，曷能致是？”群仙曰：“君既不能，吾将以梦传于天子可也。”又曰：“吾有三宝，将以赠君，能使君富敌王侯，君其受之。”乃命左右取其宝。始出一杯，其色碧，而光莹洞澈，顾谓弇曰：“碧瑶杯也。”又出一枕，似玉，微红，曰：“红蕤枕也。”又出一小函，其色紫，亦似玉，而莹澈则过之，曰：“紫玉函也。”已而皆授弇。弇拜谢别去

    Explanation

    206

    Although this is a fairy realm, and it is said that mortals cannot linger here, you can live here without any harm. Please do not doubt it. They immediately ordered a feast and music in the pavilion. All the string and wind instruments were played, floating and cool, transcending the mysterious and the profound, not of earthly tunes. When the wine had been drunk deeply, the immortals said, "I have heard that the Tang emperor reveres immortals. I have a new piece of music, called 'Purple Cloud,' and I wish to present it to the Holy Emperor. You, being a Tang person, can convey it to the emperor once, can you do this?" I replied, "I am just a scholar. In Chang'an, I am merely preoccupied with trivial matters, and I cannot even see the gates of the emperor's palace, nor is there anyone who understands music. How can I bring this about?" The immortals said, "Since you cannot, I will convey it to the emperor through a dream." They also said, "I have three treasures, which I will give to you. These can make you as wealthy as kings and dukes. Please accept them." He then ordered his attendants to bring the treasures. First, he brought out a cup, green in color, and brilliantly transparent, and looked at me, saying, "This is a green jade cup." Then he brought out a pillow, resembling jade, slightly red, and said, "This is a red phoenix pillow." Then he brought out a small box, purple in color, also resembling jade, but even more transparent, and said, "This is a purple jade box." Afterward, he gave all of them to me. I bowed in gratitude and left.

    207

    行未及一里，回望其亭，茫然无有。弇异之，亦竟不知何所也。遂挈其宝还长安。明年下第，东游至广陵。因以其宝集于广陵市。有胡人见而拜曰：“此天下之奇宝也。虽千万年，人无得者。君何得而有？”弇以告之，因问曰：“此何宝乎？”曰：“乃玉清真三宝也。”遂以数千万为直而易之。弇由是建甲第，居广陵中为豪士，竟卒于白衣也。知
    ○三宝村
    　　扶风县之西南，有三宝村。胡老相传云：“建村之时，有胡僧谓村人曰：‘此地有宝气，而今莫得之，其启发将自有时耳。’村人曰：‘是何宝也’曰：‘此交趾之宝，数有三焉。’故因以“三宝”名其村，盖识其事。知

    Explanation

    207

    Yan had not walked a mile when he looked back at the pavilion, but it was nowhere to be seen. Astonished by this, he still did not know where it was. So he took the treasure back to Chang'an. The following year, he failed the examination and traveled east to Guangling. He then gathered his treasure in the Guangling market. A foreigner saw it and bowed, saying, "This is a rare treasure in the world. Even after a thousand years, no one has obtained it. How did you obtain it?" Yan told him, and the foreigner asked, "What kind of treasure is this?" Yan replied, "It is the Three Treasures of the Jade Clear Truth." So he exchanged it for several million in value. Yan then built a grand mansion and lived in Guangling as a wealthy and influential person, but he ultimately died as a commoner.

    ○ Three Treasures Village

    In the southwest of Fufeng County, there is a village called Three Treasures Village. An old man from the Hu people said, "When the village was founded, a foreign monk told the villagers, 'This place has a treasure aura, but it cannot be obtained now. The time for its revelation will come.' The villagers asked, 'What kind of treasure is this?' The monk replied, 'It is the Three Treasures of Jiaozhi.' So they named the village 'Three Treasures' to commemorate the event."

    208

    开成元年春，村中民夜梦一丈夫者，黑簪帻，被广袂之衣，腰佩长剑，仪状峻古，谓民曰：“吾尝仕东汉，当光武时，与飞将马公同征交趾，尝得南人之宝。其后马公遭谤，以为多掠南货，尽载以归。光武怒，将命索其家。吾惧且及祸，故埋于此地。”言未讫而寤。民即以所梦具告于邻伍中。知
    　　是岁仲夏夕，云月阴晦，有牧竖望见西京原下炯然有光，若曳练焉，久而不灭。牧竖惊，告其父，即驰往视之，其光愈甚。至明夕，亦然。于是里人数辈，夜寻其光，俯而观之，其光在土而出，若焰薪火。里人乃相与植榫以表之。其明日，携锸具，穷表之下，深约丈余，得一金龟，长二寸许，制度奇妙，代所未识。又得宝剑一，长二尺有四寸；又得古镜一，径一尺余。皆尘迹蒙然。里人得之，遂持以诣县

    Explanation

    208

    In the spring of the first year of the Kai Cheng era, a villager dreamed of a man at night. The man wore a black headband and cap, a robe with wide sleeves, and carried a long sword at his waist. His appearance was dignified and ancient. He said to the villager, "I once served in the Eastern Han Dynasty during the reign of Emperor Guangwu, alongside the Flying General Ma Gong in a campaign against Jiaozhi. I once obtained treasures from the southern people. Later, Ma Gong was slandered for allegedly looting goods from the south and bringing them back. Emperor Guangwu became angry and ordered a search of Ma Gong's home. I feared that I would also be implicated in the scandal, so I buried the treasures here." He had not finished speaking when the villager woke up. The villager then told his neighbors about the dream.

    That summer, on a cloudy and dim evening, a young herdsman saw a bright light in the western fields, like a long piece of silk being dragged, and it remained for a long time without disappearing. The herdsman was surprised and told his father, who rushed to see it. The light was even brighter. The next evening, it was the same. The villagers then went out at night to follow the light, and they saw it emerging from the ground, like a flame of burning wood. The villagers planted a stake to mark the spot. The next day, they brought shovels and tools, dug down to the marked spot, and found a golden turtle about two cun long, with a design that was intricate and unusual, unlike anything seen before. They also found a treasure sword about 2.4 chi long and an ancient mirror with a diameter of more than one chi. All the items were covered in dust. The villagers took them to the county office.

    209

    时县令沛国刘随得之。发硎，其剑澹然若水波之色，虽利如切玉，无以加焉。其长二尺四寸者，盖古以八寸为尺，乃古三尺。其镜皆文迹繁会，有异兽环绕镜鼻，而年代绵邈，形理无缺。乃命磨莹，其清若上水之洁，真天下之奇宝也。县令刘君曰：“此为古之珍玩，宜归王府，可与天球、和璧，焜耀于上庠。”遂缄胶其事闻岐阳帅，愿表献天子。时陈君亦节度岐陇，得而爱之，因有其宝。由是人无知者。知
    ○玉龙膏
    　　安南有玉龙膏，南人用之，能化银液。说者曰：“此膏不可持北来，苟有犯者，则祸且及矣。”大和中，韩约都护安南，得其膏，及还，遂持以归。人有谓曰：“南人传此膏不可持以北，而公持去，得无有悔于后耶！”约不听，卒以归焉。后约为执金吾，是岁京师乱，约以附会郑注，竟赤其族，岂玉龙膏之所归祸乎由是南去者，不敢持以北也

    Explanation

    209

    During the time of the county magistrate of Pei State, Liu Sui, he obtained it. When drawn from the whetstone, the sword was as calm as water waves in color, though sharp enough to cut through jade, there was nothing sharper. Its length of 24 inches, according to the ancient standard of 8 inches per foot, equates to three ancient feet. The mirrors were intricately patterned with mythical creatures surrounding the handle, and they were of ancient origin, perfectly preserved. They were then polished, resulting in a clarity as pure as clear water, truly rare treasures of the world. The county magistrate Liu said, "These are ancient treasures suitable for the royal treasury, comparable to the Heavenly Ball and the Jade Bi, shining in the upper hall." They sealed the matter and reported it to the commander of Qiyang, wishing to present it to the emperor. At that time, Chen, who was also the military governor of Qilong, obtained and cherished these treasures, thus possessing them. From then on, no one knew about them. Known.

    The Jade Dragon Ointment
    In An Nan, there is the Jade Dragon Ointment, which the people of the south use to transform silver into liquid. Some say, "This ointment should not be brought north, for anyone who does so will bring disaster upon themselves." During the Great Harmony period, Han Yue, the protector of An Nan, obtained the ointment and took it back with him. Someone told him, "The people of the south say this ointment should not be brought north, and you are taking it away. Will you not regret it later?" Han Yue did not listen and eventually took it back. Later, Han Yue became the chief of the Gold Bearer, and that year, the capital was in turmoil. Han Yue, associating with Zheng Zhu, ultimately had his entire clan executed. Is it because of the Jade Dragon Ointment that brought the disaster? Therefore, those going south dare not bring it north.

    210

    ○地下肉芝
    　　兰陵萧逸人，亡其名。尝举进士，下第，遂焚其书，隐居潭水上，従道士学神仙。因绝粒吸气，每旦屈伸支体，冀延其寿。积十年余，发尽白，色枯而背偻，齿有堕者。一旦，引镜自视，勃然发怒，且曰：“吾弃声利，隐身田野间，绝粒吸气，冀得长生。今亦衰瘠如是，岂我之心哉？”即还居邺下，学商人逐什一之利。凡数年，资用大饶，为富家。后因治园屋发地，得物状类人手，肥而且润，色微红。逸人得之，惊曰：“岂非祸之芽且吾闻太岁所在，不可兴土事，脱有犯者，当有修肉出其下，固不祥也。

    Explanation

    210

    A Fleshy Fungus Underground

    A man from Lanling, known as Xaio Yi Ren, whose name has been lost to history. Once he took the imperial examination but failed, so he burned his books and retired to live by a lake, following a Taoist to learn the ways of immortality. He abstained from food and breathed deeply, bending and stretching his limbs every morning, hoping to extend his life. After more than ten years, his hair turned completely white, his complexion became dry, and his back hunched; some of his teeth had fallen out. One day, looking at himself in the mirror, he suddenly became furious and said, "I abandoned fame and profit, hid myself in the fields, abstained from food and breathed deeply, hoping to achieve immortality. Now I am as thin and weak as this, is this what I intended?" He then returned to live in Ye, learning to trade for a profit of one-tenth. Over several years, he accumulated great wealth and became a wealthy family. Later, while repairing his garden and digging the ground, he found something resembling a human hand, fleshy and moist, slightly red in color. Xaio Yi Ren was startled and exclaimed, "Isn't this a sign of impending misfortune? I have heard that where the Tai Sui resides, earthworks cannot be undertaken. If someone were to violate this, there would be a fleshy object emerging from below, certainly an omen of bad luck."

    211

    今果有，奈何然吾闻得肉食之，或可以免。”于是烹而食，味甚美，食且尽。自是逸人听视明，力愈壮，貌愈少，发之秃者尽黰然而长矣，齿之堕者亦骈然而生矣。逸人默自奇异，不敢告于人。后有道士至邺下，逢逸人，惊曰：“先生尝得饵仙药乎何神气清晤如是。”道士因轸其脉。久之，又曰：“先生尝食灵芝矣。夫灵芝状类人手，肥而且润，色微红者是也。”逸人悟其事以告，道士贺曰：“先生之寿，可与龟鹤齐矣。然不宜居尘俗间，当退休山林，弃人事，神仙可致。”逸人喜而従其语，遂去，竟不知所在。知
    ○卢虔
    　　东洛有故宅，其堂奥轩级甚宏特，然居者多暴死，是以空而键之且久。故右散骑常侍万阳卢虔，贞元中，为御史分察东台，常欲贸其宅而止焉。或曰：“此宅有怪，不可居。”虔曰：“吾自能弭之。”

    Explanation

    211

    Now that it exists, what should be done? I heard that if one gets to eat it, perhaps one can avoid [some misfortune]. So they cooked and ate it, and the taste was extremely delicious, almost finished. From then on, the hermit's hearing and vision became clear, his strength grew stronger, his appearance younger, his bald hair turned black and grew again, and his fallen teeth grew back in pairs. The hermit silently wondered at these changes and dared not tell others. Later, a Taoist came to Ye and met the hermit, amazed and said, "Have you ever taken immortal elixir, Master? Your spirit and appearance are so clear and bright." The Taoist then examined the hermit's pulse. After a while, he said, "You must have eaten lingzhi mushrooms. Lingzhi mushrooms resemble human hands, are fat and moist, and have a slight red color." The hermit realized the truth and told the Taoist, who congratulated him, saying, "Your lifespan can match that of turtles and cranes. However, you should not live in the mundane world; you should retire to the mountains, abandon worldly affairs, and achieve immortality." The hermit was delighted and followed the advice, leaving and disappearing without a trace.

    In Donglu, there was an old house with a grand hall and high steps. However, many people who lived there died suddenly, so it had been vacant and locked for a long time. Right Censor Lu Qian, during the Tang Dynasty, was responsible for inspecting the eastern region and often wanted to buy the house. Someone warned him, "This house is haunted and not suitable for living in." Lu Qian confidently said, "I can dispel the supernatural phenomena."

    212

    后一夕，虔与従吏同寝其堂，命仆使尽止于门外。従吏勇悍善射，于是执弓矢坐前轩下。夜将深，闻有叩门者，従吏即问之，应声曰：“柳将军遣奉书于卢侍御。”虔不应。已而投一幅书轩下，字似濡笔而书者，点画纤然。虔命従吏视，其字云：“吾家于此有年矣。堂奥轩级，皆吾之居也；门神户灵，皆吾之隶也。而君突入吾舍，岂其理耶！假令君有舍，吾入之可乎既不惧吾，宁不愧于心耶君速去，匆招败亡之辱。”读既毕，其书飘然四散，若飞烬之状。俄又闻有言者：“柳将军愿见卢御史。”已而有大厉至，身长数十寻，立庭，手执一瓢。其従吏即引满而发，中所执。其厉遂退，委其瓢。久之又来，俯轩而立，挽其首且窥焉，貌甚异。従吏又射之，中其胸。厉惊，若有惧，遂东向而去

    Explanation

    212
