    One night later, Ruan and his followers slept together in the hall, ordering all servants to stay outside the door. The followers were brave, fierce, and skilled in archery, so they held bows and arrows and sat under the front veranda. As the night deepened, they heard someone knocking on the door. The followers immediately asked who it was, and the response came: "General Liu has sent a letter to Magistrate Lu." Ruan did not respond. Soon after, a sheet of paper was thrown under the veranda, and the writing appeared as if it had been written with a wet brush, delicate and fine. Ruan ordered the followers to look at it, and the writing said: "My family has lived here for many years. The inner rooms and verandas are all my dwelling; the door gods and household spirits are all my servants. And you have suddenly entered my home—how is that right? If you have a place, can I enter yours? Since you are not afraid of me, are you not ashamed in your heart? Leave quickly, and do not bring upon yourself the shame of defeat." After reading the message, the paper scattered in the air, like flying ashes. Soon after, they heard someone speaking again: "General Liu wishes to see Magistrate Lu." Then, a ghost appeared, towering dozens of fathoms tall, standing in the courtyard, holding a ladle. Ruan's follower immediately drew the bow and shot, hitting what the ghost was holding. The ghost retreated, dropping the ladle. After a long time, it came back, bending over the veranda and standing, pulling its head and peering at it, looking very strange. The follower shot again, hitting its chest. The ghost was startled, seeming to be afraid, and then turned east and left.

    213

    至明，虔命穷其迹，至宅东隙地，见柳高百余尺，有一矢贯其上，所谓柳将军也。虔伐其薪。自此其宅居者无恙。后岁余，因重构堂室，于屋瓦下得一瓢，长约丈余，有矢贯其柄，即将军所执之瓢也。知
    ○江夏従事
    　　大和中，有従事江夏者，其官舍尝有怪异。每夕见一巨人，身尽黑，甚光，见之即悸而病死。后有许元长者，善视鬼。従事命元长以符术考召。后一夕，元长坐于堂西轩下，巨人忽至，元长出一符飞之，中其臂，剨然有声，遂堕于地。巨人即去。元长视其堕臂，乃一枯木枝。至明日，有家童谓元长曰：“堂之东隅有枯树焉。先生符今在其上。”即往视之。其树有枝稍折者，果巨人所断臂也。即伐而焚之。宅遂无怪

    Explanation

    213

    Until dawn, the official ordered the traces to be investigated. They reached the eastern empty space of the house, where they saw a willow tree over a hundred feet tall, with a single arrow piercing through it. This is what is known as the Willow General. The official cut down the wood of the tree. From then on, the occupants of the house were unharmed. After more than a year, the house was rebuilt, and a ladle about ten feet long was found under the roof tiles, with an arrow piercing through its handle. This was the ladle held by the general.

    During the Dahe period, there was an official named Jiangxia. His official residence once had strange occurrences. Every night, he saw a giant. The entire body of the giant was black and very bright. Seeing it caused fear and led to illness and death. Later, there was a man named Xu Yuanzhang, skilled at seeing ghosts. The official ordered Xu Yuanzhang to use talismanic arts to summon and investigate. One night, Xu Yuanzhang sat under the western veranda of the hall, and the giant suddenly appeared. Xu Yuanzhang threw a talisman at it, which hit the giant's arm, making a loud sound, and it fell to the ground. The giant then left. Xu Yuanzhang examined the fallen arm and found it to be a dried branch of wood. By the next day, a servant boy told Xu Yuanzhang, "There is a dead tree in the eastern corner of the hall. Your talisman is now on it." He went to look at it. The tree had a slightly broken branch, indeed the arm cut off by the giant. He then cut it down and burned it. The house was then free of strange occurrences.

    214

    ○窦宽
    　　唐扶风窦宽者，家于梁山。大和八年秋，自大理评事解县推盐使判官，罢职退归。因治园屋，命家仆伐一树。既伐，而有血滂溜，汪然注地，食顷而尽。宽异之，且知为怪。由是闭门绝人事。至明年冬十一月，郑注、李训反，宽与注连，遂诛死于左禁军中。知
    ○吴偃
    　　有厉泉县民吴偃，家于田野间。有一女十岁余，一夕，忽遁去，莫知所往。后数余日，偃梦其父谓偃曰：“汝女今在东北隅，盖木神为崇。”偃惊而寤。至明日，即于东北隅穷其迹，果闻有呼吟之事，偃视之，见其女有一穴内。口甚小，然其中稍宽敞。傍有古槐木，盘根极大。于是挈之而归，然兀若沉醉者。会有李道士至，偃请符术呵禁。其女忽瞬而语曰：“地东北有槐木，木有神，引某自树腹空入地下穴内，故某病。”于是伐其树。后数日，女病始愈

    Explanation

    214

    ○窦宽
    　　唐扶风窦宽者，家于梁山。大和八年秋，自大理评事解县推盐使判官，罢职退归。因治园屋，命家仆伐一树。既伐，而有血滂溜，汪然注地，食顷而尽。宽异之，且知为怪。由是闭门绝人事。至明年冬十一月，郑注、李训反，宽与注连，遂诛死于左禁军中。

    ○Duan Kuan
    　　Duan Kuan, a man from Fufeng, lived in Liangshan. In the autumn of the eighth year of the Dahe era (834 AD), he retired from his position as an administrative judge for the salt distribution office in Jie County, which he had held under the title of "Dali Pingshi." After leaving his post, he returned home and began renovating his garden and house. He ordered his servant to cut down a tree. After the tree was felled, blood flowed from it, pooling on the ground and disappearing within a short time. Duan Kuan found this strange and realized it was a supernatural occurrence. He then closed his doors and cut off contact with others. In the eleventh month of the following year (December 835), Zheng Zhu and Li Xun staged a coup, and Duan Kuan was implicated in the conspiracy and was executed in the Left Forbidden Army.

    ○吴偃
    　　有厉泉县民吴偃，家于田野间。有一女十岁余，一夕，忽遁去，莫知所往。后数余日，偃梦其父谓偃曰：“汝女今在东北隅，盖木神为崇。”偃惊而寤。至明日，即于东北隅穷其迹，果闻有呼吟之事，偃视之，见其女有一穴内。口甚小，然其中稍宽敞。傍有古槐木，盘根极大。于是挈之而归，然兀若沉醉者。会有李道士至，偃请符术呵禁。其女忽瞬而语曰：“地东北有槐木，木有神，引某自树腹空入地下穴内，故某病。”于是伐其树。后数日，女病始愈。

    ○Wu Yan
    　　Wu Yan, a man from Liquan County, lived in the countryside. He had a daughter who was over ten years old. One night, she suddenly disappeared, and no one knew where she had gone. Several days later, Wu Yan dreamed that his father told him, "Your daughter is now in the northeast corner; it seems that a tree spirit has caused this." Wu Yan woke up in shock. The next day, he went to the northeast corner to investigate the traces and indeed heard sounds of calling and moaning. He saw his daughter inside a hole. The entrance was very small, but inside it was somewhat spacious. There was an ancient locust tree nearby with very large roots. He then carried her back home, but she seemed dazed as if drunk. Later, a Taoist named Li arrived, and Wu Yan asked him for a talisman and exorcism. His daughter suddenly opened her eyes and spoke, "There is a locust tree in the northeast; the tree has a spirit, it led me into the hollow inside the tree to an underground hole, and that's why I am sick." He then cut down the tree. After several days, his daughter's illness began to improve.

    215

    ○董观（二）
    　　有董观者，尝为僧，居于太原佛寺。太和七年夏，与其表弟王生南游荆楚。后将入长安，道至商於。一夕，舍山馆中。王生既寐，观独未寝。忽见一物出烛下，既而掩其烛，状类人手，而无指。细视，烛影外若有物。观急呼王生，生起，其手遂去。观谓王曰：“慎无寝。魅当再来。”因持挺而坐伺之。良久，王生曰：“魅安在兄妄矣。”既就寝。顷之，有一物长五尺余，蔽烛而立，无手及面目。观益恐，又呼王生。生怒，不起。观因以挺椹其首，其躯若草所穿，挺亦随入其中，而力取不可得。俄乃退去。观虑又来，迨晓不敢寝。明日，访馆吏，吏曰：“此西数里有古杉，常为魅，疑即所见也。”即与观及王生径寻，果见古杉，有挺贯其柯叶间。吏曰：“人言此为妖且久，未尝见其真，今则信矣。”急取斧，尽伐去之

    Explanation

    215

    A Tale of Dong Guan (Part Two)

    There was a man named Dong Guan who had once been a monk and lived in a Buddhist temple in Taiyuan. In the summer of the seventh year of the Taihe era, he traveled south with his cousin Wang Sheng to Jingchu. Later, they planned to enter Chang'an, and on their way, they passed through Shangyu. One night, they stayed at a mountain inn. Wang Sheng had already fallen asleep, but Dong Guan remained awake. Suddenly, he saw something emerge from under the candlelight, then cover the candle. It resembled a human hand but lacked fingers. On closer inspection, there seemed to be something outside the candlelight. Dong Guan quickly called out to Wang Sheng, who got up, and the hand then disappeared. Dong Guan warned Wang, saying, "Be careful not to sleep. The demon will come again." He held a spear and sat waiting for it. After a long time, Wang Sheng said, "Where is the demon? Brother, you are mistaken." They then went to bed. Soon after, a creature more than five feet long stood blocking the candlelight, without hands or a face. Dong Guan became even more afraid and called out to Wang Sheng again. Wang Sheng was angry and did not get up. Dong Guan struck it with the spear, and its body seemed to be pierced by grass, with the spear following into it, but it could not be taken out with force. Soon, it withdrew. Dong Guan worried it would come again and, until dawn, dared not sleep. The next day, he visited the innkeeper, who said, "A few miles west there is an ancient pine tree that often becomes a demon. I suspect it was what you saw." Immediately, Dong Guan and Wang Sheng went to search, and indeed found the ancient pine tree, with a spear piercing through its branches and leaves. The innkeeper said, "People say this has been an evil spirit for a long time, but never seen its true form. Now, we believe it." They quickly took an axe and cut it down completely.

    216

    ○邓珪
    　　晋阳西有童子寺，在郊牧之外。贞元中，有邓珪者，寓居于寺。是岁秋，与朋友数辈会宿。既阖扉后，忽见一手自牖间入，其手色黄而瘦甚。众视之，惧怵然，独珪无所惧。反开其牖，闻有吟啸之声，珪不之怪。讯之曰：“汝为谁？”对曰：“吾隐居山谷有年矣。今夕纵风月之游，闻先生在此，故来奉谒。诚不当列先生之席，愿得坐牖下，听先生与客谈，足矣。”珪许之。既坐，与诸客谈笑极欢。久之告去，将行，谓珪曰：“明夕当再来，愿先生未见摈。”既去，珪与诸客议曰：“此必鬼也。不穷其迹，且将为患矣。”于是缉丝为缗数百寻，候其再来，必缚之。明夕果来，又以手出于牖间。珪即以缗系其臂，牢不可解。闻牖外问：“何罪而见缚，其议安在得无悔邪“遂引缗而去。至明日，珪与诸客俱穷其迹，至寺北百余步，有蒲桃一株，甚蕃茂，而缗系其枝。有叶类人手，果牖间所见者。遂命掘其根而焚之

    Explanation

    216

    In the west of JinYang, there was a temple called Tongzi Temple, located beyond the outskirts of the pasture. During the reign of Emperor Dezong in the Tang Dynasty, there was a man named Deng Gui who temporarily resided in the temple. One autumn, he gathered with several friends for an overnight stay. After closing the door, they suddenly saw a hand enter through the window. The hand was yellow and very thin. The others looked at it with fear, but Deng Gui was not afraid. He opened the window and heard a sound of singing or whistling. Deng Gui did not find it strange. He asked, "Who are you?" The hand replied, "I have been living in seclusion in the mountains for many years. Tonight I am indulging in the pleasures of the wind and moon. I heard that the master is here, so I came to pay respects. I truly do not deserve a seat at the master's table, but I would be content to sit under the window and listen to the master and guests talk." Deng Gui agreed. They sat down, and they talked and laughed happily. After a while, the hand said goodbye and was about to leave, saying to Deng Gui, "I will come again tomorrow night. I hope the master does not reject me." After it left, Deng Gui and his friends discussed, "This must be a ghost. If we do not trace its tracks, it will surely cause trouble." So they wove silk into hundreds of fathoms of rope and waited for its return, intending to bind it. The next night, the hand appeared again, entering through the window. Deng Gui immediately bound its arm with the rope, which was firmly and could not be untied. The hand asked from outside the window, "What crime has led to my being bound? Where is the discussion? Could there be regret?" Then it pulled the rope and left. The next day, Deng Gui and his friends traced its tracks to a grapevine about a hundred steps north of the temple. The grapevine was very lush and abundant, and the rope was tied to its branch. There were leaves that resembled human hands, indeed what was seen in the window. They then dug up its roots and burned them.

    217

    ○刘皂
    　　灵石县南尝夜中妖怪，由是里中人无敢夜经其地者。元和年，董叔经为河西守，时有彭城刘皂，假孝义尉。皂顷尝以书忏董叔，怒甚，遂弃职。入汾水关，夜至灵石南，逢一人立于路旁，其状绝异。皂马惊而坠，久之乃起。其路旁立者即解皂衣袍而自衣之。皂以为劫，不敢拒。既而西走，近十余里，至逆旅，因言其事。逆旅人曰：“邑南夜中有妖怪，固非贼尔。”明日，有自县南来者，谓皂曰：“县南野中有蓬蔓，状类人，披一青袍，不亦异乎？”皂往视之，果己之袍也。里中人始悟为妖者乃蓬蔓耳。由是尽焚，其妖遂绝

    Explanation

    217

    Duan Zhao

    In the south of Lingshi County, there were often supernatural phenomena at night, so no one in the village dared to pass through that area after dark. During the reign of Emperor Xianzong (Yuanhe era), Dong Shujing was the governor of Hexi. At that time, Liu Zhao from Pengcheng held a temporary position as a magistrate in Xiaoyi. Liu Zhao once wrote a letter to Dong Shujing, which greatly angered him, leading Liu Zhao to abandon his post. He entered the Fen River Pass and arrived at the south of Lingshi County at night. There he encountered a person standing by the roadside, who had an extremely unusual appearance. Liu Zhao's horse was startled and he fell off, taking a long time to get up. The person by the roadside then took off Liu Zhao's clothes and put them on himself. Liu Zhao thought he was being robbed and did not dare to resist. He then walked westward, covering about ten miles, and arrived at an inn, where he told the story. The innkeeper said, "There are supernatural phenomena in the south of the town at night, but they are not robbers." The next day, someone who came from the south of the county said to Liu Zhao, "There are weeds in the fields south of the county, which look like a person, wearing a green robe, isn't that strange?" Liu Zhao went to see it and found that it was indeed his own robe. The villagers finally realized that the supernatural phenomena were caused by the weeds. As a result, all the weeds were burned, and the supernatural phenomena ceased.

    218

    ○梁生
    　　唐兴平之西有梁生别墅，其后园有梨树十余株。大和四年冬十月初雪霁，其梨忽有花发，芳而且茂。梁生甚奇之，以为吉兆。有韦氏谓梁生曰：“夫木发春而荣，冬而瘁，固其常矣。今反是，焉可谓之吉兆乎？”生闻之不悦。后月余，梁生父卒
    ○赵生
    　　天宝中，有赵生者，其先以文学显。生兄弟数人，俱以进士、明经入仕。独生性鲁钝，虽读书，然不能分句详义，由是年壮尚不得为郡贡。常与兄弟友生会宴，盈座朱绿相接，独生白衣，甚为不乐。及酒酣，或靳之，生益惭且怒。后一日，弃其家遁去，隐晋阳山，葺茅为舍。生有书百余编，笈而至山中，昼习夜息，虽寒热切肌，食粟袭纻，不惮劳苦。而生蒙懵，力愈勤而功愈少，生愈恚怒，终不易其志。

    Explanation

    218

    ○梁生
    In the west of Xingping during the Tang Dynasty, there was a villa belonging to Liáng Shēng, and in his garden, there were more than ten pear trees. In the tenth month of winter in the fourth year of Dàhé, after the snow had melted, the pears suddenly began to bloom, fragrant and luxuriant. Liáng Shēng found it very strange and considered it a good omen. Wéi Shì said to Liáng Shēng, "Trees bloom in spring and flourish, but wither in winter, which is their natural cycle. Now it is the opposite; how can this be considered a good omen?" Liáng Shēng was not pleased when he heard this. After a month, Liáng Shēng's father died.

    ○赵生
    During the Tianbao era, there was a man named Zhao Shēng, whose ancestors were renowned for their literary achievements. Zhao Shēng had several brothers, all of whom entered officialdom through the imperial examinations. However, Zhao Shēng was simple-minded and slow-witted. Although he read, he could not understand the meaning of individual sentences. As a result, even when he was grown, he had not yet been recommended as a candidate for the provincial examination. He often attended banquets with his brothers and friends, where the room was filled with red and green attire, but only Zhao Shēng wore plain white clothing, and he was very unhappy. When the wine flowed, someone mocked him, and Zhao Shēng became even more ashamed and angry. The next day, he abandoned his home and fled, hiding in Jinyang Mountain and building a thatched cottage. Zhao Shēng had over a hundred volumes of books, which he carried to the mountain, where he studied during the day and rested at night. Despite the cold and heat, he ate millet and wore coarse cloth, and he did not fear hard work. But Zhao Shēng remained dim-witted, and the harder he worked, the less he achieved. He became increasingly angry, but he never changed his resolve.

    219

    后旬余，有翁衣褐来造之，因谓生曰：“吾子居深山中，读古人书，岂有志于禄仕乎虽然，学愈久而卒不能分句详议，何蔽滞之甚邪？”生谢曰：“仆不敏，自度老且无用，故入深山，读书自悦。虽不能达其精微，然必欲死于志业，不辱先人。又何及于禄仕也。”翁曰：“吾子之志甚坚。老夫虽无术能，有补于郎君，但幸一谒我耳。”因徵其所止，翁曰：“吾段氏子，家于山西大木之下。”言讫，忽亡所见。生怪之，以为妖，遂径往山西寻其迹。果有椴树蕃茂，生曰：“岂非段氏子乎？”因持锸发其下，得人参长尺余，甚肖所遇翁之貌。生曰：“吾闻人参能为怪者，可愈疾。”遂瀹而食之。自是醒然明悟，目所览书，尽能穷奥。后岁余，以明经及第。历官数任而卒

    Explanation

    219

    After more than ten days, an old man dressed in coarse clothes came to visit him. He then said to the man, "My son, living in the deep mountains and reading ancient books, do you have aspirations for official positions? Although, the longer you study, the more you cannot even parse sentences or discuss them in detail, how severely are you obstructed?" The man replied, "I am not intelligent; I consider myself old and useless, so I entered the deep mountains to read books for my own pleasure. Although I cannot grasp their subtleties, I still wish to die for my aspirations and not disgrace my ancestors. How could I even think about official positions?" The old man said, "Your aspirations are very firm. Although I have no skills, I can be of some help to you, just visit me once." He then asked where the man lived. The old man said, "I am a descendant of the Duan family, living under a large tree in Shanxi." After he finished speaking, he suddenly disappeared. The man was surprised and thought it was a ghost, so he went straight to Shanxi to find his traces. Indeed, there was a large, flourishing paulownia tree. The man said, "Is this not the descendant of the Duan family?" He then took a spade and dug under it, finding a ginseng root over a foot long, which greatly resembled the old man he had met. The man said, "I have heard that ginseng can transform into strange things and can cure diseases." He then boiled it and ate it. From then on, he was suddenly enlightened, and he could understand all the books he read. After a year or so, he passed the imperial examination in the classics. He held several official positions before he died.

    220

    ○兴庆池龙
    　　唐玄宗尝潜龙于兴庆宫，及即位，其兴庆池尝有一小龙出游宫外御沟水中，奇状蜿蜒，负腾逸之状。宫嫔内竖，靡不具瞻。后玄宗幸蜀，銮舆将发，前一夕，其龙自池中御素云，跃然亘空，望西南而去。环列之士，率共观之。及上行至嘉陵江，乘舟将渡，见小龙翼舟而进。侍臣咸睹之。上泫然泣下，顾谓左右曰：“此吾兴庆池中龙也。”命以酒沃酹，上亲自祝之。龙乃自水中振鬣而去。知

    Explanation

    220

    The Dragon of Xingqing Pool

    Emperor Xuanzong of Tang once lived in the Xingqing Palace, where he was known as the "hidden dragon." After ascending the throne, a small dragon appeared in the Xingqing Pool, wandering outside the palace moat. It had an extraordinary and winding form, displaying a posture of leaping and escaping. The palace ladies and eunuchs all watched in awe. Later, when Emperor Xuanzong traveled to Shu, his imperial carriage was about to depart. On the eve of his journey, the dragon emerged from the pool, riding on a white cloud, leaping into the sky and heading southwest. The court officials gathered around to witness this miraculous event. When the emperor reached the Jialing River and was about to cross on a boat, he saw the small dragon flying alongside the boat. All the court officials saw it. The emperor shed tears involuntarily, turning to his attendants and saying, "This is the dragon from my Xingqing Pool!" He ordered wine to be poured as an offering and personally prayed to it. The dragon then shook its mane and flew away from the water.

    221

    ○萧昕
    　　唐故兵部尚书萧昕常为京兆尹。时京师大旱，炎郁之气，蒸为疾疬。代宗命宰臣下有司褥祀山川，凡有月余，暑气愈盛。时天竺僧不空三藏居于静住寺，三藏善以持念召龙兴云雨。昕于是诣寺，谓三藏曰：“今兹骄阳累月矣，圣上悬忧，撤乐贬食，岁凶是念，民瘵为忧。幸吾师为结坛场致雨也。”三藏曰：“易与耳。然召龙以兴云雨，吾恐风雷之震，有害于生植，又何补于稼穑耶！”昕曰：“迅雷甚雨，诚不能滋百谷，适足以清暑热，而少解黔首之病也。愿无辞焉。”三藏不获已，乃命其徒，取华木皮仅尺余，缵小龙于其上，而以炉瓯香水置于前，三藏转咒，震舌呼祝。咒者食顷，即发缵龙授昕曰：“可投此于曲江中。投讫亟还，无冒风雨。”昕如言投之。旋有白龙才尺余，摇鬣振鳞自水出，俄而身长数丈，状如曳素，倏忽亘天。昕鞭马疾驱，未及数十步，云物凝晦，暴雨骤降。比至永崇里，道中之水，已若决渠矣。知

    Explanation

    221

    The Former Minister of War Xiao Xin, who served as the Governor of Jingzhao, was known for his administrative skills. During a severe drought in the capital, the oppressive heat and sickness were widespread. Emperor Daizong ordered the high officials to perform rituals to the mountains and rivers, but after over a month, the heat only intensified. At the time, the Indian monk Fokong Sanzang, a renowned practitioner of Buddhist rituals, resided at Jingzhu Temple. Known for his ability to summon rain through spiritual practices, Fokong was approached by Xiao Xin, who said, "For months now, the sun has been scorching, and the emperor is deeply concerned. He has stopped music and reduced food consumption, worrying about the poor harvest and the suffering of the people. I hope, Master, that you will perform the ritual to bring rain."

    Fokong replied, "It's easy. However, summoning rain through dragons may cause harm to crops due to the thunder and lightning, and it may not benefit the fields." Xiao Xin argued, "Even if the rain cannot nourish the crops, it will at least cool the heat and alleviate the people's suffering. I beg you not to refuse." Reluctantly, Fokong instructed his disciples to prepare a small piece of fragrant wood and a silk dragon, about a foot long, and placed an incense burner with incense water in front. He then recited spells and made loud invocations. After a short while, he handed the silk dragon to Xiao Xin and said, "Throw this into Qujiang. Do it quickly and return without being caught in the storm." Xiao Xin followed the instructions and threw the dragon into the river. Soon, a small white dragon, about a foot long, emerged from the water, shaking its mane and scales. It quickly grew to several zhang in length, appearing like a white ribbon stretching across the sky. Xiao Xin urged his horse and rode away, but before he covered even a few dozen steps, dark clouds gathered and a heavy rain began to fall. By the time he reached Yongchongli, the rain had turned into a flood, as if a dam had broken.

    222

    ○任顼
    　　唐建中初，有乐安任顼者，好读书，不喜尘俗事，居深山中，有终焉之志。尝一日，闭关昼坐，有一翁叩门来谒，衣黄衣，貌甚秀，曳杖而至。顼延坐与语。既久，顼讶其言讷而色沮，甚有不乐事，因问翁曰：“何为而色沮乎，岂非有忧耶不然，是家有疾而翁念之深耶！”老人曰：“果如是。吾忧俟子一问固久矣。且我非人，乃龙也。西去一里有大湫，吾家之数百岁，今为一人所苦，祸且将及，非子不能脱我死，辄来奉诉。子今幸问我，故得而言也。

    Explanation

    222

    ○ Ren Xu
    In the early years of the Jianzhong era of the Tang dynasty, there was a man from Le'an named Ren Xu, who loved reading and disliked worldly matters. He lived in a deep mountain, with the intention of spending his final days there. One day, he shut himself in and sat in meditation during the day, when an old man knocked on the door to pay a visit. The old man wore a yellow robe, had an exceptionally refined appearance, and came dragging a staff. Ren Xu invited him to sit and spoke with him. After a while, Ren Xu was surprised by the old man's slow speech and dejected expression, which suggested he had a very unpleasant matter on his mind. He therefore asked the old man, "Why is your expression so dejected? Is it not because you are troubled? Or is it perhaps because your family is ill and you are deeply concerned about them?" The old man replied, "Indeed, as you said. My sorrow has been waiting for you to ask about it for a long time. Moreover, I am not a human, but a dragon. One li west of here is a great pool. My family has existed for hundreds of years, but now we are suffering because of one person, and disaster is about to come. Only you can save me from death, so I have come to report this to you. You are fortunate to have asked me, so I can now speak."

    223

    ”顼曰：“某尘中人耳，独知有诗书礼乐，他术则某不能晓，然何以脱翁之祸乎？”老人曰：“但授我语，非藉他术，独劳数十言而已。”顼曰：“愿受教。”翁曰：“后二日，愿子为我晨至湫上，当亭午之际，有一道士自西来者，此所谓祸我者也。道士当竭我湫中水，且屠我。子伺其湫水竭，宜厉声呼曰：‘天有命，杀黄龙者死。’言毕，湫当满。道士必又为术，子因又呼之。如是者三，我得完其生矣。必重报，幸无他为虑。”顼诺之。已而祈谢甚恳，久之方去

    Explanation

    223

    Xu replied, "I am just a commoner, knowing only about poetry, books, rites, and music; other arts I cannot understand. But how can I save the old man from his misfortune?" The old man said, "Just teach me the words; not relying on other arts, only requiring dozens of words." Xu said, "I wish to learn." The old man said, "Two days later, I wish you to come to the spring in the morning. At noon, there will be a Taoist priest coming from the west, this is the one who will harm me. The Taoist priest will drain the water from my spring and will kill me. You wait until the spring water is drained, then loudly call out: 'Heaven has a decree, those who kill the yellow dragon will die.' After saying this, the spring will fill up. The Taoist priest will surely use another art, you should call out again. Three times in this manner, I will be able to save my life. I will surely reward you, so please don't worry about anything else." Xu agreed. Later, he prayed and thanked very earnestly, for a long time before leaving.

    224

    后二日，顼遂往山西，果有大湫。即坐于湫旁以伺之。至当午，忽有片云，自西冉冉而降于湫上，有一道士自云中下，颀然而长，约丈余，立湫之岸，于袖中出墨符数道投湫中。顷之，湫水尽涸。见一黄龙帖然俯于沙。顼即厉声呼：“天有命，杀黄龙者死。”言讫，湫水尽溢。道士怒，即于袖中出丹字数符投之，湫水又竭。即震声呼，如前词，其水再溢。道士怒甚，凡食顷，乃出朱符十余道，向空掷之，尽化为赤云，入湫，湫水即竭。呼之如前词，湫水又溢。道士顾谓顼曰：“吾一十年始得此龙为食，奈何子儒士也，奚救此异类耶！”怒责数言而去。顼亦还山中

    Explanation

    224

    Two days later, Xu went to Shanxi and indeed found a large pond. He immediately sat by the pond to wait for it. At noon, suddenly a cloud drifted down from the west onto the pond. A tall and slender Taoist emerged from the cloud, about ten feet tall, and stood on the shore of the pond. He took out several black talismans from his sleeve and threw them into the pond. Soon, the water in the pond completely dried up, revealing a yellow dragon lying flat on the sand. Xu then shouted sternly, "Heaven has decreed, whoever kills the yellow dragon shall die." After he finished speaking, the water in the pond overflowed again. The Taoist became very angry and immediately took out several red talismans from his sleeve and threw them into the pond. The water in the pond dried up again. He then shouted loudly, repeating the same words, and the water in the pond overflowed again. The Taoist became even angrier and, for the duration of a meal, took out more than ten red talismans, threw them into the air, and they all turned into red clouds, entering the pond and causing the water to immediately dry up. He shouted the same words again, and the water in the pond overflowed once more. The Taoist turned to speak to Xu, "I have been searching for ten years to find this dragon to eat. Why are you, a scholar, saving this strange creature?" He angrily scolded a few words and then left. Xu also returned to the mountains.

    225

    是夕，梦前时老人来谢曰：“赖得君子救我，不然，几死道士手。深诚所感，千万何言。今奏一珠，可于湫岸访之，用表我心重报也。”顼往寻之，果得一粒径寸珠于湫岸草中，光耀洞澈，殆不可识。顼后特至广陵市，有胡人见之曰：“此真骊龙之宝也。而世人莫可得。”以数千万为价而市之
    ○卢元裕
    　　故唐安太守卢元裕未仕时，尝以中元设幡幢像，置盂兰于其间。俄闻盆中有唧唧之音，元裕视，见一小龙才寸许，逸状奇姿，婉然可爱。于是以水沃之，其龙伸足振鬣已长数尺矣。元裕大恐。有白云自盆中而起，其龙亦逐云而去。元裕即翰之父也
    ○李修
    　　唐浙西观察使李修，元和七年为绛郡守。是岁，其属县龙门有龙见，时观者千数。郡以状闻于太府。时相国河东府张弘靖为河中节度使，相国之子故舒州刺史以宗尝为文以赞其事

    Explanation

    225

    That evening, a dream came to the old man from before, who came to thank him, saying, "I was saved by your virtue, otherwise I would have nearly died in the hands of a Taoist priest. Deeply moved by your sincerity, what can I say in return? Today I present you with a pearl, which you can find at the bank of the pool, to express my deep gratitude." Xu went to look for it, and indeed found a pearl the size of an inch in the grass at the bank of the pool, shining brightly and clearly, almost impossible to recognize. Xu later went specially to Guangling City, where a foreigner saw it and said, "This is truly the treasure of the black dragon. And no one in the world can obtain it." He bought it for several million as the price.

    ○Lu Yuanyu
    The former governor of An Prefecture, Lu Yuanyu, before he entered officialdom, once set up banners, flags, and images during the Zhongyuan Festival, placing the Ullambana Festival among them. Soon he heard a chirping sound in the pot, and Lu Yuanyu looked, and saw a small dragon about an inch long, with graceful and unique appearance, lovely and charming. Then he washed it with water, and the dragon stretched its legs and shook its mane, growing several feet long. Lu Yuanyu was greatly frightened. White clouds rose from the pot, and the dragon followed the clouds and departed. Lu Yuanyu was the father of Han.

    ○Li Xiu
    Li Xiu, the Tang Dynasty observer of Zhexi, was the governor of Jiang County in the seventh year of Yuanhe. That year, there was a dragon seen in the county of Longmen under his jurisdiction, and at that time there were thousands of spectators. The county reported the situation to the Taifu. At that time, Zhang Hongjing, the chancellor of Hedong Prefecture, was the military governor of Hedong, and his son, the former governor of Shuzhou, once wrote an article to praise the event.

    226

    ○卢君畅
    　　故东都留守判官祠部郎中范阳卢君畅为白衣时，侨居汉上。尝一日，独驱郊野，见二白犬，腰甚长而其臆丰，飘然若坠，俱驰走田间。卢讶其异于常犬，因立马以望。俄而其犬俱跳入于一湫中。已而湫浪泛腾，旋有二白龙自湫中起，云气噎空，风雷大震。卢惧甚，鞭马而归，未及行数里，衣尽沾湿。方悟二犬乃龙也。知
    ○法喜寺
    　　政阳郡东南有法喜寺，去郡远百里，而正居渭水西。唐元和末，寺僧有频梦一白龙者自渭水来，止于佛殿西楹，蟠绕且久，乃直东而去，明日则雨。如是者数矣。其僧异之，因语与人。人曰：“福地盖神祗所居，固龙之宅也。而佛寺亦为龙所依焉。故释氏有天龙八部，其义在矣。况郊野外寺，殿宇清敞，为龙之止，不亦宜乎。原以土龙置于寺楹间，且用识其梦也。”僧召工，合土为偶龙，具告其状，而于殿西楹置焉。功毕，甚得云间势，蜿蜒鳞鬣，曲尽其妙，虽丹青之巧，不能加也。

    Explanation

    226

    ○Lu Jun Chang
    　　Formerly, when Lu Jun Chang, the Cishi (censorial) official of the Eastern Capital and Langzhong (counselor) of the Sacrificial Department from Fanyang, was a commoner, he resided as a guest in the Han River region. One day, he drove alone into the countryside and saw two white dogs with long waists and full chests, floating as if they were falling, both running through the fields. Lu was surprised by their unusual appearance compared to ordinary dogs, so he stopped his horse to observe. Soon, the dogs both jumped into a pool. Shortly after, the waves of the pool surged, and two white dragons rose from the pool, filling the sky with thick clouds and causing a great storm with wind and thunder. Lu was greatly frightened, whipped his horse, and returned home. Before he had traveled several miles, his clothes were soaked. He then realized that the two dogs were actually dragons.

    ○Fa Xi Temple
    　　In the southeast of Zhengyang Prefecture, there is Fa Xi Temple, which is 100 li away from the prefecture but located on the western bank of the Wei River. At the end of the Yuanhe era during the Tang Dynasty, the temple monks frequently dreamed of a white dragon coming from the Wei River, stopping at the western pillar of the Buddha hall, coiling and twisting for a long time, then moving directly eastward, and it would rain the next day. This happened several times. The monks found this strange and told others about it. Someone said, "This blessed place is where gods and deities reside, certainly the dwelling of dragons. And the Buddhist temple is also a place where dragons rest. Therefore, in Buddhism, there are the Eight Classes of Celestial Beings, including dragons, whose meaning is clear. Moreover, a temple in the suburbs with clear and spacious halls is an appropriate place for dragons to rest. Originally, a clay dragon should be placed between the temple pillars to remember the dream." The monks summoned workers, mixed soil to make a clay dragon, described its appearance in detail, and placed it at the western pillar of the Buddha hall. When the work was completed, the clay dragon greatly resembled the appearance of a cloud, winding with scales and fins, fully expressing its wonder, even surpassing the best paintings.

    227

    至长庆初，其寺居人有偃于外门者，见一物従西轩直出，飘飘然若升云状，飞驰出寺，望谓水而去。夜将分，始归西轩下。细而视之，果白龙也。明日因告寺僧。僧奇之。又数日，寺僧尽赴村民会斋去，至午方归，因入殿视，像龙已失矣。寺僧且叹且异，相顾语曰：“是龙也，虽假以土，尚能变化无方，去莫知其适，来莫究其自，果灵物乎。”及晚，有阴云起于渭水，俄而将逼殿宇，忽有一物自云中跃而出，指西轩以入。寺僧惧惊，且视之，乃见像龙已在西楹上。迫而观之，其龙鬈鬣鳞角，若尽沾湿。自是因以铁锁系之。其后里中有旱涝，祈祷之，应若影响

    Explanation

    227

    By the beginning of the Changqing era, a resident of the temple was lying down at the outer gate when he saw an object emerging directly from the western pavilion, floating as if rising into the clouds, then swiftly flying out of the temple, seemingly heading toward the water. When nearly midnight had passed, it finally returned beneath the western pavilion. Upon closely examining it, he realized it was indeed a white dragon. The next day, he informed the monks of the temple. The monks were astonished. A few days later, all the monks went to a village meal gathering and did not return until noon. Upon entering the hall to look, they found the dragon image missing. The monks, both sighing and marveling, looked at one another and said, "This dragon, though made of earth, still possesses the power to transform without limit. Its departure is unknown, and its arrival is untraceable—surely it is a spiritual being?" By evening, dark clouds arose over the Wei River. Soon, they approached the temple. Suddenly, something leaped from the clouds, pointing toward the western pavilion and entering. The monks, frightened, looked and saw that the dragon image was already on the western pillar. Approaching to observe, they saw the dragon's curling mane, scales, and horns as if all were wet. From that day on, they secured it with an iron chain. Later, when the village suffered from drought or flood, prayers to it were answered as swiftly as shadow follows form.

    228

    ○龙庙
    　　汾水贯太原而南注，水有二桥，其南桥下尝有龙见，由是架龙庙于桥下。故相国令狐楚居守北都时，有一龙自庙中出，倾都士女皆纵观，近食顷，方拏奋而去，旋有震雷暴雨焉。又明年秋，汾水延溢，有一白蛇自庙中出，既出而庙屋摧圯，其桥亦坏。时唐太和初也。知
    ○李徵
    　　陇西李徵，皇族子，家于虢略。徵少博学，善属文，弱冠従州府贡焉，时号名士。天宝十载春于尚书右丞杨没榜下登进士第。后数年，调补江南尉。徵性疏逸，恃才倨傲，不能屈迹卑僚，尝郁郁不乐。每同舍会，既酣，顾谓其群官曰：“生乃与君等为伍耶！”其寮佐咸嫉之。及谢秩，则退归闭门，不与人通者近岁余。后迫衣食，乃具妆东游吴楚之间，以干郡国长吏。吴楚人闻其声固久矣，乃至，皆开馆以俟之。宴游极欢。将去，悉厚遣以实其囊橐。徵在吴楚且周岁，所获馈遗甚多。知

    Explanation

    228

    ○Dragon Temple
    The Fen River flows through Taiyuan and runs southward, with two bridges along its course. Once, a dragon was seen beneath the southern bridge, leading to the construction of a dragon temple under the bridge. During the time when the former chancellor Linghu Chu was in charge of the northern capital, a dragon emerged from the temple, causing all the people and women in the city to gather and watch. After a short while, the dragon struggled and flew away, and soon there was a thunderstorm. The following autumn, the Fen River overflowed, and a white snake emerged from the temple. As it emerged, the temple buildings collapsed, and the bridge also broke. This occurred in the early years of the Tahe era of the Tang dynasty.

    ○Li Zheng
    Li Zheng, a member of the imperial family from Longxi, lived in Guole. Zheng was young and widely learned, skilled in writing, and recommended by the state and county at the age of twenty. He was known as a famous scholar of his time. In the spring of the tenth year of the Tianbao era, he passed the Jinshi examination under the list of the Right Censor Yang Mo. After several years, he was appointed as the magistrate of Jiangnan. Zheng was arrogant due to his talent and could not lower himself to be a low-ranking official, often feeling depressed and unhappy. At gatherings with his colleagues, after they had drunk deeply, he would look around and say to his officials, "I am now among you low-ranking officials!" His subordinates all envied him. After resigning from his post, he closed his door and did not communicate with anyone for nearly a year. Later, due to the need for food and clothing, he prepared and traveled east to Wu and Chu to seek favor with the local officials. The people of Wu and Chu had heard of his reputation for a long time, and upon his arrival, they all opened their halls to welcome him. They feasted and played very happily. When he was about to leave, they all gave him generous gifts to fill his bags. Zheng stayed in Wu and Chu for nearly a year and received many gifts.

    229

    西归虢洛，未至，舍于汝坟逆旅中。忽被疾发狂，鞭捶仆者。仆者不胜其苦。如是旬余，疾益甚，无何夜狂走，莫知其适。家僮迹其去而伺之，尽一月而徵竟不回，于是仆者驱其乘马，挈其囊橐而远遁去
    　　至明年，陈郡袁傪以监察御史奉诏使岭南，乘传至商于於界。晨将发，其驿吏白曰：“道有虎，暴而食人，故过于此者，非昼而莫敢进。今尚早，愿且驻车，决不可前。”傪怒曰：“我天子使，众骑极多，山泽之兽能为害耶！”遂命驾去

    Explanation

    229

    He was traveling westward to the region of Guo and Luo, but before reaching his destination, he stopped at an inn by the River Luo. Suddenly, he fell ill and went mad, whipping and beating his servants. The servants could not bear the suffering. For about ten days, his illness grew increasingly severe. Without delay, he ran wildly at night, and no one knew where he was going. His household servants followed his path and waited for him, but after a whole month, they finally did not return. Thus, the servants drove away his riding horse, took his bags and belongings, and fled far away.

    The following year, Chen Commandery's Censorial Inspector Yuan You was sent by imperial decree to the Lingnan region. He arrived at the boundary of Shangyu by riding in an official carriage. In the morning, he was about to depart, and his postal official said, "There are tigers on the road, fierce and eating people, so those passing by here do not dare to proceed unless it is during the day. It is still early, I wish to stop the carriage for now, definitely cannot proceed." Yuan You became angry and said, "I am an envoy of the Son of Heaven, with many horses, can wild beasts in the mountains and marshes cause harm?" He then ordered the carriage to depart.

    230

    行未尽一里，果有一虎自草中突出。傪惊甚。俄而虎匿身草中，人声而言曰：“异乎哉，几伤我故人也。”傪聆其音似李徵。傪昔与徵同登进士第，分极深，别有年矣。忽闻其语，既惊且异，而莫测焉。遂问曰：“子为谁得非故人陇西子乎？”虎呻吟数声，若嗟泣之状，已而谓傪曰：“我，李徵也。君幸少留，与我一语。”傪即降骑，因问曰：“李君，李君，何为而至是也！”虎曰：“我自与足下别，音问旷阻且久矣。幸喜得无恙乎今又去何适向者见君，有二吏驱而前，驿隶挈印囊以导，庸非为御史而出使乎？”傪曰：“近者幸得备御史之列，今乃使岭南。”虎曰：“吾子以文学立身，位登朝序，可谓盛矣。况宪台清峻，分纠百揆；圣明慎择，尤异于人。

    Explanation

    230

    After walking not quite one li, indeed, a tiger suddenly emerged from the grass. The narrator was greatly startled. Soon after, the tiger hid itself in the grass and spoke in a human voice, saying, "How strange! It almost hurt my old friend." The narrator recognized the voice as resembling that of Li Zheng. The narrator had once been a fellow candidate with Li Zheng in the imperial examinations, and they had a deep bond, but it had been many years since they last met. Upon hearing his voice, the narrator was both surprised and puzzled, unable to understand what was happening. He asked, "Who are you? Are you not my old friend from Longxi?" The tiger groaned several times, as if in lamentation, and then said, "I am Li Zheng. Kindly stay a moment and speak with me." The narrator dismounted his horse and asked, "Li Zheng, Li Zheng, why have you come to this?" The tiger replied, "Since our parting, communication has been interrupted for a long time. Are you glad to be unharmed? Now, why are you going to such a place? Earlier, I saw you with two officials pushing you forward, a postal servant carrying an official bag leading the way. Isn't it for the position of censor and a mission?" The narrator said, "Recently, I have been fortunate enough to hold the position of censor. Now, I am being sent on a mission to Lingnan." The tiger said, "You have established yourself through your literary talents, reaching a high position in the court. This is indeed a great achievement. Moreover, the Censorate is known for its high moral standards and strict discipline, and the wise ruler carefully selects those who serve in this capacity."

    231

    心喜故人居此地，甚可贺。”傪曰：“往者吾与执事同年成名，交契深密，异于常友。自声容间阻，时去如流，想望风仪，心目俱断。不意今日获君念旧之言。虽然，执事何为不我见，而自匿于草莽中故人之分，岂当如是耶！”虎曰：“我今不为人矣，安得见君乎？”珪即诘其事。虎曰：“我前身客吴楚，去岁方还，道次汝坟，忽婴疾发狂走山谷中，俄以左右手据地而步，自是觉心愈狠，力愈倍，及视其肱髀，则有釐毛生焉。又见冕衣而行于道者、负而奔者、翼而翱者、毳而驰者，则欲得而啖之。既至汉阴南，以饥肠所迫，值一人腯然其肌，因擒以咀之立尽。由此率不为常。非不念妻孥、思朋友，直以行负神祇，一日化为异兽，有靦于人，故分不见矣。嗟夫！我与君同年登第，交契素厚。

    Explanation

    231

    I am pleased that my old friend lives here, it is very fortunate." The official said, "In the past, I and you became famous in the same year, our friendship was deep and close, different from ordinary friends. Since the separation of sounds and appearances, time has passed like a stream, I often think of and admire your appearance and demeanor, both heart and eyes are broken. I did not expect to hear your words of remembrance today. However, why don't you see me, and hide among the grasses? Is this how the relationship of old friends should be!" The tiger said, "I am no longer a human, how can I see you?" The official immediately asked about the matter. The tiger said, "My former life was spent in Wu and Chu, last year I returned, on the road near your tomb, I suddenly fell ill and went mad, running into the mountains and valleys, soon I walked with my hands on the ground, from then on, I felt my heart became more fierce, and my strength doubled, when I looked at my thighs and upper arms, I saw hair growing. I also saw people wearing crowns and robes walking on the road, carrying and running, flying with wings, and galloping with fur, I wanted to eat them. After I reached the southern part of Han Yin, because of hunger, I encountered a man with fat and strong muscles, I captured and chewed him up immediately. From then on, I rarely acted normally. I did not forget my family and friends, but because of my actions, I was punished by the gods, one day I transformed into a strange beast, ashamed to be seen by people, so I no longer have contact with people. Alas! I and you passed the imperial examinations in the same year, our friendship has always been strong.

    232

    今日执天宪，耀亲友。而我匿身林薮，永谢人寰，跃而吁天，挽而泣地，身毁不用，是果命乎？”因呼吟咨嗟，殆不自胜，遂泣。珪且问曰：“君今既为异类，何尚能人言耶！”虎曰：“我今形变而心甚悟，故有摚突，以悚以恨，难尽道耳。幸故人念我，深恕我无状之咎，亦其愿也。然君自南方回车，我再值君，必当昧其平生耳。此时视君之躯，犹吾机上一物。君亦宜严其警従以备之，无使成我之罪，取笑于士君子。”又曰：“我与君真忘形之友也。而我将有所托，其可乎？珪曰：“平昔故人，安有不可哉恨未知何如事，愿尽教之。”虎曰：“君不许我，我何敢言。今既许我，岂有隐耶初我于逆旅中，为疾发狂，既入荒山，而仆者驱我乘马衣囊悉逃去。

    Explanation

    232

    Today, I held the mandate of heaven, honoring my relatives and friends. Yet, I have hidden myself in the wilderness, forever bidding farewell to the world of humanity. I leap and cry to the heavens, pull and weep to the earth, ruined and not used—Is this truly fate?” Thus, I called out, sighed, and lamented, barely able to bear it, and then I wept. Gui asked, “Now that you have become an outsider, how can you still speak like a human?” The tiger replied, “Though my form has changed, my heart has greatly enlightened. Thus, there is collision, filled with fear and hatred, which is hard to fully express. Fortunately, old friends remember me and deeply forgive my outrageous guilt, which is also my wish. However, when you return from the south, I will meet you again, and I will surely forget my entire life. At that time, I will view your body as just an object on my machine. You should also strictly guard and prepare, so that it does not become my crime and be mocked by the wise and virtuous.” He then said, “I and you are truly friends who transcend form. And I will entrust something to you, is that acceptable?” Gui said, “Old friends from the past, how could it be unacceptable? I just do not know what matter it is, I wish to hear it all.” The tiger said, “If you do not allow me, how dare I speak. Now that you have allowed me, how could I keep it hidden? Initially, when I was in an inn, I became ill and went mad. After entering the wilderness, the attendants drove me away, taking my horse and clothes, leaving me empty-handed.

    233

    吾妻孥尚在虢洛，岂念我化为异类乎君若自南回，为赍书访妻子，但云我已死，无言今日事。幸记之。”又曰：“吾于人世且无资业，有子尚稚，固难自谋。君位列周行，素秉夙义，昔日之分，岂他人能右哉必望念其孤弱，时赈其乏，无使殍死于道途，亦恩之大者。”言已又悲泣。珪亦泣曰：“珪与足下休戚同焉。然则足下子亦珪子也。当力副厚命，又何虞其不至哉？”虎曰：“我有旧文数十篇未行于代，虽有遗稿，尽皆散落。君为我传录，诚不敢列人之阈，然亦贵传于子孙也。”珪即呼仆命笔，随其口书，近二十章，文甚高，理甚远。珪阅而叹者再三。虎曰：“此吾平生之素也，安敢望其传乎？”又曰：“君御命乘传，当甚奔迫，今久留驿隶，兢悚万端。与君永诀，异途之恨，何可言哉！”珪亦与之叙别，久而方去

    Explanation

    233

    My wife and children are still in Guo and Luo, how could they think I have transformed into a different kind? If you return from the south, deliver a letter to visit my wife and children, just say I am dead, do not mention today's events. Please remember this. He also said, "I have no assets or business in this world, I have a young son, it is indeed difficult to manage on my own. You hold a position among the officials, you have always upheld your principles, the bond we shared in the past, how could anyone else surpass you? I hope you will consider his loneliness and weakness, to provide him with relief when he is in need, do not let him die of starvation on the road, this would also be a great act of kindness." After he finished speaking, he cried again. Guan also cried and said, "Guan and you share the same joys and sorrows. Then your son is also Guan's son. I will do my best to fulfill your trust, and why should I doubt that it will not happen?" The tiger said, "I have several dozen old writings that have not been published, although there are some remaining drafts, they are all scattered. You should transcribe them for me, I truly do not dare to place them among the works of others, however, it is valuable to pass them down to future generations." Guan immediately called his servant to get a pen, and wrote according to his dictation, about twenty chapters, the writing is very profound, and the reasoning is very far-reaching. Guan read and sighed repeatedly. The tiger said, "These are my lifelong aspirations, how could I dare to hope they will be passed down?" He also said, "You are on a mission from the emperor, you should be very busy, now you have stayed for a long time at the post station, feeling extremely anxious. To part ways forever, the regret of going different paths, how can it be expressed!" Guan also bid him farewell, after a long time, he finally left.

    234

    珪自南回，遂专命持书及帽赙之礼，寄于徵子。月余，徵子自虢洛来京诣珪门，求先人之柩。珪不得已，具疏其事。后珪以己俸均给徵妻子，免饥冻焉。珪后官至兵部侍郎

    Explanation

    234

    Gua returned from the south and then took the initiative to order the sending of books and a hat as a funeral gift, which he entrusted to Zengzi's son. A month later, Zengzi's son came from Guo and Luo to the capital, visiting Gua's door to request the coffin of his father. Gua, unable to refuse, wrote a detailed report on the matter. Later, Gua distributed his own salary equally to Zengzi's family, preventing them from starving or freezing. Gua later reached the position of Vice Minister of War.

    235

    ○河内崔守
    　　有崔君者，贞元中为河内守。崔君贪而刻。河内人苦之。常于佛寺中假佛像金凡数镒，而竞不酬直。僧以太守竟不敢言。未几，崔君卒于郡。是日，寺有牛产一犊。其犊顶上有白毛若缕，出文字曰崔某者。寺僧相与观之，且叹曰：“崔君常假此寺中佛像金，而竟不还。今日事，果何如哉？”崔君家闻之，即以他牛易其犊。既至，命剪去文字。已而便生。及至其家，虽豢以刍粟，卒不食。崔氏且以为异，竟归其寺焉。知
    ○唐玄宗龙马
    　　海岱之间出玄黄石，或云茹之可以长生。玄宗皇帝尝命临淄守每岁采而贡焉。开元二十七年，江夏李邕为临淄守。是岁秋，因入山采玄黄石，忽遇一翁，质甚妙，而丰度明秀，髭髯极丰，衣褐衣，自道左出，叩李邕马，且告曰：“君侯躬自采药，岂不为延圣主之寿乎？”曰：“然。”翁曰：“圣主当获龙马，则享国万岁，无劳采药耳。”邕曰：“龙马安在？”答曰：“当在齐鲁之郊。若获之，即是太平之符。

    Explanation

    235

    ○Hebei Fui
    There was a man named Fui who served as the governor of Hebei during the Zhenyuan era. Fui was greedy and harsh, causing much suffering to the people of Hebei. He frequently borrowed gold from a Buddha statue in a temple, amounting to several jin, but never paid it back. The monks dared not speak to the governor. Soon after, Fui died in his office. On that day, a cow in the temple gave birth to a calf. The calf's head had white hair like threads, and the characters "Fui" appeared on its forehead. The temple monks gathered to observe it and sighed, saying, "Fui often borrowed gold from this temple's Buddha statue but never returned it. Today's events, what do they signify?" Fui's family heard about it and immediately exchanged the calf for another cow. Once they got home, they ordered the characters to be cut off. However, they soon reappeared. When the calf reached Fui's house, despite being fed with hay and grain, it eventually refused to eat. Fui's family found this strange and finally returned the calf to the temple.

    ○Emperor Xuanzong's Dragon Horse
    Between the Hai and Dai mountains, there is a black and yellow stone. It is said that eating it can lead to immortality. Emperor Xuanzong once ordered the governor of Linzi to collect and present it annually. In the twenty-seventh year of Kaiyuan, Li Yong of Jiangxia was the governor of Linzi. That autumn, he went into the mountains to collect the black and yellow stone. Suddenly, he met an old man who was very handsome, with a dignified and refined appearance, thick beard, wearing a rough cloth robe, emerging from the left side of the path, and tapping Li Yong's horse, saying, "Lord, you personally collect herbs, isn't it to extend the life of the holy emperor?" Li Yong replied, "Yes." The old man said, "The holy emperor will obtain a dragon horse, then he will enjoy the kingdom for ten thousand years, no need to collect herbs." Li Yong asked, "Where is the dragon horse?" The old man replied, "It should be in the outskirts of Qi and Lu. If obtained, it will be a sign of peace."

    236

    虽麟凤龟龙，不足以并其瑞。”邕方命驾以后乘，遽亡见矣。邕大异之，顾谓従事曰：“得非神人乎？”即命其吏王乾贞者，求龙马于齐鲁之间。至开元二十九年夏五月，乾真果得马于北海郡民马会恩之家。其色骓毛，两胁有鳞甲，鬃尾若龙之鬐鬣，嘶鸣真虡笛之音，日驰三百里。乾真讯其所自，会恩曰：“吾独有牝马，常浴于淄水，遂有胎而产。因以龙子呼之。”乾真即白于邕。邕甚喜，以表其事献之。上大悦，诏内闲厩，异其当豢。命画工图其状，用颁示中外

    Explanation

    236

    Even the auspicious signs of the qilin, phoenix, turtle, and dragon are not sufficient to compare with its auspiciousness. Yuan ordered to drive the carriage behind it, but it suddenly disappeared. Yuan was greatly amazed and turned to say to his subordinate, "Is this not a divine being?" He immediately ordered the official Wang Qianzhen to search for the dragon horse between Qi and Lu. In the summer of the fifth month of the twenty-ninth year of Kaiyuan, Qianzhen indeed found a horse at the home of Ma Huien, a resident of Beihai Commandery. The horse had a white mane, scales on its sides, a mane and tail like the ridges of a dragon, and neighed with the sound of a flute. It could travel three hundred miles in a day. Qianzhen asked about its origin, and Huien said, "I had only a mare, which often bathed in the Zhi River, and then conceived and gave birth. Therefore, I called it the Dragon's Son." Qianzhen immediately reported this to Yuan. Yuan was very pleased and submitted a report detailing the matter to the emperor. The emperor was greatly delighted, ordered it to be kept in the imperial stables, and treated it differently and kept it specially. He also ordered artists to draw its likeness and distribute it to be shown both inside and outside the court.

    237

    ○王薰
    　　天宝初，有王薰者，居长安延寿里中。常一夕，有三数辈挈食会薰所居。既饭食，烛前忽有巨擘出烛影下，薰与诸友且惧，相与观之。其擘色黑，而有毛甚多。未几，影外有语曰：“君有会，不能一见呼耶愿得少肉置掌中。”薰莫测其由，即与之。其臂遂引去。少顷，又伸其臂曰：“幸君与我肉，今食且尽，愿君更赐之。”薰又置肉于掌中，已而又去。于是相与谋曰：“此必怪也。伺其再来，当断其臂。”顷之果来，拔剑斩之。臂既堕，其身亦远。俯而视之，乃一驴足，血流满地。明日，因以血踪寻之，直入里中民家。即以事问民，民曰：“家养一驴，且二十年矣。夜失一足，有似刃而断者焉。方骇之。”薰具言其事，即杀而食之

    Explanation

    237

    In the early years of the Tianbao era, there was a man named Wang Xun who lived in the Yanshou Quarter of Chang'an. One evening, several people brought food to meet at Wang Xun's residence. After they had eaten, a large hand suddenly emerged from the shadow of the candle. Wang Xun and his friends were both frightened and watched it. The hand was black and had many hairs on it. Soon, a voice came from outside the shadow, saying, "You have a gathering, but you can't even invite me once? I wish to have some meat placed in my palm." Wang Xun could not understand the reason, so he gave it some meat. The hand then withdrew. A short while later, it extended its arm again and said, "Thank you for giving me meat; now it is almost finished. I wish you would give me more." Wang Xun placed more meat in its palm, and it withdrew again. They then discussed among themselves, "This must be a strange occurrence. We should wait for it to come again and cut off its arm." Soon enough, it returned, and Wang Xun drew his sword and cut it off. The arm fell, and the rest of the body disappeared. When he looked down, he saw that the arm was actually a donkey's leg, with blood flowing everywhere. The next day, following the blood trail, he found it leading to a house in the neighborhood. He asked the homeowner about the incident, and the homeowner said, "I have kept a donkey for about twenty years. Last night, one of its legs was cut off, as if by a sharp blade. I was very surprised by this." Wang Xun told the homeowner about the incident, and they killed the donkey and ate it.

    238

    ○郭钊
    　　郭司空钊，大和中，自梓潼移镇西凉府。时有阍者甚谨朴，钊念之，多委以事。常一日，钊命市纹缯丝帛百余段，其价倍，且以为欺我，即囚于狱，用致其罪。狱既具，钊命笞于庭。忽有十余犬争拥其背，吏卒莫能制。钊大异之，且讯其事。阍者曰：“某好阅佛氏《金刚经》，自孩稚常以食饲群犬，不知其他。”钊叹曰：“犬尚能感其惠，吾安可以不施恩。”遂释放阍者
    ○赵叟
    　　扶风县西有天和寺，在高冈之上。其下有龛，豁若堂，中有贫者赵叟家焉。叟无妻儿，病足伛偻，常策杖行乞。里中人哀其老病且穷无所归，率给以食。叟既得食，常先聚群犬以餐之。后岁余，叟病寒，卧于龛中。时大雪，叟无衣，裸形俯地，且战且呻。其群犬俱集于叟前，摇尾而嗥，已而环其衽席，竞以足拥叟体，由是寒少解。后旬余，竟以寒死其龛。犬具哀鸣，昼夜不歇，数日方去

    Explanation

    238

    Guo Zhao

    Guo Zhao, the Grand Controller, during the Dazhong period, was transferred from Zhitong to Xiliang. There was a gatekeeper who was very diligent and honest. Guo Zhao thought highly of him and entrusted him with many tasks. One day, Guo Zhao ordered the purchase of over a hundred pieces of fine silk fabric, but the price was double the usual amount, and he believed he had been deceived. The gatekeeper was imprisoned, and evidence was gathered to prove his guilt. Guo Zhao then ordered the gatekeeper to be flogged in the courtyard. Suddenly, more than ten dogs rushed towards the gatekeeper, surrounding him and barking, and the guards could not control them. Guo Zhao was greatly surprised and asked about the incident. The gatekeeper said, "I often read the Buddhist Diamond Sutra since I was a child, and I always fed the dogs. I don't know anything else." Guo Zhao sighed and said, "Even dogs can feel gratitude, how can I not show kindness." He then released the gatekeeper.

    Zhao Old Man

    West of Fufeng County, there is a Tianhe Temple, located on a high hill. Below the hill, there is a niche that is open like a hall. An old man named Zhao lived there. The old man had no wife or children, and he suffered from a bent back and weak legs. He often carried a walking stick and begged for food. The villagers pitied his old age, illness, and lack of a home, and they usually gave him food. After receiving food, the old man would always feed the dogs first. After over a year, the old man fell ill with a cold and lay in the niche. It was snowing heavily at the time, and the old man had no clothes, lying naked on the ground, shivering and groaning. The dogs gathered around the old man, wagging their tails and barking. Then they surrounded the old man's bed, competing to support his body with their feet, which helped to reduce the cold. After ten days, the old man died in the niche. The dogs mourned loudly, crying day and night, and only left after several days.

    239

    ○韩生
    　　唐贞元中，有大理评事韩生者，侨居西河郡南。有一马，甚豪骏。常一日清晨，忽委首于枥，汗而且喘，若涉远而殆者。圉人怪之，具白于韩生。韩生怒：“若盗马夜出，使吾马力殆。谁之罪？”乃令朴焉。圉人无以辞，遂受朴。至明日，其马又汗而喘。圉人窃异之，莫可测。是夕，圉人卧无厩舍，阖扉，乃于隙中窥之。忽见韩生所畜黑犬至厩中，且嗥且跃，俄化为一丈夫，衣冠尽黑，既挟鞍致马上，驾而去。行至门，门垣甚高，其黑衣人以鞭击马，跃而过。黑衣者乘马而去。半夜还，下马解鞍，其黑衣人又嗥跃，还化为犬。圉人惊异，不敢泄于人。知

    Explanation

    239

    During the Zhenyuan period of the Tang Dynasty, there was a Mr. Han, a judicial official residing temporarily in the southern part of Xihé Commandery. He owned a horse that was very powerful and swift. One morning, the horse suddenly bowed its head at the manger, sweating and panting, as if it had traveled far and was exhausted. The stable master found it strange and reported it to Mr. Han. Mr. Han was angry: "If someone stole the horse and rode it out at night, making my horse exhausted, whose fault is it?" He then ordered the horse to be punished. The stable master had no defense and accepted the punishment. The next day, the horse again sweated and panted. The stable master secretly found it strange, but could not understand it. That night, the stable master, unable to sleep in the stable, closed the door and peered through a crack. Suddenly, he saw Mr. Han's black dog come into the stable, barking and jumping, and soon transformed into a man wearing all-black clothing. The man took the saddle and put it on the horse, then rode away. Riding to the gate, where the wall was very high, the black-clothed man whipped the horse and leaped over the wall. He returned at midnight, dismounted and removed the saddle. The black-clothed man barked and jumped, then transformed back into a dog. The stable master was amazed and dared not tell anyone.

    240

    后一夕，黑犬又驾马而去，逮晓方归。圉人因寻马踪，以天雨新霁，历历可辨，直至南十余里一古墓前，马迹方绝。圉人乃结茅斋于墓侧。来夕，先止于斋中以伺之。夜将分，黑衣人果驾马而来。下马，系于野树。其人入墓，与数辈笑言极欢。圉人在茅斋中俯而听之，不敢动。近数食顷，黑衣人告去，数辈送出墓外。于野，有一褐衣者顾谓黑衣人曰：“韩氏名藉今安在？”黑衣人曰：“吾已收在扌寿练石下。吾子无以为忧。”褐衣者曰：“慎毋泄。泄则吾属不全矣。”黑衣人曰：“谨受教。”褐衣者曰：“韩氏稚儿有字乎？”曰：“未也。吾伺有字，即编于名籍，不敢忘。”褐衣者曰：“明夕再来，当得以笑语。”黑衣唯而去

    Explanation

    240

    The following night, the black dog once again rode off on the horse, returning only at dawn. The stableman then searched for the horse's tracks, which were clearly distinguishable because of the recent rain and clear weather, leading him over ten miles south to an ancient tomb where the horse's tracks finally disappeared. The stableman built a thatched hut beside the tomb. On the following night, he stayed in the hut to wait for the black dog. As the night was nearly half over, the black-clothed person indeed came riding the horse. He dismounted and tied the horse to a wild tree, then entered the tomb, laughing and talking merrily with several others. The stableman crouched down to listen to them, afraid to move. After several meals' time, the black-clothed person announced his departure, and several others escorted him out of the tomb. Out in the wild, a brown-clothed person turned and said to the black-clothed person, "Where is the Han family's name register now?" The black-clothed person replied, "I have already collected it under the Shoulian stone. You have nothing to worry about." The brown-clothed person warned, "Be careful not to leak it. If it is leaked, we will all be in danger." The black-clothed person said, "I will heed your advice." The brown-clothed person asked, "Does the young Han child have a name yet?" The black-clothed person answered, "Not yet. I will wait until he has a name, then compile him in the name register, and I will not forget." The brown-clothed person invited, "Come again tomorrow night, and we will be able to talk and laugh." The black-clothed person nodded and left.

    241

    及晓，圉者归，遂以其事密告于韩生。生即命肉诱其犬。犬既至，因以绳系。乃次所闻，遂穷扌寿练石下，果得一轴书，具载韩氏兄弟妻子家僮名氏，纪莫不具。盖所谓韩氏名籍也。有子生一月矣，独此子不书，所谓“稚儿未字”也。韩生大异，命致犬于庭，鞭而杀之。熟其肉以食家僮。已而率邻居士子千余辈，执弧矢兵仗，至郡南古墓前，发基墓，墓中有数犬，毛状皆异，尽杀之以归

    Explanation

    241

    By dawn, the stablekeeper returned and secretly reported the matter to Han. Han immediately ordered meat to lure the dog. Once the dog arrived, he tied it up. Following the information, he searched thoroughly under the stone slab and indeed found a scroll of writing, which fully recorded the names and surnames of the Han family members, including brothers, wives, and servants, with nothing omitted. This was the so-called register of the Han family. There was a son who had been born for a month, but this son was not recorded, which was referred to as "a young child not yet named." Han was greatly surprised and ordered the dog to be brought to the courtyard, where he whipped and killed it. He cooked its meat to feed the servants. Shortly afterwards, he led over a thousand scholars from the neighborhood, armed with bows, arrows, and weapons, to the ancient tomb in the southern part of the county. They opened the tomb, and inside there were several dogs, all with different fur patterns. They killed them all and returned.

    242

    ○李甲
    　　宝应中，有李氏子，亡其名，家于洛阳。其世以不好杀，故家未尝畜猫，所以宥鼠之死也。迨其孙，亦能世祖父意。常一日，李氏大集其亲友，会食于堂。既坐，而门外有数百鼠，俱人立，以前足相鼓，如甚喜状。家僮惊异，告于李氏。李氏亲友乃空其堂而纵观。人去且尽，堂忽摧圯，其家无一伤者。堂既摧，群鼠亦去。悲乎！鼠固微物也，尚能识恩而知报，况人乎如是则施恩者宜广其恩，而报恩者亦宜力其报。有不顾者，当视此以愧
    ○王缙
    　　唐相国王公缙，大历中与元载同执政事。常因入朝，天尚早，坐于烛下。其榻前有囊，公遂命侍童取之，侍童挈以进，觉其重不可举。公启视之，忽有一鼠长尺余，质甚丰白，囊中跃出。公大惧，顾谓其子曰：“我以不才缪居卿相，无德而贵，常惧有意外之咎。今异物接于手足，岂非祸之将萌耶！”后数日，果得罪，贬为缙云守

    Explanation

    242

    ○李甲
    In the Baoying era, there was a son of the Li family, whose name is unknown, living in Luoyang. His family had a tradition of not liking to kill, so they never kept cats, thus sparing the mice. By the time his grandson, he also understood the intentions of his ancestors. One day, the Li family gathered their relatives and friends for a meal in the hall. After they were seated, hundreds of mice appeared outside the door, standing on their hind legs and clapping their front paws, looking very joyful. The family servant was surprised and informed the Li family. The relatives and friends of the Li family then cleared the hall to watch. As people were leaving, the hall suddenly collapsed, but none of the family members were injured. After the hall collapsed, the mice also left. Alas! Mice are indeed small creatures, yet they can still recognize kindness and repay it; how much more so for humans! If so, those who bestow kindness should broaden their kindness, and those who repay kindness should also strive to repay it. Those who do not care, should feel ashamed by this.

    ○王缙
    During the Dali era, Wang Jin, a high-ranking official of the Tang Dynasty, served alongside Yuan Zai in government. One day, as he was about to enter court, it was still early, and he was sitting by the candlelight. There was a bag in front of his couch, and he ordered the attendant servant to bring it in. The servant carried it in, feeling it was too heavy to lift. Wang Jin opened it to look inside, and suddenly a mouse over a foot long, with a very plump and white body, jumped out of the bag. He was greatly frightened and looked at his son and said, "I am not talented but occupy a high position, lacking virtue but honored, often fearing unexpected misfortune. Now an unusual creature touches my hands and feet, isn't this a sign that disaster is about to come?" A few days later, he indeed committed a crime and was demoted to the governor of Jinyun.

    243

    ○王含
    　　太原王含者，为振武军都将。其母金氏，本胡人女，善弓马，素以犷悍闻。常驰健马，臂弓腰矢，入深山，取熊鹿狐兔，杀获甚多。故此人皆惮其能而雅重之。后年七十余，以老病，遂独止一室，辟侍婢，不许辄近左右，至夜即扃户而寝。往往发怒，过杖其家人辈。后一夕，既扃其户，家人忽闻轧然之声，遂趋而视之，望见一狼自室内开户而出。天未晓，而其狼自外还，入室又扃其门。家人甚惧，具白于含。是夕，于隙中潜窥，如家人言。含忧悸不自安。至晓，金氏召含，且令即市糜鹿。含熟以献，金氏曰：“吾所须生者耳。”于是，以生糜鹿致于前，金氏啖立尽。含益惧。家人辈或窃语其事，金氏闻之，色甚惭。是夕，既扃门，家人又伺而觇之，有狼遂破户而出。自是竟不还。

    Explanation

    243

    ○ Wang Han
    Wang Han of Taiyuan served as the commander of the Zhenwu Army. His mother, Madam Jin, was originally a woman of Hu ethnicity, skilled in archery and horseback riding, and was known from the beginning for her fierce and savage nature. She often rode strong horses, with bow in hand and arrows at her waist, entering deep mountains to hunt bears, deer, foxes, and hares, killing and capturing many. Thus, people all feared her strength and held her in high regard. Later, when she was over seventy years old, suffering from old age and illness, she lived alone in a single room, dismissing her female attendants and forbidding them from coming near her. At night, she would lock the door and go to sleep. She often became angry and beat her family members severely.

    One night, after she had locked the door, the family suddenly heard a grinding sound. They rushed to look and saw a wolf emerging from the room, opening the door from within. Before dawn, the wolf returned from outside, entered the room, and locked the door again. The family was greatly frightened and reported the matter in full to Wang Han. That same night, they peered through a crack and saw exactly what the family had described. Wang Han was filled with anxiety and could not find peace.

    At dawn, Madam Jin summoned Wang Han and ordered him to go immediately and buy a deer or elk. Wang Han brought a freshly killed deer and presented it to her. Madam Jin said, "I only need the living ones." So, they brought a live deer and elk before her, and Madam Jin devoured them instantly. Wang Han became even more terrified.

    Some of the family members whispered about the matter, and Madam Jin, upon hearing this, looked very ashamed. That night, after she had locked the door, the family once again watched and observed, and a wolf broke through the door and came out. From then on, it never returned.

    244

    ○晋阳民家
    　　晋阳以北，地寒而少竹，故居人多种苇成林，所以代南方之竹也。唐长庆初，北都有民，其家地多林苇，里中尝有会宴，置余食于其舍，至明日辄不知其所在。其民有贮缯帛于其室者，亦亡之。民窃异焉。后夜闻婴儿号者甚众，迫而听之，则阒然矣。明夕又闻，民惧且甚。后一日，乃语里中他民曰：“数多闻林中有婴儿号，吾度此地不当有婴儿，惧其怪耳。”即相与芟除其林，薙其草。既穷，得一穴，中有缯帛食器。见野狸十余，有颦而俯者，呻而仰者，瞬而乳者，偃而踞者，嗷然若相愁状。民尽杀之，自是里民用安其居

    Explanation

    244

    North of Jinyang, the land is cold and has few bamboos. Therefore, the residents often grow reeds into forests, to replace the bamboo of the south. In the early years of the Changqing era, there was a resident in the neighborhood whose property was covered with a dense reed forest. During a community banquet, the residents left leftover food in their house, but by the next morning, it was always gone. Some residents who stored silk and cloth in their rooms also lost them. The residents secretly found it strange. Later at night, they heard many babies crying. When they approached to listen, it was silent. The next evening, they heard it again, and the residents were even more afraid. On the following day, they told the other residents in the neighborhood, "We often hear many babies crying in the reed forest. I suspect there should not be babies here, and we are afraid of the strange occurrences." They then worked together to cut down the reed forest and clear the grass. When they dug deeper, they found a hole containing silk, cloth, and food containers. They saw more than ten wild cats, some frowning and bowing, some moaning and looking up, some blinking and nursing, some lying down and sitting, all making sounds as if they were in distress. The residents killed all of them, and from then on, the residents lived in peace.

    245

    ○唐玄宗
    　　开元二十三年秋，玄宗皇帝狩于近郊。驾至咸阳原，有大鹿兴于前，赑然其躯，颇异于常者。上命弓射之，一发而中。及驾还，及敕厨吏灸其■已进。而尚食具熟俎献。时张果老先生侍，上命果坐于前，以其肉赐之。果谢而食。既食，且奏曰：“陛下以此鹿为何如？”上曰：“吾只知其鹿也，亦未知何如哉？”果曰：“此鹿年且千岁矣。陛下幸问臣。”上笑曰：“此一兽耳，何遂言其千岁耶！”果曰：“昔汉元狩五年秋，臣侍武帝畋于上林，其従臣有生获此鹿而献者，帝以示臣，奏曰：‘此仙鹿也，寿将千岁。今既生获，不如活之。’会武帝尚神仙，由是纳臣之奏。”上曰：“先生绐矣。

    Explanation

    245

    During the autumn of the twenty-third year of the Kaiyuan era, Emperor Xuanzong of Tang went hunting in the nearby suburbs. Upon arriving at the site of ancient Xianyang, a large deer suddenly appeared before him, its massive and imposing body strikingly different from ordinary deer. The emperor ordered his bowmen to shoot it, and with a single arrow, it was struck. As the emperor returned to the palace, he commanded the kitchen staff to roast the deer and present it to him. Meanwhile, the imperial chef prepared a roasted dish and offered it to the emperor. At that time, Master Zhang Guo, the legendary Taoist sage, was in attendance. The emperor invited Zhang Guo to sit beside him and bestowed the roasted meat upon him. Zhang Guo thanked the emperor and began to eat. After finishing his meal, he said, "Your Majesty, what do you think of this deer?" The emperor replied, "I only know it is a deer, but I do not know what makes it so special." Zhang Guo responded, "This deer is nearly a thousand years old. Your Majesty, you should ask me about it." The emperor laughed and said, "It is merely an animal; how could you claim it is a thousand years old?" Zhang Guo explained, "During the autumn of the fifth year of the Yuanshou era in the Han dynasty, I served Emperor Wu of Han while he hunted in the Shanglin Park. One of his attendants caught this deer alive and presented it to the emperor. The emperor showed it to me, and I advised, 'This is a divine deer, nearly a thousand years old. Since it was captured alive, it would be better to let it live.' Coincidentally, Emperor Wu was deeply interested in the pursuit of immortality, and thus he accepted my advice." The emperor then said, "Master Zhang, you are joking!"

    246

    且汉元狩五年及今八百岁，其鹿长寿，岂历八百岁而不为畋所获乎况苑囿内麋鹿亦多，今所获何妨为他鹿乎？”果曰：“曩时武帝既获此鹿，将舍去之，且命东方朔以练铜为牌，刻成文字以识其年，系于左角下。愿得验之，庶表臣之不诬也。”上即命置鹿首于前，诏内臣高力士验之。凡食倾，曾无所见。上笑曰：“先生果谬矣。左角之下，铜牌安在。”果曰：“臣请自索之。”即顾左右，使铁钳，钳出一小牌，实铜制者，可二寸许。

    Explanation

    246

    Moreover, from the fifth year of Emperor Wu's reign (元狩五年) to the present, it has been eight hundred years. How could a deer live for eight hundred years without being hunted? There are also many deer within the parks, so how could it be any different for another deer? In the past, when Emperor Wu captured this deer, he was going to let it go and ordered Eastern朔 to make a copper plate, carve the year into it, and attach it to the left horn. I wish to verify this, so as to show that I am not lying. The emperor immediately placed the deer's head in front of him and ordered the court official Gao Lishi to verify it. For a meal's time, he saw nothing. The emperor laughed and said, "Mr. Official, you are mistaken. Where is the copper plate on the left horn?" The official replied, "I will look for it myself." He looked around and used a pair of iron tongs to pull out a small plate, which was actually made of copper, about two inches in size.

    247

    盖以年月悠久，为毛革蒙蔽，始不见耳。持以进，上命磨莹视之。其文字刓弊，不可识矣。上于是信果之不谬。又问果曰：“汉元狩五年，甲子何次史编何事吾将徵诸记传，先生第为我言之。”果曰：“是岁岁次癸亥，武帝始开昆明池，用习水战，因蒐狩以顺礼焉。迫今甲戌岁，八百五十二年。”上即命按《汉史》，其昆明池果元狩五年所开，其甲子亦无差。上顾谓力士曰：“异乎哉！张果能言汉武时事，真所谓至人矣。吾固不可得而知也。

    Explanation

    247

    Due to the passage of time, the text was covered by hair and skin, only now can it be seen. The emperor ordered it to be polished and examined. The characters are worn and damaged, and cannot be recognized anymore. The emperor thus believed that Guo was not mistaken. The emperor then asked Guo, saying, "In the fifth year of the Yuanshou era, what position does the year Jiazi hold in the historical records? What events occurred? I will verify this in the historical records. Please tell me about it." Guo replied, saying, "That year was the year Guihai. Emperor Wu began to excavate the Kunming Pool to practice water battles, as a means of hunting and following ritual. As of the year Jiaxu, it has been eight hundred and fifty-two years." The emperor immediately ordered the examination of the Han history, and it was confirmed that the Kunming Pool was indeed excavated in the fifth year of the Yuanshou era, and the Jiazi year is also correct. The emperor then said to the strong man, saying, "How extraordinary! Zhang Guo can speak about events of Emperor Wu's time, truly a sage. I certainly cannot know him."

    248

    ○陈岩
    　　颖川陈岩，字叶梦，舞阳人，侨居东吴。景龙末，举孝廉，如京师，行至渭南，见一妇人，貌甚姝，衣白衣，立于路隅，以袂蒙口而哭，若负冤抑之状。生乃讯之，妇人哭而对曰：“妾，楚人也，侯其氏，家于弋阳县。先人以高尚闻于湘楚间，由是隐迹山林，未尝肯谒侯伯。妾虽一女子，亦有箕颖之志，方将栖踪蓬瀛昆阆以遂其好。适遇有沛国刘君者，尉弋阳，常与妾先人为忘形之友，先人慕刘君之高义，遂以妾归刘氏。自为刘氏妇且十年矣，未尝有纤毫过失。

    Explanation

    248

    Tian Yan

    Yingchuan's Tian Yan, courtesy name Ye Meng, was a native of Wuyang who had settled in Dongwu. In the later part of the Jinglong era, he was recommended for official service based on his filial piety and integrity and traveled to the capital. On his way, he reached the south of the Wei River, where he encountered a woman who was exceptionally beautiful, dressed in white, standing at the edge of the road, covering her mouth with her sleeve and crying as if she were suffering from injustice. Tian Yan questioned her, and she wept and replied:

    "I am a woman from Chu, my family name is Hou, and I live in Yiyang County. My ancestors were known for their noble character in the regions of Xiang and Chu, and thus they chose to live in seclusion in the mountains and forests, never willing to visit high-ranking officials. Although I am just a woman, I also have the ideals of the ancient sages Ji and Yi, who preferred a life of seclusion and moral integrity. I intend to dwell in the mythical islands of Penglai, Yingzhou, Kunlun, and Langya to fulfill my aspirations. I happened to meet a gentleman from Pei State, who served as the magistrate of Yiyang, and he was a close friend with my ancestors, to the point of forgetting formalities. My ancestors admired the noble character of Mr. Liu, so I was married into the Liu family. I have been a member of the Liu family for nearly ten years, and I have never committed any minor fault."

    249

    前岁春，刘君调补真源尉，未一岁，以病免，尽室归于渭上郊居。刘君无行，又娶一卢氏者，濮上人，性极悍戾，每以唇齿相及。妾不胜其愤，故遁而至此。且妾本慕神仙，常欲高蹈云霞，安岩壑之隐，甘橡栗之味，亦足以终老，岂徒扰于尘世，适足为累。今者分不归刘氏矣。”已而嚬容怨咽，若不自解。岩性端悫，闻其言，甚信之。因问曰：“女郎何所归乎？”妇人曰：“妾，一穷人，安所归虽然，君之见问，其有意耶果如是，又安敢逆君之命。”岩喜，即以后乘驾而偕焉。至京师，居永崇里

    Explanation

    249

    Last spring, Mr. Liu was transferred and appointed as the Magistrate of Zhenyuan. He had not been in office for even a year when he was excused from duty due to illness and returned with his entire family to a suburban residence by the Wei River. Mr. Liu lacked virtue and behavior, and he married a woman surnamed Lu, who was from Puchang. She was extremely fierce and violent, often quarreling with each other. I could not bear the anger any longer, so I fled to this place. Moreover, I originally admired immortals and often wished to soar high into the clouds and mist, to find peace in the mountains and valleys, to enjoy the taste of acorns and chestnuts, which is also sufficient for a long life. How could it merely disturb the mundane world, just adding to the burden? Now, I will no longer belong to the Liu family. Then she frowned and sighed with resentment, as if unable to explain herself. Yan's nature is upright and sincere, and he heard her words and believed them greatly. He asked, "Where do you intend to go?" The woman replied, "I am a poor person, where to go? Although, if you ask me, do you have any intention? If so, how dare I disobey your command?" Yan was pleased and immediately rode with her. They arrived in the capital and lived in Yongchong Lane.

    250

    其始甚谨，后乃不恭，往往诟怒，若发狂之状。岩恶之而且悔。明日岩出，妇人即阖扉，键其门，以岩衣囊置庭中，毁裂殆尽。至夕，岩归，妇人拒而不纳。岩怒，即破户而入。见己之衣资，悉已毁裂，岩因诟而责之。妇人忽发怒，毁岩之衣襟佩带，殆无完缕，又爪其面，啮其肌，一身尽伤，血沾于地，已而嗥叫者移时。岩患之，不可制。于是里中民俱来观，簇其门。时有郝居士者，在里中，善视鬼，有符箓呵禁之术，闻妇人哭音，顾谓里中民曰：“此妇人非人，乃山兽也，寓形以惑于世耳。”民且告于岩。岩即请焉，居士乃至岩所居。妇人见居士来，甚惧。居士出墨符一道，向空掷之，妇人大叫一声，忽跃而去，立于瓦屋上。岩窃怪之。居士又出丹符掷之，妇人遂委身于地，化为猿而死。知

    Explanation

    250

    Initially, Rock was very cautious, but later he became disrespectful, often scolding and raging, as if in a state of madness. Rock hated this and regretted it. The next day, when Rock left, the woman immediately closed and locked the door, placing Rock's clothes in the courtyard, where they were almost completely destroyed. By evening, when Rock returned, the woman refused to let him in. Angered, Rock broke the door and entered. He saw that his clothes were all torn apart and scolded the woman for it. The woman suddenly flew into a rage, tearing apart Rock's clothes, almost leaving nothing intact. She scratched his face and bit his flesh, causing him severe injuries, with blood staining the ground. She then howled for a long time. Rock was troubled by this and could not control the situation. The villagers all came to watch, crowding around the door. At that time, there was a layman named Hao living in the village, who was skilled in exorcism and had knowledge of talismans and incantations. Upon hearing the woman's crying, he turned to the villagers and said, "This woman is not human; she is a mountain beast who has taken on a human form to deceive the world." The villagers informed Rock of this. Rock then invited the layman to his home. When the woman saw the layman coming, she was greatly afraid. The layman threw a black talisman into the air, and the woman screamed and suddenly jumped away, standing on the roof of a tile house. Rock was puzzled by this. The layman then threw a red talisman, and the woman fell to the ground, transformed into a monkey, and died.

    251

    岩既悟其妖异，心颇怪悸。后一日，遂至渭南，讯其居人，果有刘君，庐在郊外。岩即谒而问焉。刘曰：“吾常尉于弋阳，弋阳多猿狖，遂求得其一，近兹且十年矣。适遇有故人自濮上来，以一黑犬见惠，其猿为犬所啮，因而遁去。”竟不穷其事，因录以传之。岩后以明经入仕，终于秦州上邽尉。客有游于太原者，偶于铜锅店精舍解鞍憩焉。于精舍佛书中，得刘君所传之事，而文甚鄙。后亡其本。客为余道之如是

    Explanation

    251

    Ran, having realized the strange and supernatural nature of the event, felt a mix of wonder and fear in his heart. The next day, he went to Weinan and inquired among the local people. Indeed, there was a Mr. Liu, whose dwelling was in the outskirts. Ran then paid a visit and asked him. Mr. Liu said, "I once served as an official in Yiyang, where there are many monkeys. I managed to obtain one, and it has been with me for nearly ten years now. Recently, I met an old acquaintance who came from Puzhou, and he gave me a black dog as a gift. My monkey was bitten by the dog and thus escaped." He did not investigate the matter further, but he recorded it and passed it on. Later, Ran entered officialdom through the Mingjing examination and ultimately served as the magistrate of Shanggui in Qin Prefecture. There was a traveler journeying in Taiyuan who happened to stop at a hermitage called Tongguodian to rest. In the Buddhist scriptures of the hermitage, he found the story that Mr. Liu had transmitted, but the writing was quite crude. Later, the original was lost. The traveler told me about it as such.

    252

    ○王长史
    　　东都崇让里有李氏宅，里传云：“其宅非吉之地，固不可居。”李生既卒，其家尽徙居陆浑别墅，由是键其门且数年矣。开元中，有王长史者，亡其名。长史常为清显官，以使酒忤权贵，遂摈为长吏于吴越间。后退居洛中，因质李氏宅以家焉。长史素劲，闻其宅有不祥之名，且曰：“我命在天，不在宅。”即入而居。常独处堂之西宇下。后一夕，闻其哀啸之音，极清楚，若风籁焉。长史起而望之，见一人，衣黑衣，立于几上。长史严声叱之，其人即便举一足击长史肩，长史惧而退，其人亦去。长史因病疮，且甚。后旬余，方少愈。夜中又闻哀啸之音，家僮寻之，时见黑衣人在庭树上。长史有弟善射，于是命弓射之，一发遂中。其人嗥叫，跳上西庑屋瓦而去。明日寻其迹，皆无所见。岁秋，长史召工人重修马厩，因发重舍内，得一死猿，有矢贯胁。验其矢，果长史弟之矢也。方悟黑衣者乃猿尔

    Explanation

    252

    A Story of a Secretary-General

    In the Chóngràng Lǐ neighborhood of the Eastern Capital, there was a house owned by the Li family. Local lore held that the house was not a lucky place and should not be inhabited. After the death of the previous owner, the family moved to a villa in Lùhún, leaving the house abandoned. The door remained sealed for several years.

    During the Kaiyuan era, there was a man named Wáng Chángshǐ, whose name has been lost to history. Wáng Chángshǐ was a high-ranking official who had fallen from grace due to his reckless behavior after drinking, which offended powerful people. He was demoted to serve as a local official in the remote regions of Wu and Yue. Later, he retired to Luoyang and took possession of the Li family's house through a pledge. Despite hearing of the house's ominous reputation, Wáng Chángshǐ, who was known for his strong character, declared, "My fate is in the hands of heaven, not the house," and moved in.

    He often stayed in the western part of the main hall. One night, he heard a clear, mournful cry, as if it were the sound of the wind. Wáng Chángshǐ rose to look and saw a figure in black standing on a table. He sternly rebuked the figure, who immediately raised one foot to strike Wáng Chángshǐ's shoulder. Frightened, Wáng Chángshǐ retreated, and the figure disappeared. Shortly after, Wáng Chángshǐ fell ill with a severe wound. A few days later, he began to recover slightly. One night, he heard the mournful cry again. His servants searched for the source and saw the black-clad figure perched on a tree in the courtyard.

    Wáng Chángshǐ had a brother who was skilled in archery. Wáng Chángshǐ ordered him to shoot at the figure. With one arrow, the figure was hit. It let out a howl and jumped onto the tiles of the western wing of the house, then vanished. The next day, when they searched for traces of the figure, nothing was found. In the autumn, Wáng Chángshǐ called workers to repair the stables and, while opening the inner rooms, discovered a dead monkey with an arrow through its chest. Upon examination, the arrow was identified as belonging to Wáng Chángshǐ's brother. It was then realized that the black-clad figure was, in fact, a monkey.

    253

    ○张鋋
    　　吴郡张鋋，成都人，开元中，以卢溪尉罢秩。调选，不得补于有司，遂归蜀。行次巴西，会日暮，方促马前去，忽有一人自道左山迳中出，拜而请曰：“吾君闻客暮无所止，将欲奉邀，命以请，愿随某去。”鋋因问曰：“尔君为谁，岂非太守见召乎？”曰：“非也，乃巴西侯耳。”主
    　　鋋即随之，入山迳行约百步，望见朱门甚高，人物甚多，甲士环卫，虽侯伯家不如也。又数十步，乃至其所。使者止鋋于门曰：“愿先以白吾君，客当伺焉。”入久之而出，乃引鋋曰：“客且入矣。”鋋既入，见一人立于堂上，衣褐革之裘，貌极异，绮罗珠翠拥侍左右。鋋趋而拜，既拜，其人揖鋋升阶，谓鋋曰：“吾乃巴西侯也，居此数十年矣。适知君暮无所止，故辄奉邀，幸少留以尽欢。”鋋又拜以谢。已而命开筵置酒，其所玩用皆华丽珍具

    Explanation

    253

    Zhang Jian of Wu Commandery, a native of Chengdu, was dismissed from his post as the magistrate of Luxi during the Kaiyuan era. After being selected for a new position, he was not appointed by the relevant officials and thus returned to Sichuan. As he was traveling through Bashu, it happened to be evening, and he was urging his horse forward when suddenly a person emerged from a path on the left side of the road. The person bowed and asked, "My lord has heard that you have nowhere to stay tonight and wishes to invite you. Please follow me." Zhang Jian asked, "Who is your lord? Is he summoning me as the governor?" The person replied, "No, he is the Marquis of Bashu."

    Zhang Jian followed the person, and they walked into the mountain path for about a hundred steps. He saw a very high red gate, with many people and armed guards surrounding the area, even more impressive than the homes of marquises and dukes. After walking several dozen steps further, they arrived at the place. A messenger stopped Zhang Jian at the gate and said, "Please allow me to inform my lord first. You should wait here." After a long while, the messenger came out and led Zhang Jian, saying, "Please come in." Zhang Jian entered and saw a person standing on the hall, wearing a fur coat made of brown leather, with an extremely unusual appearance.
